error_codes.meaning: Water supply not detected
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the flow sensor and fill timing during the intake step. If water level fails to rise adequately within an expected window, it logs 4C (older units show 4E). Unlike a drain fault, 4C fires only during filling, and unlike leak-related codes it points to inadequate incoming flow. Both hot and cold supply are checked, so a single closed valve or clogged inlet screen can trigger it before the inlet valve solenoid is ever suspect.

causes.description: Water supply valve partially or fully closed
Kinked, pinched, or clogged inlet hose
Low water pressure from home supply (below 20 psi)
Faulty water inlet valve not opening fully

fixes.title: Check the water supply valves
fixes.description: Locate the hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer. Turn both fully counterclockwise (open). Even a slightly closed valve can trigger a 4C error.

fixes.title: Inspect the inlet hoses
fixes.description: Pull the washer out and check both inlet hoses for kinks or bends. Straighten any kinks. Also check the mesh screens inside the hose connections — clean them if clogged with sediment.

fixes.title: Check home water pressure
fixes.description: Run a tap nearby. If pressure is low throughout the home, the issue is your supply, not the washer. Low pressure (below 20 psi) will trigger 4C.

fixes.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If all above checks pass, the water inlet valve solenoid may be stuck. Test continuity with a multimeter. Replace if no continuity reading.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before accessing the inlet valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn the washer off and back on. If the water supply issue is fixed, the code should clear. Alternatively, press and hold the Start/Pause button for 5 seconds to reset mid-cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if you have confirmed water supply is good (pressure OK, valves open, hoses clear) but the error persists — the control board or water inlet valve needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and top-load washers across most series, including models such as WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, WF50BG8300AV, WV60M9900AV, and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 4C appearing right at the start of a cycle, often after the washer was moved and a hose got kinked, or after a plumbing shutoff was only partly reopened. Cleaning the mesh inlet screens frequently resolves it, and some report the code was a false alarm during periods of temporarily low household water pressure.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load detected
error_codes.detail: During the spin ramp-up, the control board monitors drum motion via the balance/vibration sensor and motor feedback. If it detects excessive wobble or uneven weight distribution, it stops the spin, refills or rocks the drum to redistribute, and retries several times. UE fires only after these rebalancing attempts fail. Unlike DC (which flags a load the machine gives up redistributing), UE is the general unbalanced-load warning, and it protects the bearings, suspension, and cabinet from damage.

causes.description: Single heavy item or small mixed load bunched to one side
Overloaded drum (too many items packed tightly)
Underloaded drum (one or two items only)
Worn drum suspension springs or shock absorbers

fixes.title: Redistribute the laundry
fixes.description: Open the door and manually redistribute items evenly around the drum. Separate any tangled items like sheets or towels that may have clumped together.

fixes.title: Check the load size
fixes.description: Remove some items if the drum is overloaded (more than ¾ full). Add a few items if washing 1–2 small items alone — small loads imbalance easily.

fixes.title: Run a spin-only cycle
fixes.description: Select Spin Only at the lowest speed and let it complete. If the cycle finishes without UE, the load is now balanced.

fixes.title: Inspect suspension rods (persistent UE only)
fixes.description: If UE occurs consistently with normal loads, inspect the 4 suspension rods. Shake the drum by hand — it should resist movement and spring back. If it flops freely, replace the suspension rods.

error_codes.reset_instructions: UE clears automatically once the drum is balanced. The washer may make several attempts to rebalance before displaying the error. No manual reset needed — just open and redistribute the load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs with every load regardless of size or balance, the drum suspension springs or shock absorbers are likely worn and need replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Most Samsung front-load and top-load washers display UE for unbalanced loads; top-load models (such as the WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, and WA54R7600AV) tend to show it more often since they rely on drum balancing during spin, while some models use the alias code E4.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report UE with bulky single items like a comforter, a bath mat, or a couple of towels that clump to one side, and note it often clears simply by opening the door and spreading the load out. It can also appear if the washer isn't level or sits on an uneven floor, which some owners fix by adjusting the leveling feet.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors drain time and water level after the pump activates. If the drum does not empty to the expected level within a set window, the OE (older 5E) code fires, indicating restricted or absent flow rather than a fill fault. It differs from 5C/SC pump-signal errors and from PE water-level sensor faults — OE specifically flags that water is still present after the drain phase should have finished, usually from a clog or weak pump.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter access panel on the lower-front of the washer. Place a towel and shallow pan below it. Turn the cap counterclockwise slowly — water will drain out. Remove and clean the filter under running water. Check for coins, pins, or lint buildup.
fixes.warning: Expect a significant amount of water. Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Inspect the drain hose at the back for kinks. Disconnect it from the standpipe and blow air through it to check for blockages.

fixes.title: Check the household standpipe
fixes.description: Make sure the drain standpipe is not clogged. Run water down it directly. If it backs up, the issue is your home plumbing, not the washer.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the above steps are clear, the pump motor may have failed. You can hear the pump when the washer tries to drain — a grinding or no-sound indicates failure. Replace the drain pump.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, restart the washer. If water remains in the drum, run a Spin/Drain-only cycle. The error should clear once a successful drain cycle completes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if the drain pump has been replaced and the error persists — there may be a control board or wiring harness issue.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and top-load washers across most recent series; front-loaders such as WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW and WF50BG8300AV typically have the lower-front pump filter access, while top-loaders like WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, WA54R7600AV and the WV60M9900AV FlexWash rely on the same drain-timing logic
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report OE appearing near the end of a wash or during spin, often after washing bulky or linty items. A clogged pump filter or a bent drain hose is the usual quick win, and many find coins or debris in the filter; false triggers can occur if the drain hose is pushed too far into the standpipe, creating a siphon.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked — drum cannot rotate
error_codes.detail: The motor control board monitors drum rotation via the hall (position) sensor and the current the motor draws. LE (also shown as 11E) fires when the board commands rotation but detects little or no movement while current spikes, indicating a stalled or locked motor. It differs from spin or drain faults: rather than an unbalanced or undrained load, the control sees the motor physically unable to turn, whether from an overload, a mechanical jam, worn brushes, or a bad hall sensor.

causes.description: Drum overloaded with too much laundry
Foreign object (bra wire, sock) jammed between drum and tub
Worn or failed motor brushes (older models)

fixes.title: Reduce the load
fixes.description: Turn off the washer. Remove several items from the drum and try restarting. LE from overloading is very common.

fixes.title: Check for foreign objects
fixes.description: Slowly rotate the drum by hand. If it feels stuck, remove all laundry and check around the drum gasket seal for items caught in the space between drum and door seal.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. If the motor stalled from a momentary overload, this may clear it.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes (top-load models)
fixes.description: On older top-load Samsung models, the motor brushes wear down and cause stalling. If you hear the motor trying to start but failing, brushes are likely worn.
fixes.warning: Requires accessing the motor — unplug first.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor or motor
fixes.description: If drum rotation is physically free but the error persists, test the hall sensor with a multimeter. If resistance is out of spec, replace it. As a last resort, the motor itself may need replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5–10 minutes, remove excess laundry, then plug back in and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drum is free, load is normal, and the error persists after power cycling, the motor or control board needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and top-load washers across most modern series; both direct-drive models and older brushed-motor top-loaders can display this. Applicable examples from the known list include WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, and WA54R7600AV.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report LE right after loading a bulky or overstuffed drum, and simply removing a few items and restarting often clears it. A wire or sock lodged near the gasket is another frequent trigger; if the drum won't turn freely by hand, that's usually the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Door not properly closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the door lock switch to confirm the interlock has engaged before allowing a cycle. DE fires when it does not receive the expected locked signal, meaning the door isn't fully latched or the solenoid/switch failed. Because the drum can spin at high speed, the machine refuses to start without a confirmed lock. Unlike FL, which points more directly at a failed lock mechanism, DE often reflects a simple latch or alignment problem before hardware failure.

causes.description: Door not fully pushed closed (laundry caught in seal)
Damaged or worn door latch/strike plate
Wiring harness to door lock loose or damaged

fixes.title: Check for items caught in the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber door gasket and check for socks or small items caught between the gasket and drum. A small item can prevent the door from fully closing.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door firmly until you hear and feel a click. The lock is spring-loaded and requires a definitive push.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch and catch
fixes.description: Look at the door catch on the door and the strike inside the machine. If the plastic is cracked or the latch feels loose, replace the assembly.

fixes.title: Test the door lock solenoid
fixes.description: If the latch looks fine but the error persists, the lock solenoid (the electrical component inside the door lock) may have failed. Test with a multimeter for continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing the solenoid.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Make sure the door is fully closed and click-locked. The error should clear when the cycle restarts with a properly secured door.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but the error still shows, the door lock solenoid or wiring needs professional replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load washers with an electronic door interlock, including models such as WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, and WF50BG8300AV. Some top-loaders with lid-lock circuits may show a related door/lid fault.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appears right at cycle start, often after a bulky load where a sock or garment corner slips into the gasket and blocks the latch. Many find a firm push until it clicks, plus clearing the seal, resolves it; if it persists after a solid click, the lock assembly or wiring is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Water heater error — water not heating to target temperature
error_codes.detail: The washer is not able to heat the wash water to the programmed temperature within the expected time. This affects wash quality for hot water cycles and can indicate a failing heater element or thermistor.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on control board
Wiring fault between heater and control board

fixes.title: Check water supply temperature
fixes.description: Make sure the hot water supply to the washer is actually hot. Run the hot water tap at the sink — if it's cold, the issue is your water heater.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor (NTC sensor)
fixes.description: The thermistor monitors water temperature. Test resistance at room temperature — should be approximately 10k ohms. If significantly out of spec, replace it.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test the heating element with a multimeter for continuity. No continuity means the element has burned out.
fixes.warning: The element is inside the drum tub — requires significant disassembly.

fixes.title: Inspect the control board heater relay
fixes.description: If element and thermistor both test good, the heater relay on the control board may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and restart. If the error is intermittent and clears on restart, the thermistor is failing. If it returns consistently, the heater element likely needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element replacement requires disassembling significant parts of the washer. If you're not comfortable with appliance repair, call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected
error_codes.detail: SUD (also shown as 5D or SD) fires when the control board detects excessive foam during wash or spin, typically inferred from an unstable spin ramp-up and sensor readings that indicate the drum cannot reach or hold speed through a cushion of suds. Rather than a mechanical fault, it is a protective pause: the machine holds and often adds rinse water to break down foam before resuming. Unlike drain or spin-error codes, no water is trapped and no hardware has failed.

causes.description: Wrong detergent type — non-HE detergent in HE washer
Detergent residue buildup from previous overuse

fixes.title: Wait for suds to settle
fixes.description: The washer will automatically continue once suds reduce enough. This may take 15–30 minutes. Do not turn off the washer.

fixes.title: Run a Rinse+Spin cycle
fixes.description: If the cycle doesn't continue within 30 minutes, run a Rinse+Spin cycle to clear the suds.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent going forward
fixes.description: For HE (High Efficiency) washers, use HE-labeled detergent only, and use significantly less than the measuring line suggests — 1–2 tablespoons for a normal load.

fixes.title: Clean the drum (if recurrent)
fixes.description: Run a drum clean cycle monthly to remove detergent residue buildup.

error_codes.reset_instructions: SUD clears automatically once foam reduces. No manual reset needed. If you want to speed up the process, run a Rinse+Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is not a fault requiring professional service. If it occurs every cycle despite using minimal HE detergent, the detergent dispenser drawer may need cleaning.
error_codes.models_affected: All Samsung HE (High Efficiency) washers, both front-load and top-load, including models such as the WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WF50BG8300AV, WV60M9900AV, WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SUD showing up mid-cycle, especially after switching detergents or overfilling the dispenser, and it often clears on its own if left alone for a while. A frequent quick win is simply using far less HE detergent going forward; some also find a non-HE or overly sudsing detergent to be the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor (thermistor) fault
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors resistance across the NTC thermistor mounted near the heater assembly. tE fires when that resistance falls outside the expected window—effectively an open or short—so the board reads an implausible water temperature. Because it cannot safely command the heater without a trustworthy reading, it halts the cycle. This is distinct from HE, which flags an actual heating failure or over-temperature condition rather than the sensor signal itself being invalid.

causes.description: Wiring harness connector loose at thermistor
Short circuit in temperature sensor wiring

fixes.title: Check thermistor wiring connector
fixes.description: Locate the thermistor (usually mounted on the heater assembly inside the tub). Check that the wiring connector is firmly seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixes.description: At room temperature (~68°F/20°C), a healthy Samsung thermistor should read approximately 6,000–7,000 ohms. A reading of 0 or infinite indicates failure.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Thermistors are inexpensive and easy to replace. Disconnect the connector, unscrew the sensor, and install the new one.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or reseating the connector, unplug the washer for 1 minute, then restart to clear the error.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor tests good and connector is secure but the error persists, the main control board may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung washers with a water-heating circuit, including many front-load and select top-load models; the heater-mounted thermistor and tE logic are shared across the range roughly 2013 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tE appearing at the start of a heated wash or sanitize cycle rather than on cold rinses. A loose or corroded connector is a frequent false trigger, and many find that simply reseating the thermistor harness after unplugging clears the code without any part replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — key stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: The control board scans the panel's button matrix and expects each key to read as open until pressed. bE (including bE1/bE3 variants) fires when a key registers as continuously closed or shorted, so the board cannot start or continue a cycle. Common triggers are a physically stuck button, detergent residue bridging contacts, or moisture in the membrane; a degraded UI board can also send false signals. Unlike sensor or motor faults, this points squarely at the user-interface, not the wash mechanics.

causes.description: Button physically stuck from detergent residue
Moisture or condensation in control panel

fixes.title: Check for stuck buttons
fixes.description: Press each button individually and ensure none feel stuck. Clean around buttons with a dry cloth.

fixes.title: Dry the control panel
fixes.description: If moisture has entered the panel, dry the area thoroughly. Leave the machine off for several hours.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5–10 minutes. This often clears transient button errors.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If the error persists, the user interface board needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes. If a button is stuck, clear it before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no button appears stuck and the error returns after a power cycle, the control board needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers with electronic touch/membrane control panels
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report bE appearing after spills, steam-cleaning, or a humid laundry room, and it often clears after unplugging and drying the panel. If one button feels sticky or mushy, gently cleaning around it frequently resolves the code before any board replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Heating over-temperature error — water heated beyond safe limit
error_codes.detail: HC (and its HC1/HC2/HE3 variants) fires when the control reads wash-water temperature — or drying-air temperature on combo units — above a safe threshold and cuts power to the heater. It usually stems from a thermistor reporting a false high value or a control-board relay stuck closed that keeps energizing the element. Unlike 1C, which flags a bad or open thermistor signal, HC indicates the sensed temperature actually exceeded the over-temp limit, prompting a protective shutdown.

causes.description: Heater relay stuck closed on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. A one-time over-temp glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Access the heater area and check the NTC sensor — roughly 10k ohms at room temperature. Out of spec = replace.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the heater.

fixes.title: Inspect the heating element
fixes.description: Check the element for continuity and signs of scaling or burn-through.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart. If HC returns once a heated cycle begins, test the thermistor first.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load washers and washer-dryer combos with an internal heating circuit (including combo models such as the WV60M9900AV series)
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the thermistor and element test good but HC persists, the control board heater relay is stuck and the board needs replacement.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report HC appearing shortly after a heated or sanitize cycle begins rather than at start-up. A one-time over-temp glitch sometimes clears with a power cycle, but if it returns each heated cycle, a failing thermistor is the fault most people end up finding first.

error_codes.meaning: Water level sensor error — pressure (level) sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The 1C code (also shown as 1E or LC1) fires when the control loses a trustworthy water-level signal. Samsung uses a pressure sensor connected to the tub by a thin clear hose; air pressure rises as the tub fills. If that signal drifts out of range, or the hose is kinked, clogged, or loose, the reading becomes unreliable. The control halts fill and drain to avoid overflow or dry operation. Unlike PE, which flags a specific out-of-band value, 1C generally indicates a broken or disconnected sensing path.

causes.description: Hose connection loose at sensor or tub

fixes.title: Check the sensing hose
fixes.description: Locate the small clear hose from the tub to the level sensor. Straighten kinks and clear any sludge.

fixes.title: Blow test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose at the sensor and gently blow — you should hear a click as the diaphragm moves.

fixes.title: Replace the level sensor
fixes.description: If it doesn't click or tests out of range, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off for 1 minute and restart. If 1C returns during fill, recheck the sensing hose for kinks before replacing the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor and clear hose don't resolve 1C, the control board pressure input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers with an analog pressure-based water-level sensor, spanning most recent model years
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing at the start of a cycle during fill, sometimes after moving the machine or after residue builds up in the sensing hose. A frequent quick win is simply straightening or clearing that small clear hose, which can clear the code without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Power supply error — abnormal/low line voltage detected
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously samples the incoming AC line voltage and expects it to stay within a defined window (near 120V, or ~240V on applicable models). When it reads voltage that is too low, unstable, or sagging under load — often from a brown-out, undersized extension cord, or a loose outlet — it throws 9C1 and stops the cycle to protect the motor and electronics. Unlike a hard component-failure code, this points at the power feeding the machine rather than an internal part.

causes.description: Extension cord or power strip in use

fixes.title: Plug directly into the wall
fixes.description: Remove any extension cord or power strip — plug the washer straight into a dedicated grounded outlet.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixes.title: Check outlet voltage
fixes.description: Have an electrician confirm a steady ~120V (or ~240V where applicable) at the outlet under load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Plug directly into a wall outlet (no extension cord), power cycle, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If voltage at the outlet is confirmed correct but 9C1 persists, the control board's voltage-sensing circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and top-load washers with electronic control boards that monitor incoming line voltage; the code appears across recent series regardless of specific model.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing when the washer is run through a power strip or long extension cord, or during periods of unstable household power. A frequent quick win is simply plugging directly into a grounded wall outlet and power cycling, which often clears it if the outlet itself is sound.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — main and sub control boards not talking
error_codes.detail: The AE code (also shown as AE6 or AC) fires when the main control board and the sub display/UI board fail to exchange data reliably over their communication harness. The board that detects the dropout halts the cycle, since neither can coordinate wash steps without a confirmed handshake. Unlike sensor or drainage faults, AE points to the boards' interconnection itself — most often a loose or corroded ribbon/harness rather than a mechanical or plumbing problem.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5–10 minutes. A transient communication glitch often clears.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Remove the top/console panel and firmly reseat the ribbon/harness between the main and UI boards.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, the UI or main board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5–10 minutes and restart. If AE returns, reseat the harness between the control boards.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness is sound and reseating doesn't help, professional board diagnosis identifies which board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers with separate main and display/UI control boards, spanning most current series
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report AE appearing right at power-up or mid-cycle, sometimes after a move, a power surge, or work near the console. Many find a simple unplug-and-restart clears a one-off glitch, and reseating the harness between the boards often resolves recurring cases.

error_codes.meaning: Over-current motor protection — motor drawing excessive current
error_codes.detail: The motor control board has detected that the motor is drawing more current than its rated maximum. This is a protective shutdown to prevent motor damage. An overloaded drum is the most common trigger, but motor or board failure can cause it without overloading.

causes.description: Drum overloaded — too many heavy items
Wiring fault causing incorrect current reading

fixes.title: Remove half the laundry
fixes.description: Turn off the washer, open the door, remove roughly half the items. This is the first and most common fix.

fixes.title: Power cycle for 10 minutes
fixes.description: Unplug, wait 10 full minutes, restart. The motor control firmware resets.

fixes.title: Test the motor resistance
fixes.description: With the washer unplugged, access the motor and test winding resistance. Compare to spec in the service manual. Out-of-spec = motor needs replacement.

fixes.title: Replace motor or control board
fixes.description: If motor tests OK but error persists, the control board's current sensor or relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes. Restart with a reduced load. If CE returns within the first minute on a light load, the motor has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If error persists with normal loads after a reset, professional motor or board diagnosis is required.

error_codes.meaning: Water pressure sensor (pressure switch) failure
error_codes.detail: PE fires when the control board reads an out-of-range or implausible signal from the analog water-level pressure sensor, which converts tub air-column pressure into a frequency the board uses to gauge fill. The code triggers on an open, shorted, or drifting sensor, or when a kinked or blocked pressure hose prevents pressure from reaching it. Unlike OE (overflow) or nF/4E (no-fill) codes that indicate actual water problems, PE points specifically to the sensing circuit itself misreporting the level.

causes.description: Control board pressure sensor input failure

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose runs from the outer tub up to the pressure sensor. Check for kinks, cracks, or blockages. Blow gently through the hose — you should hear the sensor click.

fixes.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixes.description: The sensor should audibly click when you blow into the hose connection. Test electrical continuity at each water level position.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is usually accessed by removing the top panel. Disconnect the hose and wiring, unscrew, and swap.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug for 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new pressure sensor and clear hose don't resolve PE, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers across most recent series, including models such as WF45T6000AW, WF42H5000AW, WA50R5400AV, and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PE showing up at the start of a cycle or during fill, sometimes after the machine has been moved or tilted, which can pinch the pressure hose. A frequent quick win is reseating or clearing that thin hose before assuming the sensor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor drive error — motor cannot reach commanded speed
error_codes.detail: The motor control board commands a target speed and monitors feedback from the hall sensor tracking rotor position. When actual rotation does not match the command — no signal, erratic signal, or the motor stalling — a 3E fault registers. Sub-codes 3E1/3E2/3E3 flag specific drive phases or speed ranges. Unlike CE, which points to a communication link between boards, 3E reflects a genuine drive or motor hardware fault: a failed hall sensor, damaged stator windings, or a faulty drive circuit.

causes.description: Drive belt broken or slipping (belt-drive models)

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart with a light load.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor monitors rotor position. On direct-drive Samsung models, it is mounted near the motor and costs under $20. It's the single most common cause of 3E.
fixes.warning: Lay the washer on its front to access the motor.

fixes.title: Inspect stator and rotor
fixes.description: Check rotor magnets for cracks. Check stator windings for burn smell or discoloration.

fixes.title: Replace motor control board
fixes.description: If hall sensor and motor hardware look fine, the drive circuit on the control board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: 3E on direct-drive Samsung washers is almost always the hall sensor. Try that $20 repair before spending $200+ on a board.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers with digital inverter drive systems; the direct-drive design where the hall sensor is the leading cause covers most modern units, e.g. WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WA50R5400AV, WA54R7600AV, WV60M9900AV, WF50BG8300AV. Older belt-drive models can also throw it.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 3E appearing at the start of spin or agitation, often with the drum barely turning or refusing to move. A single reset sometimes clears a one-off glitch, but recurring codes on direct-drive models are frequently traced to the inexpensive hall sensor, which many report swapping successfully themselves.

error_codes.meaning: Motor tachometer / hall sensor error
error_codes.detail: SE fires when the motor control board fails to read a valid pulse train from the hall-effect tachometer mounted at the motor rotor. Without a clean speed signal the board cannot regulate acceleration or spin RPM, so it halts to avoid unsafe imbalance. Unlike 3E, which flags broader motor or driver faults, SE points specifically to the sensor itself, its 3-wire harness, or the board's sensor input, rather than the winding or drive stage.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small 3-wire sensor near the motor rotor. It's very inexpensive and quick to replace.
fixes.warning: Unplug washer and lay on its front to access the motor.

fixes.title: Check wiring harness
fixes.description: Inspect the 3-wire harness from hall sensor to control board for damage.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After hall sensor replacement, unplug for 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor is replaced and SE persists, the control board signal input for the hall sensor has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers with a hall-sensor-equipped drive motor, including models such as WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, WA54R7600AV, WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, and WF42H5000AW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SE appearing during the transition into spin, sometimes after a load that shifts heavily to one side. A loose or corroded hall-sensor connector is a frequent false trigger, and reseating the 3-wire plug or swapping the inexpensive sensor often clears it for good.

error_codes.meaning: Low voltage detected — power supply problem
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously samples incoming line voltage and flags 9E1 when it reads below the acceptable window (roughly 108V, or under 100V during high-draw phases), while 9E2 signals over-voltage. The washer halts to shield its inverter and electronics from unstable power. Unlike bE, which points to a button or interface fault, 9E1/9E2 specifically concern the AC supply itself, whether from household wiring, the circuit, or the utility, and only rarely the internal power board.

causes.description: Low household voltage — shared circuit under heavy load
Extension cord or undersized outlet wiring

fixes.title: Check the outlet voltage
fixes.description: Use a multimeter on the outlet. Samsung washers require 120V (±10%), typically 108–132V. Below 100V during spin cycle is common if sharing a circuit.

fixes.title: Move to a dedicated circuit
fixes.description: Washers should be on a dedicated 20A circuit. Sharing with other appliances causes voltage sag during spin.

fixes.title: Check power board
fixes.description: If voltage is good but error persists, the internal power monitoring circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore proper voltage and unplug for 5 minutes. Restart on a dedicated circuit.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent 9E1 on a known-good circuit indicates an internal power regulation fault requiring professional service.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front- and top-load washers with a digital control board that monitors input voltage, roughly 2015 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 9E1 appearing mid-cycle, especially during spin when current draw peaks, and often on circuits shared with other appliances or fed through an extension cord. Many find the quick win is simply relocating the washer to its own dedicated outlet, after which the code tends not to return.

error_codes.meaning: No fill — water supply insufficient at start of cycle
error_codes.detail: At cycle start the control board opens the inlet valve and watches the pressure sensor for a rising water level within a set fill-check window. If it sees no measurable fill, nF (or nF1) fires. It differs from 4C, which flags a fill problem detected mid-cycle, and from HE, a heater fault. The most common trigger is closed valves, low household pressure, or clogged inlet screens rather than the solenoid itself.

causes.description: Water supply valve closed or pressure too low

fixes.title: Open supply valves fully
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves must be completely open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Unscrew hoses at the back of the washer, remove the mesh screens, and clean under running water.

fixes.title: Test water pressure
fixes.description: Turn on a nearby tap to check pressure. Below 20 PSI triggers nF.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If pressure and screens are fine, test solenoid coils (200–500 ohms) and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix the supply issue and press Start. nF clears when fill is successfully detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and valve is new but nF continues, the fill sensor or control board water detection circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung top-load and front-load washers across most recent generations, including models such as WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW, WF50BG8300AV, WV60M9900AV, and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report nF right after installation, after a plumbing shutoff, or in winter when a supply line partially freezes. A frequent quick win is simply confirming both hot and cold taps are fully open and rinsing the mesh inlet screens, which often clears the code without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Aqua Protection activated — flood protection triggered
error_codes.detail: The AP code fires when a float switch in the base pan detects standing water beneath the tub. The control interprets any accumulation as a potential flood, so it aborts the cycle, drains, and locks out operation until the pan is dry and the float drops. Unlike OE (drain/overflow at the tub) or DE (door lock), AP specifically reflects water pooling in the chassis base — from an external leak, a failed seal, or foam overflow — rather than a sensor or drainage timing fault.

causes.description: Door seal torn or pulled away from channel
Detergent oversudsing causing foam overflow into base
Supply hose connection leaking at machine inlet
Drain hose loose at pump connection

fixes.title: Tilt the machine to drain the base
fixes.description: Turn off power. Carefully tilt the machine forward to drain water from the base pan. Have towels ready.
fixes.warning: Turn off water supply and disconnect power before tilting.

fixes.title: Allow base to dry
fixes.description: Leave machine upright with door open for several hours.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check the rubber door gasket for tears, pulled sections, or mold damage.

fixes.title: Check detergent amount
fixes.description: Excessive suds can overflow into the base. Use HE detergent, max 1 tablespoon.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply hose both ends, pump outlet, and drain hose connection.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak source, let it dry completely, then restart. The float sensor resets when the base is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible external leak, the tub, drum bearing seal, or pump housing may have an internal leak requiring professional inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load and select top-load Samsung washers equipped with the Aqua Stop / Aqua Protection base tray and float switch, including models such as WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, and WF50BG8300AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report AP appearing mid-cycle after using too much or non-HE detergent, where excess suds spill into the base and trigger the float even without a true leak. A frequent quick win is tilting the machine to drain the pan, letting it dry overnight, then cutting the detergent dose; if it recurs with no visible external leak, an internal seal or pump leak is more likely.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — cannot complete lock engagement
error_codes.detail: The control board sends power to the door lock and waits to confirm the interlock has physically latched, usually via a feedback switch. FL fires when that confirmation never arrives within the expected window, meaning the mechanism failed to complete engagement. Unlike DE, which reports the door sensed as open before a cycle, FL indicates the washer attempted to lock but the solenoid, latch, or relay did not achieve a confirmed locked state, blocking cycle start.

causes.description: Door lock mechanism jammed by clothing
Control board door lock relay failure

fixes.title: Check for clothing in the door
fixes.description: Pull back the door gasket and look for items caught in the lock area.

fixes.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixes.description: The lock clicks audibly when engaged. If no click when power applied, the solenoid has failed.

fixes.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixes.description: Straightforward replacement accessible from inside the door frame.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart. If FL returns, the lock assembly needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door lock doesn't resolve FL, the control board's lock relay or wiring has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load washers with an electronic door interlock; the FL code applies most directly to front-loaders such as the WF-series (e.g. WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WF50BG8300AV) rather than top-load models.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report FL appearing right as a cycle tries to start, often after a garment or gasket fold blocks the latch. Reseating the door firmly or clearing trapped fabric sometimes clears it, but a code that returns after restart usually points to a worn lock assembly.

error_codes.meaning: Specific button on control panel shorted
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously scans the button matrix on the control panel. bE2 and bE3 fire when the board reads a specific button as held down for longer than a valid press, indicating a short in that key's input line. Unlike the generic bE code, these variants identify which individual button is stuck. The most common trigger is detergent or moisture bridging the membrane contacts, distinguishing a cleanable fault from a failed UI board that reads the input as permanently closed.

causes.description: Membrane keypad failure at specific button

fixes.title: Identify and press the stuck button
fixes.description: Press each button around the display firmly. A stuck button will feel different.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe with a dry cloth, paying attention to button edges where detergent residue accumulates.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If a button remains stuck after cleaning.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If a stuck button is cleared, code should not return.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't resolve bE2/bE3, the main control board keypad interface has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung washers with electronic touch/button control panels, both front-load and top-load models across recent generations
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a cycle where detergent or water splashed onto the panel, and many find that wiping the panel and firmly pressing each button clears it. It can also be falsely triggered by residual moisture, so letting the panel dry after unplugging often resolves it without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Heater circuit error — TRIAC failure
error_codes.detail: E7 fires when the control board's self-diagnostics detect that the TRIAC — the solid-state switch feeding the heating element — is no longer switching power correctly, typically because it has burned or shorted. The board sees the heater fail to energize or draw abnormal current and flags a heater circuit fault. Unlike codes tied to sensor or temperature readings (such as tE), E7 points at the power-switching hardware itself, which is why the element can test fine yet still not heat.

causes.description: Heater TRIAC burned on control board
Heater element short-circuit causing TRIAC damage

fixes.title: Test the heating element first
fixes.description: Check element for continuity and for any short to the casing. A shorted element damages the TRIAC.

fixes.title: Replace damaged element if shorted
fixes.description: Replacing a shorted element without replacing the board may cause the new TRIAC to fail again.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: E7 almost always means the control board's heater circuit needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E7 returns, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E7 almost always requires control board replacement. Not a typical DIY repair.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung washers with an internal water heating circuit, primarily front-load models and heated-wash top-loaders
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E7 appearing mid-cycle on wash programs that call for heated water, often with the load left cold. Because a shorted element can take the TRIAC with it, many find that swapping only one part isn't enough; testing the element before replacing the board is the frequently recommended quick check.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor (temperature sensor) failure
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously monitors the drum thermistor, an NTC sensor mounted on the heating element housing. tE (or tE3) fires when the measured resistance falls outside the expected range — indicating an open, shorted, or drifting sensor, or a broken connection in its harness. Because safe heating depends on a valid temperature reading, the board halts the cycle. Unlike HE, which points to heating-element or thermal-cutoff problems, tE specifically flags the sensing circuit rather than the heat source itself.

fixes.title: Locate and test the thermistor
fixes.description: The thermistor is mounted on the heating element housing. Test resistance with a multimeter — should read ~10k ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Unplug and inspect the thermistor connector for corrosion or loose fit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: This is a simple clip-in repair. Disconnect the old sensor and plug in the new one.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before any repair.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor, unplug for 1 minute then restart. The code clears when a successful cycle completes without temperature anomaly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the thermistor is new and tests correctly, the control board's temperature input circuit may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers across most recent electric and gas platforms, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tE appearing mid-cycle or shortly after startup, sometimes intermittently as a loose or corroded connector makes and breaks contact. A reseated connector occasionally clears it, but a persistent code usually points to a failed thermistor, which many describe as a quick clip-in swap.

error_codes.meaning: Heater fault — drum not heating or overheating
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the heating circuit and drum temperature sensors, then fires HE when the measured temperature falls outside expected limits for the selected cycle. It triggers when the element sits open and produces no heat, or when restricted airflow drives temperatures high enough to trip the high-limit thermostat. Unlike tE, which flags an out-of-range thermistor reading, HE points to the actual heat-generating circuit — element, thermostat, relay, or wiring — failing to deliver or safely regulate heat.

causes.description: Tripped high-limit thermostat from restricted airflow
Faulty heater relay on control board

fixes.title: Check the lint filter and vent
fixes.description: A completely blocked vent can cause the high-limit thermostat to trip, cutting off heat. Clean the lint filter and disconnect/clean the vent duct.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect the element wires and test continuity. A working element will show resistance (around 10–20 ohms); an open element shows infinite resistance.

fixes.title: Test the high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: If the element is good but heat isn't reaching the drum, the high-limit thermostat may have tripped permanently. Test for continuity — no continuity = failed.

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Replace whichever component tested as failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer and ensure it's gas-disconnected if applicable before opening.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, the code may require clearing by unplugging for 5 minutes. Check that vent is clear before first test run.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both the element and thermostat test OK, the issue is likely on the control board and requires professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric dryers with a resistive heating element, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, and DVE50BG8300V; gas models like DVG45T3200W can show a related heater fault tied to ignition rather than an element.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code showing up when loads come out damp or the cycle quits early, and a surprising number trace it to a clogged vent or full lint filter rather than a dead element. Clearing the vent and running a fresh cycle is the frequent quick win before any parts get swapped.

error_codes.meaning: Vent blockage detected
error_codes.detail: The dryer's internal sensors have detected restricted airflow through the exhaust duct. Continuing to run with a blocked vent is a fire hazard — the dryer will reduce heat or stop the cycle.

causes.description: Lint-blocked exhaust duct (most common after 1+ years)
Kinked or crushed flexible duct behind the dryer
Bird or pest nest in exterior vent cap
Duct run is too long or has too many bends

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the lint screen. This should be done after every single drying cycle.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean the vent duct
fixes.description: Pull the dryer out, disconnect the duct from the back, and blow through it. If you feel resistance, clean with a dryer vent brush kit.

fixes.title: Check the exterior vent cap
fixes.description: Go outside and find where the dryer vents. Remove any debris, lint buildup, or animal nesting blocking the flap.

fixes.title: Inspect duct routing
fixes.description: Flexible foil or plastic ducts kink easily. Replace with rigid metal duct for runs longer than 4 feet. Maximum recommended run is 25 feet with fewer than two 90° turns.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a short air-dry cycle. The sensor clears once airflow is detected as normal.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is completely clear and code persists, the airflow sensor or control board may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — a control-panel key is stuck
error_codes.detail: The user-interface board continuously scans each keypad contact. When it reads the same button as pressed for roughly 30 seconds or longer, it assumes a fault, displays bE2 (also shown as bE or b2), and locks out the keypad so no cycle can start. The usual trigger is a physically stuck key or moisture and detergent residue bridging the membrane contacts. Unlike bC2, which points to a communication problem, bE2 is specifically a stuck-key condition, rarely the main board.

fixes.title: Find the stuck button
fixes.description: Press each button in turn and feel for one that sticks or doesn't spring back.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around every button with a barely-damp cloth and dry fully — residue under a key registers as a press.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient bE2.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If a button stays stuck or the error returns, replace the user-interface board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes, free or clean the stuck button, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new UI board doesn't clear bE2, the main board's button interface has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers across most electric and gas lines with membrane-style touch/button control panels, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report bE2 appearing right after cleaning the panel or in humid laundry rooms, and it often clears once a sticky button is worked loose and the surface is wiped dry. A quick unplug for a few minutes reportedly resolves many transient cases before any parts are needed.

error_codes.meaning: Power supply error — abnormal/low line voltage
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously monitors incoming line voltage and fires 9C1 when the reading falls outside its safe operating window, typically sagging low under the dryer's motor and heater load. Because voltage drop is worst under load, extension cords, power strips, shared circuits, or a loose outlet are the usual triggers rather than a true utility problem. This distinguishes it from 9E1, which flags a genuine over/under-voltage condition, and from control-side faults that persist even with confirmed steady voltage at the outlet.

causes.description: Extension cord or power strip in use

fixes.title: Plug directly into the wall
fixes.description: Remove any extension cord/power strip — the dryer needs a dedicated grounded outlet.

fixes.title: Check the circuit
fixes.description: Confirm the dryer is on its own circuit and the breaker isn't shared or undersized.

fixes.title: Verify outlet voltage
fixes.description: Have an electrician confirm steady voltage at the outlet under load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Plug directly into a dedicated wall outlet (no extension cord/strip), then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If outlet voltage is confirmed correct but 9C1 persists, the control board's voltage-sensing circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas dryers using the alphanumeric fault-code system, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing right after moving the dryer or plugging it into a power strip or extension cord, and it often clears once the unit is plugged directly into a dedicated wall outlet. On stacked washer/dryer kits it can also flash briefly if the dryer is operated within a few seconds of powering the washer.

error_codes.meaning: Line voltage abnormality — supply voltage out of range
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously monitors incoming line voltage through its power-sensing circuit. 9E1 fires when it reads voltage that is too low or fluctuating beyond the safe operating window, preventing a run to protect the motor and heater. Unlike 9C1, which flags a broadly abnormal supply, 9E1 leans toward under-voltage or instability. It is distinct from FE, which points to a line-frequency mismatch rather than a voltage-level problem, though both stem from the incoming power feed.

fixes.title: Remove extension cords
fixes.description: Plug straight into the wall outlet.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixes.title: Have the outlet checked
fixes.description: An electrician should verify correct, stable voltage at the receptacle.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Plug directly into the wall, power cycle, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the outlet checks out but 9E1 persists, the control board voltage-sensing circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas dryers with digital fault-code displays, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing when the dryer is plugged into an extension cord or power strip, or on a circuit shared with other heavy loads. Plugging directly into a properly wired wall outlet and power cycling often clears it; if it returns with a verified good outlet, the control board is the likely suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — main and sub control boards not communicating
error_codes.detail: The AC (or AC6) code fires when the main control board and the sub/UI board fail to exchange data reliably over their inter-board connection. The control monitors this handshake continuously; when messages are missing or corrupted, it flags a communication fault. Unlike a sensor or heater code, AC points at the boards or the harness linking them rather than a specific component. A loose or corroded ribbon connector triggers it far more often than an actual board failure, which distinguishes it from board-death codes.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes — a transient AC often clears.

fixes.title: Reseat the harness
fixes.description: Open the console and firmly reseat the ribbon/harness between the main and UI boards.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, replace the UI board first (cheaper), then the main board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart. If AC returns, reseat the harness between the control boards.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness is sound, professional diagnosis identifies which board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers with a two-board control architecture (separate main and UI/sub boards), spanning both electric and gas models across recent model years
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report AC appearing at startup or mid-cycle with a frozen display or unresponsive buttons, and many find a simple unplug-for-ten-minutes power cycle clears it, at least temporarily. When it keeps returning, reseating the harness between the console boards is the fix people mention most often; humidity or vibration over time can loosen those connectors.

error_codes.meaning: Frequency error — abnormal power supply frequency
error_codes.detail: The control board samples the incoming AC line and measures its frequency, expecting a stable 60 Hz in North America. FE fires when the measured frequency drifts outside the accepted window, prompting the board to halt operation so the motor is never driven at an incorrect speed. Unlike voltage-related faults, FE is specifically about cycle rate rather than magnitude, which is why it commonly surfaces on generator or inverter power that produces an unstable or off-spec waveform.

causes.description: Power inverter or generator with non-standard frequency

fixes.title: Check power source
fixes.description: If using a generator or inverter, ensure it outputs a clean 60 Hz sine wave.

fixes.title: Test on utility power
fixes.description: Plug directly into utility power (not generator) to confirm.

fixes.title: Service the control board
fixes.description: If on utility power and FE persists, the frequency monitoring circuit on the board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore proper power source and unplug 5 minutes. Restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: FE on utility power without a generator indicates a control board fault.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas dryers with electronic control boards that monitor incoming AC power, broadly across recent model years
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report FE when running the dryer from a portable generator, an inverter, or during unstable utility conditions, and it often clears once the machine is plugged back into clean household power. When the code persists on normal utility power with no generator involved, owners typically find the fault points to the control board rather than the outlet.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — connection between boards
error_codes.detail: Et fires when the main control board loses reliable serial communication with the sub-control or display board over the interconnecting harness. The main board expects periodic handshake data; when responses are missing, corrupted, or timed out, it flags Et. Unlike a component-level sensor fault, this is a board-to-board link failure, so it commonly appears as unresponsive buttons or garbled display while the drum may still power up. It differs from bC2, which points more specifically at button/keypad-level faults.

causes.description: Wiring harness connector loose between boards
Main control board communication circuit failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart. Communication errors sometimes clear.

fixes.title: Inspect wire harness connections
fixes.description: Access the control board area and check all multi-pin connectors for secure seating.

fixes.title: Replace the sub-board
fixes.description: The sub-board (user interface board) is typically cheaper than the main board.

fixes.title: Replace main control board
fixes.description: If sub-board replacement doesn't help.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If Et returns, check all wiring connections before replacing boards.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Et diagnoses require working through both boards systematically.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers with a separate main board and user-interface/sub-board architecture, including many touch-control front-load models roughly 2014 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report the code appearing at startup or randomly mid-cycle, with buttons that stop responding or a display showing nonsense characters. A power cycle sometimes clears it temporarily, and reseating the connectors between boards is the quick win many describe before any board is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Button card error — control panel communication failure
error_codes.detail: The button card behind the control panel has lost its data link to the main control board, so presses don't register and the dryer won't start. A loose or partly unseated ribbon connector between the panel and the main board is the most common cause, not a failed card.

causes.description: Ribbon/connector between button card and main board loose
Main board UI communication port failure

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes so the boards reset, then restart and check whether the buttons respond again.

fixes.title: Reseat the button-card connector
fixes.description: Open the console and firmly reseat the ribbon cable between the button card and the main control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Clean the connector contacts
fixes.description: Inspect the connector pins for corrosion and clean them with electronics contact cleaner before reconnecting.

fixes.title: Replace the button card
fixes.description: If the connector is secure but bC2 stays, the button card itself has failed and needs replacing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes to let both boards power down, then reconnect, reseat the ribbon cable between the button card and main board, and restart a cycle to confirm the buttons respond.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new button card and a securely reseated, clean connector still throw bC2 — at that point the main board's UI communication port has failed and the control board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: High temperature detected — overheating safety shutdown
error_codes.detail: The HC code fires when the dryer's control board reads a drum or exhaust temperature above the safe threshold, typically after the high-limit thermostat trips on the heater circuit. Restricted airflow lets heat build past design limits, so the board halts the cycle to prevent damage. Unlike a blown thermal fuse—which kills all heat and often shows no-heat symptoms—HC reflects an active overheat event, and unlike OD it points to temperature rather than extended drying time.

causes.description: High-limit thermostat failure (rare — usually victim not cause)

fixes.title: Clean the entire vent system
fixes.description: Clean lint filter, full duct length, and exterior vent cap. This is the fix in most cases.

fixes.title: Test and replace the high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Once tripped by overheating, high-limit thermostats can fail permanently. Test for continuity — failed = open circuit.

fixes.title: Test the thermal fuse
fixes.description: If the fuse has blown, the dryer won't heat at all. Test continuity — no continuity = blown fuse.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the vent and replacing the thermostat, unplug 5 minutes and restart. Always confirm vent is clear before the first test run.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent is completely clear and thermostats test good but overheating continues, the heating element may be stuck on (failed relay) requiring professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas dryers with thermal safety monitoring, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report HC appearing partway through a cycle, often after months without cleaning the exhaust duct or vent cap. It is frequently a false alarm for a failed part—a full vent cleaning tends to resolve it, and many find the quickest win is checking the exterior vent flap and the run behind the dryer for lint buildup.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — internal signal failure
error_codes.detail: The main control board and a sub-board (display or motor controller) have stopped exchanging signals, so the dryer freezes mid-cycle or won't accept commands. A loose ribbon/harness between the boards or connector corrosion from laundry-room humidity is the usual cause, not a dead board.

causes.description: Inter-board wiring harness loose or unseated
Main control board communication circuit failure

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to clear a transient AE, then restart a cycle and watch whether the code returns.

fixes.title: Reseat the board harnesses
fixes.description: Open the console, unplug each ribbon/harness between the main and sub boards, and firmly reconnect them.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Clean corroded connectors
fixes.description: Inspect the multi-pin connectors for green/white corrosion and clean the contacts with electronics cleaner.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If the harness is sound, swap the cheaper sub-board first, then the main control board if AE persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes to fully drain the boards, then restore power and start a short cycle; if AE returns immediately, reseat the harness between the main and sub boards before testing again.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if reseating and cleaning the connectors doesn't clear AE — professional diagnosis is needed to identify which board has failed before spending money on the more expensive main control board.

error_codes.meaning: Leak sensor activated — water detected at base of dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The LC code (also shown as LE or 1C) fires when the leak sensor or float switch in the base pan detects standing water. The control board interrupts the cycle and typically forces a continuous drain to protect against flooding until the pan is dry and the fault clears. Unlike drain-related codes such as 5C or overflow code OC, LC specifically indicates water has accumulated at the base rather than a blockage or drain-timing failure.

causes.description: Overloaded detergent causing excessive suds and foam overflow
Loose or failed door seal allowing water to leak during cycle
Cracked or loose water inlet hose connection
Drain hose loose at pump connection
Wash arm cracked or broken, spraying water sideways

fixes.title: Remove water from the base pan
fixes.description: Tilt the dishwasher slightly forward (remove the bottom rack first) to drain accumulated water from the base pan. Use towels to soak up remaining water.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before tilting the unit.

fixes.title: Check detergent usage
fixes.description: If you used too much detergent or the wrong type (non-HE), this creates excess foam that overflows into the base. Use only dishwasher-specific detergent, maximum 1 tablespoon per cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixes.description: Run your hand along the entire door seal. Look for tears, hardened sections, or sections that have pulled away from the channel. A bad seal lets water out during the cycle.

fixes.title: Check all hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect the water supply line, inlet valve connection, drain hose at the pump, and drain hose at the sink drain. Tighten any loose fittings. Replace cracked hoses.

fixes.title: Inspect the wash arms
fixes.description: Remove the lower wash arm and check for cracks. A cracked arm sprays water at pressure against the base, triggering the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After removing water from the base pan and fixing the leak source, the sensor should reset automatically once dried. Run the dishwasher with no detergent on a short cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the leak source is not obvious and the LC code persists, the leak detection float switch itself may be stuck. A technician will be able to locate the exact leak path.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers with a base-pan leak sensor, including models such as DW80R5060US, DW80K5050US, DW80B6060US, and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle, and a frequent false trigger is excess suds from too much or non-dishwasher detergent rather than an actual leak. Many find the quick win is drying the base pan and switching to a small amount of dishwasher-specific detergent, after which the code clears on a rinse-only run.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining from dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher cannot drain within the expected time. Water will remain standing in the bottom of the tub. This usually means the drain pump is blocked or the drain hose is kinked.

causes.description: Debris blocking the drain filter and sump area
Blocked sink drain or garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew the cylindrical drain filter (turn counterclockwise), lift out the flat filter underneath, and rinse both under running water. This should be cleaned monthly.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Trace the drain hose from the back of the dishwasher to the sink drain/garbage disposal. Make sure it has a high loop (up to the underside of the counter) and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal
fixes.description: If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal before starting the dishwasher. Also confirm the knockout plug was removed when the disposal was installed.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, the pump may have failed. You should hear it running when the dishwasher attempts to drain. No sound = possible pump failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a rinse cycle. The code clears once a complete drain cycle succeeds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if the drain filter and hose are clean but the pump is not running — the pump motor or wiring needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor error — circulation pump failure
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the circulation pump motor's speed and current feedback while it should be spraying water during the wash phase. 3C (also shown as 3E) fires when the motor stalls, draws abnormal current, or returns no rotation signal — often from a jammed impeller or failed winding. This distinguishes it from drain-pump faults like 5C, which involve water not leaving the tub rather than the wash motor itself failing to circulate.

causes.description: Foreign object (broken glass, bone) jammed in pump impeller

fixes.title: Check for debris in the pump
fixes.description: Remove the filter assembly and reach into the sump area. Feel around the pump impeller for any hard debris. Remove carefully.
fixes.warning: Sharp broken glass may be present — use gloves.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Disconnect power for 5 minutes and restart. If the pump jammed briefly, a reset may clear it.

fixes.title: Test the pump motor
fixes.description: Access the pump from under the dishwasher. Test the motor windings with a multimeter. No continuity indicates a burned motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing any jams, unplug for 1 minute then restart. Pump replacement requires significant disassembly and the error will clear after a successful wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement is a complex repair best left to a technician if you're not experienced with appliance repair.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers using the 3C/3E code family, including models such as DW80R5060US, DW80K5050US, DW80B6060US, and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing shortly after a cycle starts or after a glass has broken inside, with water not spraying and dishes left dirty. A frequent quick win is clearing debris lodged in the sump or impeller and power cycling; if the code returns after a clean pump, the motor itself is usually at fault.

error_codes.meaning: Heater error — water not heating
error_codes.detail: The heating element is not bringing the water up to temperature, so dishes aren't sanitized and the heated-dry stage leaves them wet. A burned-out element or a failed NTC sensor that misreports water temperature are the most common causes; the heater relay is the next suspect.

causes.description: Loose wiring at the element terminals

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: With power off, measure across the element terminals — about 15–30 ohms is normal; no continuity means it's open.
fixes.warning: Cut power at the breaker before testing.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Measure the temperature sensor at room temperature — roughly 6k–7k ohms is normal; far off means replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Install the element or sensor that tested bad and run a full cycle to confirm the water now heats.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker for 5 minutes after replacing the element or sensor, then run a full normal cycle and check that the water heats and the dishes come out dry to confirm HE has cleared.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if both the element and the NTC sensor test within spec but HE persists, since the control board's heater relay has then failed and requires board-level diagnosis and replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor failure — NTC thermistor fault
error_codes.detail: The NTC water-temperature thermistor is reading out of range, so the control can't verify wash and sanitize temperatures and disables heating and heated dry. A failed thermistor or a loose connector at the sensor is the usual cause; the heating element itself is unaffected.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: With power off, measure the sensor near the heating element — about 6k–7k ohms at room temperature is normal.
fixes.warning: Turn off the breaker or unplug before testing.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Find the two-wire sensor plug and firmly reseat it; a loose or corroded connector reads as an open circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: If the reading is far off spec, replace the NTC sensor — it's usually clipped near the heating element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the dishwasher at the breaker or unplug it for 5 minutes, reseat the sensor connector, then run a short test cycle; if tE returns and the thermistor reads out of spec, replace the sensor rather than resetting again.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new thermistor and a securely seated connector still trigger tE, since the fault then lies in the control board's sensor input circuit, which requires board-level diagnosis to confirm.

error_codes.meaning: Water wall reflector error — water wall sensor blocked
error_codes.detail: On Water Wall models, a moving bar sweeps across the tub floor, and an optical sensor tracks its position using a reflective strip. The 7E code fires when the control board cannot confirm the bar's travel — either because the reflector beam is blocked or dirty, or the sensor or drive motor has failed.
