Unlike spray-arm faults, this is specific to the Water Wall positioning system, so a dish overhanging the bottom rack is enough to trip it.

causes.description: Large item blocking water wall reflector strip
Water wall motor or sensor failure

fixes.title: Check for obstructions
fixes.description: Ensure no large pots or pans are blocking the bottom rack reflector strip.

fixes.title: Clean the reflector strip
fixes.description: Wipe the silver reflector strip on the bottom rack with a damp cloth.

fixes.title: Check water wall motor
fixes.description: The water wall should move back and forth during the wash. If stationary, the motor has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear obstructions and restart. If 7E persists, the water wall motor may need replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung Water Wall dishwashers, primarily DW80K and DW80H series equipped with the moving water wall bar rather than a spray arm
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Water Wall motor replacement requires Samsung-authorized parts and careful disassembly.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing when a tall pot, cutting board, or item hanging off the bottom rack blocks the bar's path, and the quick win is usually just clearing the obstruction and wiping the silver strip clean. It sometimes false-triggers from a discolored or soap-filmed reflector, so cleaning before replacing parts is often worth trying.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The 1E code fires when the control board reads implausible signals from the water inlet flow sensor, which counts the volume entering during fill. If pulses fall below the expected threshold within the allotted fill time, the board flags a sensor failure rather than a simple no-water fault. This distinguishes it from supply-side leak or overfill codes like 5C: 1E points specifically at the flow sensor, its wiring, or genuinely reduced supply flow that keeps the sensor below its detection threshold.

causes.description: Water supply issue reducing flow below sensor threshold

fixes.title: Check water supply
fixes.description: Confirm supply valve open, hose not kinked.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Remove and clean the inlet valve mesh screen.

fixes.title: Test flow sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect and check with multimeter.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve assembly
fixes.description: If sensor is integrated with the valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply or replacing sensor, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and sensor replaced but 1E persists, control board water detection failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers using flow-sensor water metering, including models such as DW80R5060US, DW80K5050US, DW80B6060US and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 1E appearing early in a cycle right after filling, and a partially closed supply valve, kinked hose, or clogged inlet screen is a frequent false trigger. Many find that restoring full water flow or reseating the sensor connector clears it before any part replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — control panel button shorted
error_codes.detail: A button on the control panel is registering as continuously pressed or has an internal short, so the control locks out the keypad and won't start a cycle. A key stuck from a food or detergent splash, or a failing membrane panel, is almost always the cause rather than the main board.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around every button with a dry cloth and free any key that doesn't spring back fully.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Cut power at the breaker or unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient stuck-key reading, then retry.

fixes.title: Replace the control panel
fixes.description: If a key stays stuck or bE2 returns, the membrane/touch panel has failed and needs replacing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker or unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes, clear and dry any stuck button, then restore power and start a cycle; if bE2 returns, the control panel membrane has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new control panel doesn't resolve bE2, because the fault then lies in the main board's button-input circuit, which requires board-level diagnosis and replacement to fix correctly.

error_codes.meaning: Low water level error — dishwasher not filling enough
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detects that the water level inside the tub is too low to run the cycle. Either not enough water is entering, or the level-sensing components believe the tub is under-filled. Samsung dishwashers feed from a small fill reservoir/case-break before the sump, so a clogged inlet, a partially closed supply, or a kinked fill hose commonly starves the wash.

causes.description: Water supply valve partially closed or fill hose kinked
Water level (case break) sensor or float fault

fixes.title: Check the water supply
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink supply valve is fully open and the fill hose isn't kinked or crushed.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill hose, and clear the inlet screen/filter of mineral debris.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply before disconnecting the hose.

fixes.title: Run a clean test cycle
fixes.description: Restart with a normal supply to confirm the tub fills. Persistent 9E after a clean fill points to the inlet valve or level sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm the supply valve is open and the fill hose is clear, then power cycle. If 9E returns once filling begins, test the inlet valve.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and inlet screen are confirmed clear but 9E persists, the inlet valve or water-level sensor has failed and needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — a control-panel key is stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously scans each panel key and flags bE when one input reads as pressed for longer than a valid keystroke. Because the board can't distinguish a genuine command from a jammed contact, it halts operation. The trigger is usually a physically stuck key or a degraded membrane keypad rather than a cycle fault; bE covers the general button error, while bE1/bE2 point to specific key groups on the panel.

fixes.title: Wipe the button area
fixes.description: Clean around the buttons with a dry cloth and free any physically stuck key.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient stuck-key reading.

fixes.title: Replace the control panel
fixes.description: If a key stays stuck, the membrane/touch panel needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and clear any stuck button before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new control panel doesn't resolve bE, the main board's button interface has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers with membrane or touch-style control panels, including top-control models such as DW80R5060US, DW80K5050US, DW80B6060US, and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report bE appearing right after a spill near the console, steam exposure, or aggressive wiping while wet, and a quick wipe-down plus a five-minute power cycle often clears it. If a single key still feels sticky or unresponsive afterward, the membrane panel is the likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Access Point mode — Wi-Fi pairing, not a fault
error_codes.detail: AP is a status indicator, not an error. The control board displays it when the dishwasher enters Access Point mode, broadcasting a temporary setup network so a phone can join it and hand off home Wi-Fi credentials through SmartThings. It reflects a deliberate connectivity state, distinct from true fault codes that report sensor or component failures. It appears at the start of Wi-Fi setup or when the Smart Control button is held, and clears once pairing completes or the mode is exited.

causes.description: Connectivity button held long enough to enter AP mode
Display showing AP after a power event during pairing

fixes.title: Complete or cancel pairing
fixes.description: Finish the SmartThings Wi-Fi setup to connect, or press the connectivity/Smart Control button to exit AP mode.

fixes.title: Power cycle to clear
fixes.description: If you don't want to connect, unplug for 1 minute; the dishwasher returns to normal operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Either finish Wi-Fi setup in the SmartThings app or press the Smart Control button to leave AP mode. No repair is needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: AP is not a fault — a service call is unnecessary. Contact support only if the dishwasher will not exit AP mode after a power cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: Wi-Fi/SmartThings-enabled Samsung dishwashers, including models such as the DW80B6060US and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly see AP right after tapping Smart Control or when first pairing with SmartThings, and sometimes mistake it for a malfunction. A quick press of the connectivity button or a brief power cycle usually clears it if you don't intend to connect.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator fan error — fresh-food compartment fan not running
error_codes.detail: 22E (also shown as 22C) fires when the control board expects the fresh-food compartment fan to be running but detects no rotational feedback from its sensing circuit. Because that fan circulates cold air into the fridge section, the symptom is a warming fridge while the freezer stays cold. It differs from 21E, which points to the freezer evaporator fan. Frost from a weak defrost cycle jamming the blade is the usual trigger, versus an outright motor or wiring failure.

causes.description: Ice or frost buildup jamming the fan blade (defrost-system weakness)
Fan motor failure (bearing failure or burned windings)
Fan blade physically broken or obstructed
Wiring harness to fan motor damaged or loose

fixes.title: Defrost the fridge-section evaporator
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator and let it fully thaw (12–24 hours, doors open, towels down) — or run forced defrost from the panel. Ice jamming the fan blade is the most common cause and a thaw clears it without parts.

fixes.title: Inspect the fan blade
fixes.description: Remove the back interior panel of the fridge compartment to expose the fan. Spin the blade by hand — it should rotate freely. Clear any ice or debris.

fixes.title: Check the wiring connector
fixes.description: Reseat the fan connector at the motor and check the harness where it passes through the cabinet for chafing.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but the motor doesn't spin when powered, replace the fan motor assembly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, press and hold both the 'Energy Saver' and 'Power Freeze' buttons simultaneously for 5 seconds to clear diagnostic error codes (button pair varies by model).
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan ices over again within weeks, the defrost system (heater, sensor, or control) is the real fault — replacing the fan alone won't fix it. That diagnosis is worth a technician visit.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators with a separate fresh-food evaporator fan, including French-door, side-by-side, and bottom-freezer designs such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing alongside a fridge that won't stay cold while the freezer seems fine, often after frost quietly builds behind the rear interior panel. A full thaw or forced defrost frequently clears it as a quick win, but if it returns within weeks the defrost system is usually the real culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer fan error — freezer evaporator fan not running
error_codes.detail: The control board is not sensing rotational feedback from the freezer fan when it should be turning. This is the fan that pulls air across the freezer evaporator coils — without it, both freezer and fridge cooling degrade. Per Samsung's service documentation the usual culprits are ice jamming the blade or a failed fan motor.

causes.description: Ice or frost buildup jamming the fan blade
Fan motor winding or bearing failure
Fan wire harness disconnected or damaged

fixes.title: Defrost the freezer section
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator and let all ice melt (12–24 hours), or run forced defrost from the control panel. This clears the most common cause — an iced fan blade.

fixes.title: Inspect blade and wiring
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel, spin the fan blade by hand, and reseat the connector at the fan motor and control board.

fixes.title: Replace the freezer fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but the motor won't spin when powered, replace it.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the freezer back panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold 'Energy Saver' + 'Power Freeze' for 5 seconds to clear the code after repair (button pair varies by model).
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost keeps returning to the freezer back panel after a full thaw, the defrost heater or sensor is failing — that's the repair to make, not another fan.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor error — freezer temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The 24E code (also shown as 24C) fires when the control board reads an out-of-range signal from the freezer evaporator NTC thermistor clipped to the coils. Because the board relies on this sensor to time defrost heater cycles, an open, shorted, or drifting reading trips the fault. It differs from 25E, which points to the defrost heater or thermal cutoff circuit itself; 24E is specifically the sensor input feeding that logic.

fixes.title: Locate the evaporator sensor
fixes.description: The evaporator sensor is clipped to the evaporator coils behind the back panel in the freezer compartment.

fixes.title: Test the sensor resistance
fixes.description: At 32°F/0°C, a healthy Samsung thermistor reads approximately 16.3k ohms. Test and compare to spec.

fixes.title: Check and reseat connector
fixes.description: The connector often vibrates loose over time. Unplug and firmly reseat.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: The thermistor clips onto the evaporator coil. Unclip the old one, clip in the new one, reconnect.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Run a manual defrost cycle by holding 'Freezer Temp' and 'Lighting' for 8 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor is new and correct but errors continue, the main PCB sensor input circuit may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators using the numeric fault-code system, including French-door and side-by-side models such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code surfacing alongside frost buildup on the back freezer panel or a warming freezer, and sometimes after a power blip. A loose or corroded connector behind the rear panel is a frequent false trigger, and many find reseating that plug clears it before any part swap is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer defrost sensor error
error_codes.detail: 25E indicates the control board reads the freezer defrost thermistor as out of range—typically an open circuit, or an out-of-spec resistance from a loose connector or ice-bridged short. This sensor confirms the evaporator has warmed enough to end each defrost cycle. Without a trusted reading, the board cannot safely terminate defrost. Unlike 24E, which points to the defrost function or heater itself, 25E is specifically the temperature sensor feedback that governs cycle timing.

causes.description: Connector loose at sensor or control board
Sensor shorted from ice or moisture

fixes.title: Locate the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Behind the freezer back panel, near the defrost heater. Often a two-wire sensor clipped to the evaporator.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature: should read approximately 5.5k–6.5k ohms. At 32°F: ~16k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect, unclip from evaporator, and install new sensor. Handle the evaporator carefully.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after repair. The code should clear after a successful defrost cycle (every 6–12 hours normally).
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring defrost errors with correctly functioning sensors point to a defrost heater or timer issue requiring professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators using the two-digit fault-code system with a dedicated freezer defrost sensor, including French-door, side-by-side, and bottom-freezer models such as the RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a period of ice buildup on the rear freezer panel or following a frost-heavy defrost. A loose or moisture-corroded connector at the sensor is a frequent false trigger, and reseating the plug or clearing ice sometimes clears the fault before any part swap is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Display test mode active — not a fault code
error_codes.detail: The 88-88 readout is not a fault but the control board's display or demo (showroom) mode indication, lighting every segment as all 8s. In this state the board deliberately disables the compressor and cooling system so the unit runs cold-free for retail display. It differs from genuine sensor or communication faults, which show alphanumeric codes; here the board reports no error at all, only that normal temperature control has been suspended pending exit from the mode.

causes.description: Display board failure causing erratic readout

fixes.title: Exit demo mode
fixes.description: The method depends on your model. Most commonly: hold 'Energy Saver' and 'Power Freeze' simultaneously for 5 seconds. Or: hold 'Freezer Temp' and 'Refrigerator Temp' for 5 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your manual
fixes.description: If the above combination doesn't work, check your specific model's manual for the demo mode exit sequence.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Exit demo mode as described above. The display should return to normal temperature readings.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you cannot exit demo mode using standard button combinations, the display board may have a software fault. Contact Samsung support.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators with a digital control display across most current French-door, side-by-side, and four-door lines, including models such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report seeing 88-88 right after unboxing a new or floor-model fridge, or after a power interruption that retriggered demo mode, and worry the food never gets cold. The usual quick win is holding the two designated buttons for about five seconds until a chime confirms the display returns to normal temperatures.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker function error — ice maker not cycling
error_codes.detail: The ice maker is detecting a fault in its operating cycle. The ice maker motor or sensor has not responded within the expected time. Ice production has stopped.

causes.description: Ice maker frozen solid — ice stuck in tray or ejection arm
Water inlet solenoid to ice maker not opening

fixes.title: Force a manual defrost
fixes.description: Hold the test/reset button on the ice maker for 3 seconds. If the ice maker is frozen, the motor strain will reveal it.

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker
fixes.description: Turn off the refrigerator and place towels in the freezer. Let the ice maker thaw completely (2–4 hours). Check that the ejection arm moves freely.

fixes.title: Test the ice maker water supply
fixes.description: Listen for the water fill valve clicking when a new cycle starts. If you hear clicking but no water fills, the inlet solenoid to the ice maker is faulty.

fixes.title: Replace ice maker assembly
fixes.description: If defrost and water supply are fine but the motor still won't cycle, replace the ice maker module.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press the reset button on the ice maker. Alternatively, turn the ice maker off and back on using the ice maker switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the ice maker is replaced and errors continue, the freezer is not cold enough (below 0°F/-18°C required) or the control board is not sending power to the ice maker.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor error
error_codes.detail: The sensor watching the evaporator temperature during defrost has failed, so the control board can't tell when the coils have cleared and may end defrost early. Frost then builds on the evaporator and cooling slowly weakens. A burned-out sensor is most common; a loose connector is next.

causes.description: Heavy frost skewing the sensor reading

fixes.title: Manually defrost the freezer
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge and let all frost on the coils melt fully — several hours — before testing the sensor.

fixes.title: Test the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Measure resistance — about 16k ohms at 32°F and ~6k ohms at room temperature; far off means replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip the failed sensor from the evaporator coil and fit the new one, then reconnect its plug firmly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the sensor, plug the fridge back in and allow about 24 hours for the cabinet to stabilize and a normal defrost cycle to run before judging whether 5E and any frost buildup are resolved.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if frost rebuilds on the coils after the sensor is replaced, since the defrost heater or defrost control circuit has then failed and needs professional testing to isolate the faulty part.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The ice maker's temperature sensor has failed, so the control can't tell when the tray is cold enough to harvest a batch and ice production stalls. An open-circuit sensor is the usual cause; frost packed around it or a loose connector can also throw 8E before the part is truly dead.

causes.description: Frost on the sensor skewing readings

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker
fixes.description: Turn the ice maker off and let any frost around the sensor and tray melt fully before testing.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Measure the sensor at room temperature — roughly 5k–10k ohms is normal; an open reading means replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip the small sensor from the ice maker assembly, fit the new one, and reseat its connector.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the sensor, press the ice maker's reset/test button and allow a full harvest cycle; give the unit several hours to produce the first batch before deciding whether 8E has truly cleared.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if 8E returns soon after the sensor is replaced, since the ice maker module or its wiring harness then has a broader fault that needs professional diagnosis and likely a full assembly swap.

error_codes.meaning: Ambient temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: The ambient sensor that measures room temperature — used to fine-tune cooling and defrost timing — is reading out of range, so the fridge falls back to a default profile. It keeps cooling but may run less efficiently in a very hot or cold room. A failed thermistor is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Locate the ambient sensor
fixes.description: Find it near the top of the fresh-food compartment or behind a door shelf, depending on the model.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Measure resistance and compare to spec — roughly 6k ohms at room temperature; an open reading means replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Swap the thermistor if it's out of spec and reseat the connector, then power cycle to clear the code.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the sensor or reseating its connector, unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, then restore power; allow the cabinet a few hours to stabilize before confirming 6E has cleared.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician only if 6E persists after the ambient sensor is replaced, since the fault then lies in the control board's sensor input — though 6E rarely threatens cooling and is low urgency next to fan or compressor errors.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker sensor failure — ice maker position sensor
error_codes.detail: A sensor in the ice maker assembly that monitors the ice tray or ejection arm position has failed.

causes.description: Ice jam preventing sensor from reading correctly

fixes.title: Defrost ice maker
fixes.description: Let the ice maker area fully thaw.

fixes.title: Test and replace position sensor
fixes.description: Access the ice maker assembly and test the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace ice maker module
fixes.description: If individual sensor is not serviceable.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press ice maker reset button after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice maker is replaced and error persists, control board ice maker input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Pump / compressor communication error — critical cooling failure
error_codes.detail: The control board cannot communicate with the variable-speed compressor or its control module. This is a critical error. The refrigerator will stop cooling entirely.

causes.description: Wiring harness fault between board and compressor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes. Some communication errors clear on restart.
fixes.warning: If food is warm, move perishables immediately.

fixes.title: Check wiring harness to compressor
fixes.description: Access the compressor area (back/bottom of unit) and check the communication wire harness for damage or loose connections.

fixes.title: Contact Samsung service
fixes.description: PC ER on Samsung's linear/inverter compressors is often covered under Samsung's 10-year compressor warranty — even out of standard warranty.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart. If PC ER returns, do not attempt DIY compressor repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PC ER requires professional diagnosis. Samsung offers a 10-year compressor warranty — call 1-800-726-7864 and provide the model number before paying for service.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker defrost sensor error
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or shorted circuit from the sensor that monitors the ice maker's defrost cycle. Without it, the ice compartment cannot defrost properly, so frost builds up and ice production slows or stops. Most often the sensor itself has failed; a loose connector is the next suspect.

fixes.title: Defrost ice maker section
fixes.description: Fully defrost before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Replace defrost sensor
fixes.description: Located on or near the ice maker evaporator.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a full defrost cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor replaced and error recurs, the defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Demo / Cooling Off (shop) mode — not a fault code
error_codes.detail: Your refrigerator is in Demo mode (also called Cooling Off or Shop mode). This is a showroom setting: the lights and display work normally but the compressor and cooling are intentionally disabled so the unit can be displayed without running. It is NOT a malfunction. It is usually triggered accidentally by holding the energy-saver/freezer buttons, or set at the store before delivery.

causes.description: Unit was left in shop mode from the retailer
Power event during a button press

fixes.title: Exit Demo mode
fixes.description: Press and hold the two designated buttons together (commonly Energy Saver + Power Freeze, or Freezer + Fridge) until you hear a chime. Cooling resumes.

fixes.title: Try the extended combination
fixes.description: If the first combination doesn't work, hold the three buttons your model specifies (often Button 1 + Button 2 + Button 4) until a chime sounds.

fixes.title: Check your model's manual
fixes.description: Button labels vary by model; confirm the exact Demo-mode exit sequence for your unit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the designated button combination until a chime sounds to leave Demo/Cooling Off mode; the refrigerator will begin cooling again.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: OF OF is not a fault — no service is needed. Contact Samsung only if the unit will not exit demo mode using any documented button combination.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators across most current lines with a digital display panel, including French-door, side-by-side, and top/bottom-freezer models such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing right after a new delivery or after cleaning the display, when buttons are pressed inadvertently. A frequent tell is that the interior lights and panel look fine but the fridge never gets cold and the compressor stays silent. The usual quick win is holding the correct two- or three-button combination until a chime sounds, after which cooling resumes on its own.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: E-08 fires when the control board reads an open circuit on the oven's NTC temperature probe — resistance measures effectively infinite instead of the expected ~1,080 ohms at room temperature. Without a valid sensor signal the board cannot regulate cavity heat safely, so it locks out heating. This differs from E-09, which typically indicates a shorted sensor (resistance too low) rather than a broken or disconnected one. A loose or corroded 2-pin connector can mimic the same open reading.

causes.description: Connector at sensor loose or corroded

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the sensor connector. Test resistance: at room temperature, expect 1,080–1,090 ohms for a standard oven sensor. An open circuit (OL/∞) means the sensor has failed.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Before replacing, reseat the 2-pin connector at the sensor. Corrosion on the pins can mimic an open circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the temperature sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is in the upper-rear of the oven cavity, usually 1–2 screws. Disconnect the 2-wire connector and install the new sensor.
fixes.warning: Let oven cool completely before reaching inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, plug in and test with a short bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung NE/NX series gas and electric ranges, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and E-08 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E-08 appearing at power-on or partway into a bake cycle, with the oven refusing to heat. Before buying parts, many find that simply reseating the sensor connector at the upper-rear of the cavity clears a false trigger caused by corrosion or a loose pin.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven's RTD temperature probe is reading abnormally low resistance — near a dead short — so the control can't trust the cavity temperature and locks out baking to prevent runaway heating. An internally shorted sensor is the usual cause; a pinched or melted sensor wire is next.

causes.description: Sensor wire pinched or melted, shorting to chassis

fixes.title: Test the sensor probe
fixes.description: With the oven cool, measure the RTD sensor — about 1,080–1,090 ohms at room temperature; near 0 ohms confirms a short.
fixes.warning: Let the oven cool completely before testing.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor wiring
fixes.description: Trace the probe wire to the connector and check for melted insulation or a pinch shorting it against the cavity.

fixes.title: Replace the temperature sensor
fixes.description: A shorted RTD probe can't be repaired; unscrew it from the cavity rear wall and install a new one.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Let the oven cool completely, turn the breaker off for 5 minutes, then restore power and run a short bake; if E-09 returns and the probe reads near 0 ohms, replace the sensor rather than resetting again.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new sensor reads correctly but E-09 persists, because the short then lies in the control board's sensor input circuit, which involves high-voltage connections and board-level replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad/touchpad short — stuck button on control panel
error_codes.detail: The SE (or 5E) code fires when the control board reads one of the touchpad buttons as held down continuously beyond a normal press. Because a stuck key blocks all other input, the board halts operation and displays the fault. The most common trigger is a degraded touch membrane, though grease or moisture bridging a contact can mimic it. This is distinct from communication or sensor codes — it points specifically at the panel's key-input circuit rather than heating or temperature hardware.

causes.description: Food/grease on touchpad causing false contact

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe carefully around all touch buttons with a slightly damp cloth. Let dry completely before testing.
fixes.warning: Do not spray water directly — let dry before powering on.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. This can clear transient SE codes.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/membrane panel
fixes.description: If SE returns after cleaning and power cycle, the membrane has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If SE returns, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SE that returns after touchpad replacement indicates the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung touch-control ovens and ranges with a membrane keypad, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SE appearing after spills, steam, or heavy cleaning near the panel, and it sometimes clears on its own once the surface dries. A careful wipe-down and a five-minute breaker reset often resolve false triggers; if the code keeps returning, the membrane has usually failed and needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Oven overheating — temperature above safe limit
error_codes.detail: The control board reads the oven temperature sensor and fires E-0A when it detects a value above the safe operating limit — roughly 570°F in normal cooking or around 900°F during self-clean. The code signals either a genuine runaway (a relay welded closed keeps an element powered) or a sensor reporting falsely high resistance. Unlike E-08/E-09, which flag general sensor faults or open circuits, E-0A specifically indicates an over-temperature condition that shuts the oven down for safety.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure causing incorrect readings

fixes.title: Power off immediately
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker. Allow oven to cool completely — at least 1 hour.
fixes.warning: Do NOT open the oven door during overheating. Stand clear.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor
fixes.description: After cooling, check sensor resistance (should be ~1,080 ohms at room temp).

fixes.title: Check bake/broil element
fixes.description: Inspect for visible burn marks, holes, or arcing on either heating element.

fixes.title: Replace failed element
fixes.description: If element has visible damage.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Do not use until inspected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E-0A requires professional inspection. A runaway relay requires control board replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ranges with electronic control boards, including freestanding and slide-in models such as the NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code during or after a self-clean cycle, or when an element glows and won't turn off. Some cases turn out to be a failed sensor rather than true overheating, but because a stuck relay can be dangerous, most owners cut power at the breaker and hold off on further use until it's inspected.

error_codes.meaning: No flame detected — gas ignition failure (gas range models)
error_codes.detail: On gas models, the control monitors the oven's flame-sensing circuit during ignition. When the igniter glows, it should draw enough current to open the safety gas valve, letting gas flow and light. If no flame is confirmed within the expected window — because the igniter is too weak to open the valve, ports are clogged, or gas is shut off — the board fires NF and cuts gas as a safety measure. This differs from electric heating faults, which report failed elements or sensor readings rather than absent flame.

causes.description: Igniter too weak to open gas valve (most common)
Clogged burner ports from spilled food

fixes.title: Check gas supply
fixes.description: Ensure gas supply valve behind range is fully open. Check other gas appliances in the home.

fixes.title: Clean burner ports
fixes.description: Clogged burner ports prevent gas flow. Remove burner cap, clean ports with a pin or toothpick.
fixes.warning: Do not use water — let burner dry completely before igniting.

fixes.title: Test the igniter amperage
fixes.description: A Samsung oven igniter draws 3.2–3.6A when working correctly. Below 3A = too weak to open gas valve.

fixes.title: Replace the igniter
fixes.description: If igniter glows but draws < 3.2A, replace it. This is the most common NF repair.
fixes.warning: Gas igniter replacement: always test for gas leaks after.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Check gas supply and burner cleanliness. If NF persists, test igniter amperage.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung gas ranges, including the NX58R5601SS
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: All gas igniter work should be checked for leaks with soapy water after repair. If you smell gas, do not ignite — call the gas company.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report NF appearing after a big spill or boil-over clogs the burner ports, or after the gas was shut off for other work. A weakening igniter that glows but no longer lights promptly is a frequent culprit, and many find that cleaning the ports or replacing the igniter resolves it.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock motor failure — self-clean door latch issue
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors a feedback switch on the self-clean door latch to confirm the lock motor drives the latch fully to its locked or unlocked position within an expected time window. E-3 fires when that confirmation never arrives — the motor stalled, the switch failed to actuate, or wiring broke the signal. Unlike temperature or sensor faults, this is purely a mechanical lock-position error, most often surfacing when a self-clean cycle is started or ending.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. A transient E-3 can be cleared this way.

fixes.title: Check door alignment
fixes.description: Ensure oven door closes flush. A warped door can prevent the lock mechanism from engaging.

fixes.title: Test and replace door lock motor
fixes.description: The lock motor is at the top-back of the oven. Test solenoid continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing lock assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes and restart. If E-3 returns, lock motor needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door lock assembly is replaced and E-3 persists, control board door lock relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ranges with a motorized self-clean door latch, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E-3 appearing when starting or finishing a self-clean cycle rather than during normal baking. A warped or not-fully-closed door can falsely trigger it, and many find a simple breaker power cycle clears a one-off occurrence; a code that keeps returning usually points to the lock motor itself.

error_codes.meaning: Power relay failure on main control board
error_codes.detail: A power relay on the main control board that switches a heating element has failed, usually with burned or welded contacts, so the oven can't control that element and shuts down. A power surge or years of switching cycles wears the relay out, and it isn't separately serviceable.

causes.description: Relay worn out from years of cycling

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn the breaker off for 15 minutes, then back on; a transient E-43 from a surge can occasionally clear this way.

fixes.title: Inspect the board relay
fixes.description: With the breaker off, look at the relays on the control board for scorching or melted housings around the contacts.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting anything.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: E-43 almost always needs a new board, since the relay isn't sold separately and high-voltage repair isn't DIY-safe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn the oven's breaker off for 15 minutes to fully de-energize the control board, then restore power; if E-43 returns, the relay contacts have failed and the board must be replaced rather than reset again.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Have a technician replace the control board, since E-43 repair involves disconnecting high-voltage element wiring and matching a board to your exact model — mistakes here risk shock or a runaway heating element.

error_codes.meaning: Button/key problem — a key is registering as pressed
error_codes.detail: C-D0 fires when the control board senses that a touch key has been continuously active for longer than a normal press — the panel interprets it as a stuck or held button. The most frequent trigger is a conductive bridge across the keypad from spilled food, grease, or moisture; a degrading membrane or a faulty touch interface on the control board can also mimic a held key. It is a diagnostic lockout on the panel rather than an oven or heating failure.

fixes.title: Clean the buttons
fixes.description: Wipe around all keys with a barely-damp cloth and make sure there is no water on or around them. Let dry fully.
fixes.warning: Do not spray water directly onto the panel.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the oven (or the breaker) for 30 seconds or more, then restart.

fixes.title: Replace the touch panel
fixes.description: If C-D0 returns after cleaning and a power cycle, the membrane/touch panel has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the keys and power cycle for 30 seconds. If C-D0 persists, the touch panel needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If C-D0 returns after the touchpad is replaced, the control board touch interface has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ranges/ovens with capacitive touch control panels, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report C-D0 appearing after cooking spills, steam, or wiping the panel while it is still wet. In many cases a thorough dry-off around the keys plus a brief power cycle clears it; if the code keeps returning after the panel is dry, the touchpad itself is often the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor fault — check needed
error_codes.detail: C-20 fires when the control board reads the oven's resistance-based temperature sensor and sees a value outside its valid window — typically an open circuit or a resistance too high or low to represent a plausible cavity temperature. The board halts heating because it has no trustworthy feedback. This differs from C-21, which usually flags a shorted or over-range sensor, and from E-08/E-09 errors that point more toward heating or control-side faults rather than a missing sensor signal.

causes.description: Temperature sensor (NTC) open or out of range

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the power for 30 seconds or more, then restart to clear a transient.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance (about 1,080–1,090 ohms at room temperature). An open reading means a failed sensor.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Corrosion on the 2-pin sensor connector can mimic an open circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the temperature sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is at the upper-rear of the cavity, 1–2 screws.
fixes.warning: Let the oven cool before reaching inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 30 seconds. If C-20 returns, test and replace the temperature sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor reads correctly but C-20 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ovens using an RTD-style cavity temperature sensor, including many freestanding and slide-in ranges roughly 2016 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report C-20 appearing at startup or partway through preheat, sometimes after a self-clean cycle stresses the sensor. A loose or corroded 2-pin connector can mimic a dead sensor, so reseating it is a frequent quick win before buying parts; a brief power cycle occasionally clears a one-off glitch.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature too high — sensor reads above setpoint
error_codes.detail: C-21 fires when the cavity temperature sensor reports a reading above the setpoint or the control board's safe upper limit. The board interprets this as an overheat condition and locks out heating as a safety measure. It differs from C-20 (open or shorted sensor) because C-21 reflects a plausible but excessive temperature — pointing to either a truly hot cavity, a heating relay welded closed, or a sensor drifting low in resistance and misreporting heat.

causes.description: Cavity genuinely too hot (let it cool)

fixes.title: Let the oven cool
fixes.description: Turn the oven off, unplug or switch off the breaker, and let it cool for an hour before retrying.
fixes.warning: Do not open the door during overheating; stand clear.

fixes.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixes.description: After cooling, confirm the sensor reads about 1,080 ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Check for a runaway relay
fixes.description: If the oven keeps overheating, a stuck heating relay on the control board is likely.
fixes.warning: An oven that overheats should not be used until repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off and let the oven cool an hour. Do not use it if it keeps overheating.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A genuine overheat (stuck relay) is a safety issue — have it serviced before further use.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ovens/ranges with electronic temperature control, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after long high-heat cooking or a self-clean cycle, where it often clears once the oven fully cools. If it returns quickly or the oven keeps climbing past the setpoint, a stuck relay is the more likely culprit and warrants stopping use until serviced.

error_codes.meaning: Divider/partition check needed (Flex Duo / Dual Cook models)
error_codes.detail: The control board reads a partition/divider switch at the rear of the cavity to confirm whether the divider is installed. dC (or dE) fires when the selected mode's required divider state doesn't match what the switch senses — for example, a Dual Cook mode expects the divider seated but it is missing or not fully engaged. Unlike a true door fault, this is a configuration prompt: the oven halts to prevent running an upper/lower zone without the partition properly positioned.

causes.description: Divider not seated in the correct rack position
Partition switch obstructed at the back of the cavity

fixes.title: Reinstall the divider correctly
fixes.description: Seat the partition fully — each side in the correct rack position for your model (electric/dual fuel vs gas).
fixes.warning: Let the oven cool before reaching inside.

fixes.title: Check the partition switch
fixes.description: If no divider is in use, make sure nothing presses on the partition switch at the back of the cavity.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the power for 30 seconds or more and restart.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reinstall the divider correctly (or clear the partition switch) and restart.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung Flex Duo / Dual Cook ranges and wall ovens (for example the NX58R5601SS); other single-cavity models without a divider do not use this code
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If dC persists with the divider correctly installed, the partition switch or its wiring needs service.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly see this right after removing the divider for a large item or after cleaning, when the panel is bumped or not clicked fully into its rack slots. The usual quick win is reseating the divider on both sides and power-cycling; if it lingers with the divider clearly in place, something is likely pressing on or fouling the rear partition switch.

error_codes.meaning: Door error — door state cannot be confirmed
error_codes.detail: The 'door' message means the control board cannot resolve the door's open/closed state from its switch inputs. Each time the door moves, the plunger switch should change state; if the plunger sticks and never extends, or the lock motor fails to seat during a self-clean cycle, the board sees a contradictory or unchanging signal and flags the fault. This differs from lock-specific faults like dC, which point more directly at the latch motor or its position feedback rather than general door-state confirmation.

causes.description: Door plunger switch stuck or stiff
Wiring to the door switch faulty

fixes.title: Check the door plunger
fixes.description: With the door open, press the plunger switch — it should extend and move freely. If it is stiff or does not extend, it needs service.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the power for 30 seconds or more, then restart.

fixes.title: Test the door switch/latch
fixes.description: Test the switch for continuity; replace the door switch or lock assembly if faulty.
fixes.warning: Let the oven cool and unplug before accessing the latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 30 seconds. If the door code persists, check the door plunger switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the plunger and switch test good, the control board door input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas ranges/ovens with a self-clean door-lock circuit and plunger switch, including models such as NE59M4320SS, NX58R5601SS, and NE63A6511SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing after a self-clean cycle or when the door is slammed, and a simple power cycle often clears it temporarily. A stiff or gummed-up plunger switch is a frequent quiet culprit, so exercising the plunger by hand and cleaning around it sometimes restores normal operation before any parts are needed.

error_codes.meaning: Touchpad keypad error — button stuck or panel failure
error_codes.detail: The SE (sometimes shown as 5E) code fires when the control board detects a keypad input held continuously beyond a normal press, reading it as a stuck button. This can stem from a physically stuck key, a shorted or degraded membrane trace, or moisture and food residue bridging contacts. Unlike a control-board reset fault, SE points specifically at the touch-input layer, which is why the panel stops responding to any further commands until the stuck signal clears.

causes.description: Food splatter causing false key press

fixes.title: Clean the touchpad
fixes.description: Wipe the keypad with a barely-damp cloth. Dry completely before powering on.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace touchpad membrane
fixes.description: If SE returns after cleaning.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 2 minutes. If SE returns after cleaning, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and SE persists, control board failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung countertop and over-the-range microwaves with membrane touch controls, including the ME19R7041FS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SE showing up after cooking greasy or steamy foods, or during humid conditions, and a careful cleaning plus a two-minute unplug often clears it. If it returns quickly after cleaning, the membrane itself has usually failed and needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Touchpad/membrane failure
error_codes.detail: The control board scans the touchpad membrane as a grid of row-and-column contacts. F-3 fires when one key's contacts read as continuously closed, so the board interprets it as a stuck or shorted button and locks out the keypad to prevent unintended operation. It differs from a general SE/5E panel fault in that F-3 points to a single failed key trace, most often from a cracked membrane or absorbed cooking moisture rather than the board's logic itself.

fixes.title: Power cycle the microwave
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes so the control resets, then plug back in and test each key for response.

fixes.title: Wipe the touchpad
fixes.description: Clean around every key with a barely-damp cloth and dry fully — surface moisture can mimic a shorted key.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/membrane
fixes.description: If F-3 returns after a power cycle, the membrane has failed and the touchpad panel must be replaced.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes to clear the control's memory, then restore power and test each button; if F-3 reappears, the membrane has failed and the touchpad needs replacing rather than another reset.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new touchpad doesn't clear F-3, since the fault then lies in the control board's key-input circuit — and any work behind the case exposes high-voltage capacitors that hold a lethal charge.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung countertop and over-the-range microwaves with membrane touchpads, including the ME19R7041FS over-the-range series
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F-3 appearing after steamy cooking or on units mounted over a range, where grease and moisture reach the panel. Many find a quick unplug-and-wipe clears it temporarily, but a code that keeps returning usually means the membrane itself has failed and needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor shorted — over-temperature protection
error_codes.detail: The cavity thermistor is reading as a short, so the microwave can't trust temperature feedback and shuts down to prevent undetected overheating. A failed thermistor is the most common cause, though blocked vents can overheat the cavity and damage the sensor first.

causes.description: Overheating from blocked vents or poor clearance

fixes.title: Let the microwave cool
fixes.description: Unplug for 30 minutes, clear the vent grille, and make sure the microwave has proper air clearance.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: A qualified tech should measure the thermistor; near 0 ohms confirms the sensor or its wiring is shorted.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: If the sensor tests shorted, replace the cavity thermistor and inspect its harness for pinched insulation.
fixes.warning: DANGER: Microwave capacitors hold lethal charge. Discharge before any internal work.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 30 minutes, clear any blocked vents, and restore power only after it has cooled; if F-10 returns, the thermistor or its wiring is shorted and must be replaced by a qualified technician.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Always call a professional for F-10 if cooling and vent cleaning do not clear it, because testing or replacing the internal thermistor requires opening the microwave case where high-voltage capacitors can retain a lethal charge.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad membrane failure — dead or stuck key
error_codes.detail: The control board scans the keypad membrane as a matrix of row and column contacts. F-1 fires when the board reads a key line as permanently closed (stuck) or fails to detect an expected contact, suggesting a shorted or open membrane trace. Unlike SE/5E stuck-key codes on some units, F-1 on Samsung typically points to the membrane or its ribbon rather than the display; a control-board input failure is the least common cause and is diagnosed only after a membrane swap fails.

fixes.title: Power cycle the microwave
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes to reset the control, then plug back in and test whether every key responds normally.

fixes.title: Clean and dry the keypad
fixes.description: Wipe the panel with a barely-damp cloth and dry it fully; moisture around the membrane can hold a key on.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad membrane
fixes.description: If F-1 returns after cleaning and reset, replace the membrane or keypad assembly for that exact model.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes, dry the keypad area, then restore power and test all buttons; if F-1 comes back, the membrane has failed and needs replacement rather than repeated resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new keypad membrane doesn't clear F-1, because the control board's key-input circuit has failed and opening the microwave case exposes components that can retain dangerous high voltage.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung over-the-range and countertop microwaves with membrane touch keypads, including the ME19R7041FS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F-1 appearing on its own during standby, sometimes after cooking steam or grease reaches the panel. A power cycle plus thorough drying of the keypad often clears it temporarily; if the code returns quickly, a worn membrane is the usual culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Drainage error — water not draining within expected time
error_codes.detail: OE fires when the control board doesn't sense the water level dropping to empty within the allotted drain window. It monitors the pressure sensor during the pump phase; if the level stays high past the timeout, the cycle halts with standing water. This differs from LE, which flags a drain-pump motor lock or overload rather than a drainage timeout — OE is about flow, not the motor's electrical condition, though a failed pump can trigger either.

causes.description: Clogged pump filter (lint, coins, small items)

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: The pump filter is behind the lower-front panel. Place towels and a shallow bowl below it. Turn the cap slowly counterclockwise — water will drain out. Remove the filter, clean it, and check for obstructions in the housing.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — expect a significant amount of water.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: The drain hose runs from the back of the washer to the standpipe. Make sure it's not kinked, and the hose height is between 28–47 inches from the floor.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Pour water directly into the standpipe. If it backs up, the blockage is in your plumbing.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, run a drain cycle and listen. If you hear nothing (no motor sound), the pump has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the pump filter or hose blockage, run a Spin Only cycle. The OE code clears once a successful drain cycle completes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump filter is clean and hose is clear but no draining occurs and the pump is silent, the pump motor needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Most LG front-load and top-load washers across recent model generations, including front-loaders like the WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, and WM8100HVA and top-loaders such as the WT7300CW, WT7900HBA, and WM3400CW.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report OE appearing mid-cycle after heavier loads or when the pump filter hasn't been cleaned in a while, and clearing that filter often resolves it quickly. A kinked drain hose or a drain hose pushed too far down the standpipe can also falsely trigger the code.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — cannot complete spin cycle
error_codes.detail: UE fires when the drum's balance-sensing system detects uneven weight distribution as spin speed ramps up. The control monitors motor load and drum wobble; if imbalance exceeds a safe threshold, it halts acceleration, adds water, and re-tumbles to reposition items. UE appears only after several failed redistribution attempts. It differs from mechanical fault codes because it signals a load-distribution issue rather than a motor, drainage, or sensor failure — though chronic UE can point to worn suspension components.

causes.description: Heavy items clumped to one side of the drum
Single large item (comforter, towels) that absorbs water unevenly
Worn suspension springs or shock absorbers

fixes.title: Manually redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the door and move items around so weight is evenly distributed around the drum.

fixes.title: Adjust load size
fixes.description: For single heavy items like rugs or comforters, add a few towels to counterbalance.

fixes.title: Check the drum suspension
fixes.description: Grab the drum through the door and shake it gently. On a healthy machine, the drum should resist and spring back. Floppy movement means worn shock absorbers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, redistribute laundry, then press Start/Pause to resume. The error clears automatically after successful rebalancing.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs consistently with normal loads, the shock absorbers or spider arm (drum bearing) may need replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Both front-load models (e.g. WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, WM8100HVA) and top-load models (e.g. WT7300CW, WT7900HBA); the UE code appears across LG's washer lineup, though top-load and front-load balancing behavior differs slightly.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report UE with bulky single items like comforters, bath mats, or a lone pair of jeans that clump to one side. Redistributing the load or adding a few towels to balance usually clears it right away; if it keeps happening with normal, well-spread loads, worn shocks are often the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — water not filling within expected time
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the flow meter and pressure sensor during the fill cycle. When it doesn't register enough water reaching the target level within the allotted fill time, it throws IE (displayed as 1E on some panels). This flags a supply-side or valve problem rather than drainage. It differs from FE, which fires when the tub overfills — IE means too little water, FE means too much, so the two point to opposite failures.

causes.description: Supply valve partially or completely closed
Inlet hose screens clogged with sediment

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Make sure both hot and cold valves behind the washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Unscrew the inlet hoses from the back of the washer. Inside each port are small mesh screens — clean them under running water.

fixes.title: Test home water pressure
fixes.description: Run a nearby faucet while the washer tries to fill. If pressure is low throughout the home, the issue is the supply.

fixes.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixes.description: Using a multimeter, test each solenoid coil for continuity (should read 200–500 ohms). Replace if an open circuit is found.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the supply issue, the error clears automatically on the next fill attempt. Press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If valves and screens are fine but the error persists, the water inlet valve needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load and top-load washers across most modern series, including models such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WM8100HVA, WM4500HBA, and WT7900HBA
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report IE right after moving the machine or reconnecting hoses, often because a supply valve wasn't fully reopened or a hose got kinked. A frequent quick win is simply opening both taps fully and clearing sediment from the inlet screens; the code usually clears on the next fill attempt.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked — hall sensor or stator fault
error_codes.detail: LE means the motor control board commanded the drum to turn but the hall sensor reported little or no rotor movement, or motor current climbed abnormally high — the board reads this as a locked or stalled motor. It differs from an unbalanced-load or drain error because it points at the motor circuit itself: the rotor position sensor, stator windings, or the control board driving them, rather than water level, balance, or door state.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: Remove half the laundry and restart. LE from overloading is common.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. This resets the motor control firmware.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small sensor near the rotor. It's inexpensive and often the sole cause of LE. Remove the back panel, locate the sensor near the motor, and swap it.
fixes.warning: Unplug washer and lay it on its front to access the motor.

fixes.title: Inspect the rotor and stator
fixes.description: Check for damage to the rotor magnets and stator windings. Replace damaged components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, then restart with a reduced load. After motor sensor replacement, the code clears on the first successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve LE, the stator or main control board needs professional evaluation.
error_codes.models_affected: LG direct-drive washers, both front-load and top-load models (for example WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WM4500HBA, WT7900HBA)
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report LE appearing mid-cycle, especially with heavy or tangled loads like towels or bedding, and it often clears after removing part of the load and restarting. When it returns on normal or light loads, the hall sensor near the rotor is a frequently cited quick, inexpensive fix.

error_codes.meaning: Door open or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the door interlock switch to confirm the door is physically closed and the lock solenoid has engaged before allowing the drum to spin. dE fires when that confirmation is missing—either the latch never seated or the lock circuit never reported locked. dE1 and dE2 narrow the fault to specific lock states, distinguishing a door reported open from a lock that failed to actuate or unlock, helping separate a simple latching issue from a solenoid or wiring failure.

causes.description: Laundry or fabric caught in the door seal

fixes.title: Check for laundry in the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the door gasket and check for small items caught between the gasket and drum.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door until it clicks definitively.

fixes.title: Inspect the door catch
fixes.description: Look at the door latch hook and the catch bracket. If the plastic is cracked or bent, replace the assembly.

fixes.title: Test the door lock
fixes.description: Use a multimeter to test the door lock switch for continuity when engaged.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure the door is firmly latched and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but the error shows, the lock solenoid assembly needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load washers with an electronic door interlock (dE/dE1/dE2); the dE1/dE2 variants appear on newer front-loaders such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, and WM8100HVA. Top-load models like WT7300CW use a lid-lock scheme and typically report door faults differently.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code right after loading, often traced to a sock or small garment wedged in the gasket that keeps the door from seating fully. Many say a firm push until it clicks clears it; when the door clicks but the code persists, the latch or lock assembly is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Tub clean reminder — drum needs cleaning
error_codes.detail: tCL is a periodic maintenance prompt, not a fault code. The control board tracks completed wash cycles and displays tCL after roughly 30 cycles to recommend running the Tub Clean routine, which flushes residual detergent, hard-water scale, and biofilm that cause odor and mildew. Unlike genuine error codes (such as drain or motor faults), tCL does not interrupt washing and clears once a full Tub Clean cycle finishes. It reflects usage counting only, not any sensed mechanical or electrical problem.

causes.description: Drum clean cycle due after ~30 washes
Reminder not cleared after running Tub Clean

fixes.title: Run the Tub Clean cycle
fixes.description: Select 'Tub Clean' on the cycle selector. Add a washing machine cleaner tablet or 1 cup of bleach to the empty drum (not the dispenser) and run the full cycle.
parts: Affresh or LG washing machine cleaner

fixes.title: Clear the reminder
fixes.description: The tCL indicator clears automatically once a Tub Clean cycle completes. If it lingers, press and hold the Tub Clean button for 3 seconds or power cycle the washer.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a complete Tub Clean cycle. The tCL counter resets automatically after the cycle finishes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Tub Clean is a maintenance reminder, not a fault. No professional service needed.
error_codes.models_affected: Most LG front-load and top-load washers with a dedicated Tub Clean cycle, including models such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WM8100HVA, WM4500HBA, and WT7900HBA.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tCL appearing on the display or as a blinking indicator after a stretch of frequent loads, and it can seem alarming despite being harmless. A common quick win is running the empty Tub Clean cycle with a cleaner tablet or bleach; if the reminder lingers afterward, owners often find a power cycle or holding the Tub Clean button clears it.

error_codes.meaning: Water overflow error — drum overfilling
error_codes.detail: The FE (Flow/overFill Error) code fires when the pressure sensor reports a water level above the maximum safe threshold, meaning the tub is overfilling. The control board reacts by draining immediately to prevent leaks. It differs from IE (inlet/no-fill), which signals too little water arriving. FE typically points to an inlet valve stuck open letting water in continuously, or a pressure sensor misreading level due to a kinked or disconnected pressure hose.

causes.description: Failed water inlet valve stuck open
Pressure hose to sensor kinked or disconnected

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose connects the tub to the pressure sensor. Make sure it is not kinked, blocked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose from the sensor and gently blow into the hose port — you should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: A stuck-open inlet valve continuously allows water in. Test by turning off the water supply when the error occurs. If water stops, the valve needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off water supply and unplug the washer. Drain remaining water through the pump filter. After repair, restart with water supply restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the inlet valve is replaced and overflow continues, the pressure sensor needs replacement or the control board has a fault.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load and top-load washers across the current lineup, including models such as the WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WT7900HBA, and WM8100HVA
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report FE appearing right after the tub fills, often with water actively draining. A quick win some report is checking whether the inlet valve keeps admitting water after the cycle stops; shutting off the supply taps can confirm a stuck valve. A pinched or clogged pressure hose is another frequent, easily overlooked culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Heater error — water heater circuit fault
error_codes.detail: HE fires when the control board monitors the heating circuit and finds no expected response: the element draws no current (open circuit) or the NTC thermistor reports no temperature rise within the allotted time. The board reads this as a failed heat request and disables steam/sanitize functions while letting the wash continue unheated. This distinguishes HE from tE, which flags an out-of-range or shorted thermistor reading rather than an absent heating response.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. A transient HE from a one-time heating glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access the element at the base of the tub and check continuity — an open element reads OL.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the element.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Check the NTC sensor resistance — should read roughly 10k ohms at room temperature.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart. If HE returns when a heated cycle begins, the element or thermistor needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load washers with an internal heater for steam and sanitary cycles, including models such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, and WM8100HVA
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and thermistor both test good but HE persists, the control board heater relay has failed.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report HE appearing only when a steam or sanitary cycle begins, while normal cold or warm washes finish fine. A one-time glitch sometimes clears after a power cycle, but if it returns each time a heated cycle starts, the element or thermistor usually needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — washer cannot distribute the load for spin
error_codes.detail: UB is a load-distribution warning, not a hardware fault. During the pre-spin ramp, the control board monitors drum motion and motor current for wobble that signals uneven weight. If the drum won't settle within limits, it adds water and re-tumbles to redistribute, retrying several times before halting with UB. Unlike LE, which flags a motor or rotor-lock/overcurrent fault, UB simply means the machine refused to accelerate an off-balance load to protect itself.

causes.description: Single heavy/bulky item bunched on one side

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the lid/door, separate tangled items, and add a couple of towels to balance a single bulky item.

fixes.title: Level the washer
fixes.description: Check with a level front-to-back and side-to-side; adjust the leveling feet so the unit doesn't rock.

fixes.title: Run a smaller, mixed load
fixes.description: Loading to about 2/3 capacity with mixed item sizes lets the drum balance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, redistribute the laundry, close, and restart a Spin cycle. UB clears once the load balances.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UB occurs on every load even when balanced and level, the suspension dampers or rotor position sensor may need professional inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load and top-load washers across the range, including models such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WM8100HVA, WM4500HBA, and WT7900HBA
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report UB after washing a single bulky item like a comforter, bath mat, or hoodie, or when a very small load rides to one side. Adding a couple of towels to balance the drum and confirming the machine sits level usually clears it on the next spin.

error_codes.meaning: Excess suds detected — too much foam in the tub
error_codes.detail: During drain and spin, the control monitors foam through pressure and motor feedback. When excess suds prevent the tub from draining or reaching spin speed, the board displays SUD (or SD) and injects extra rinses plus a foam-reduction routine to dissolve the foam before continuing. Unlike drain codes like OE, which flag a blocked pump or hose, or IE fill faults, SUD points to a detergent problem rather than a mechanical or sensor failure, and clears once foam subsides.

fixes.title: Let the cycle run
fixes.description: The washer will add rinses to clear the foam — this can take 30+ minutes. Don't interrupt it.

fixes.title: Use less HE detergent
fixes.description: Switch to HE detergent and use only 1–2 teaspoons for a normal load. Most people use far too much.

fixes.title: Run a Tub Clean / rinse cycle
fixes.description: Run an empty Tub Clean cycle to flush residual detergent from the system.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wait for the suds-reduction routine to finish, then restart. Reduce detergent next load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is almost never a hardware fault. Call a pro only if it appears with correct HE detergent and a clear drain.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load and high-efficiency top-load LG washers, including models such as WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, WM8100HVA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, and WT7900HBA
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SUD appearing mid-cycle after switching detergents or overfilling the dispenser, which stretches wash time considerably. Non-HE detergent, pods, and homes with soft water seem to trigger it most often; cutting detergent to a teaspoon or two and running an empty rinse usually resolves it.

error_codes.meaning: Cool Down — washer-dryer combo lowering tub temperature (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: On LG washer-dryer combo units, Cd is a status message, not an error. After the drying phase the unit runs a cool-down to lower the temperature inside the tub before unlocking the door. It clears on its own.

causes.description: Normal cool-down at end of drying cycle

fixes.title: Wait for cool-down to finish
