Heating element relay stuck on control board

fixes.title: Power off immediately
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker. Allow oven to cool for 1 full hour.
fixes.warning: DO NOT open the oven door during overheating. Stand clear of the appliance.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor
fixes.description: After cooling, test sensor (should be ~1,080 ohms). If sensor is OK, the issue is a stuck relay.

fixes.title: Inspect heating elements
fixes.description: Check both bake and broil elements for visible signs of arcing or burn-through.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: If element failed, replace it. If relay is stuck on control board, board replacement is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Allow full cool-down before inspecting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F2 requires professional inspection. A stuck relay is a control board failure that must be repaired by a qualified technician.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric and gas ranges with electronic oven controls, including models such as LREL6325F and LRGL5825F
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F2 appearing during or shortly after a self-clean cycle, or when the oven keeps heating past its set point. A drifting or miscalibrated temperature sensor is a frequent false trigger, and some report the code clears after a full cool-down and breaker reset—though a stuck relay tends to bring it right back.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: F3 fires when the control board measures abnormally low resistance on the oven's RTD temperature probe circuit, near zero ohms instead of the roughly 1,080 ohms expected at room temperature. The board interprets this short as an impossibly high temperature reading and locks out heating to prevent overshoot. Unlike F2, which signals an open circuit or sensor out of range high, F3 specifically indicates a short—either inside the sensor element, a pinched harness grounding to the chassis, or a shorted board input.

causes.description: Pinched wiring causing short to chassis

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temp, LG sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring
fixes.description: Check sensor wiring from probe to connector for any pinch points.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: A shorted sensor cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, test with 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor shows correct resistance but F3 persists, control board sensor input is shorted.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric and gas ranges/ovens using an RTD temperature sensor, including models such as LREL6325F and LRGL5825F
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F3 appearing at startup or partway through a bake or self-clean cycle, sometimes alongside the oven refusing to heat. A pinched or melted wire near the rear sensor connector is a frequent false trigger that mimics a bad sensor, so many find that inspecting the harness before replacing the probe saves an unnecessary part.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure during self-clean cycle
error_codes.detail: F5 fires when the control board commands the motorized door latch to lock or unlock during a self-clean cycle but does not see the expected confirmation from the latch position switch within the allowed time. The board interprets the missing feedback as a failed lock operation and halts the cycle. This differs from related door codes in that it specifically involves the self-clean latch motor and its switch, rather than a general door-open sensing fault during normal baking.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door alignment
fixes.description: Door must close completely flush. Adjust door hinges if needed.

fixes.title: Test door latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch motor is at the back-top of the oven. Test the motor solenoid.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing.

fixes.title: Replace door latch assembly
fixes.description: If motor or switch failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F5 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch assembly and F5 persists, control board door lock circuit failed.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric and gas ranges with a motorized self-clean door lock, including models such as LREL6325F and LRGL5825F
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F5 appearing right when a self-clean cycle starts or ends, sometimes leaving the door stuck locked until a power cycle clears it. A door that isn't sitting fully flush can occasionally trigger it falsely, and many find that a five-minute breaker reset plus checking door alignment resolves a one-off occurrence.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock switch open — door latch not detecting locked position
error_codes.detail: The door latch is physically in the locked position but the switch is not confirming this to the control board.

causes.description: Door latch switch contacts worn or open

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch mechanism
fixes.description: Check for physical obstructions or misalignment.

fixes.title: Replace door latch/lock assembly
fixes.description: If switch contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F6 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new latch assembly and F6 persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure — internal fault
error_codes.detail: F9 fires when the electronic range control's self-diagnostics detect an internal fault it cannot recover from — a failed onboard component, a stuck relay, or corrupted control logic — often traceable to a prior power surge or simple aging. Unlike sensor-specific codes (F1/F3 point to the oven temperature sensor circuit), F9 flags the board itself rather than an external part, so the oven may stop heating, ignore keypad inputs, or shut down mid-cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle the range
fixes.description: Turn the oven breaker off for 15 minutes to reset the ERC, then restore power and test bake briefly.

fixes.title: Reseat control harnesses
fixes.description: With the breaker off, access the control area and reseat the ribbon and multi-pin harnesses at the ERC.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F9 returns after reset and reseating, replace the ERC/control board matched to the exact model.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn the circuit breaker off for 15 minutes, then restore power and run a short bake test; if F9 returns after a full reset and the harnesses are seated, the ERC/control board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician for recurring F9, because control board replacement involves high-voltage oven wiring, exact part matching, and verification that no stuck relay or harness fault will damage the new board.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric and gas ranges with an electronic range control (ERC), including models such as LREL6325F and LRGL5825F
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F9 appearing suddenly after a storm or power flicker, sometimes as a one-time glitch that clears with a breaker reset. When it keeps returning after a full reset and reseated harnesses, most find the control board itself has failed and needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor error — over-temperature detected
error_codes.detail: The control board continuously reads a thermistor mounted in the cavity or near the magnetron. F-3 fires when that sensor reports a temperature above the safe threshold, or when its resistance falls outside the expected range (open or shorted), which the board interprets as an over-temperature or failed-sensor condition. This differs from magnetron or high-voltage faults because the trigger is the thermal reading itself, not power delivery, and it often follows restricted airflow rather than a component burnout.

fixes.title: Check ventilation
fixes.description: Ensure the microwave has adequate clearance on all sides. Clean the grille and vent areas.

fixes.title: Allow to cool
fixes.description: Unplug for 20 minutes.

fixes.title: Test and replace sensor
fixes.description: If error returns after cooling, sensor has likely failed.
fixes.warning: DANGER: Microwave capacitors hold lethal charge. Only professionals should open microwave cases.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 20 minutes and ensure ventilation is clear. If F-3 returns, call a technician.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: ALWAYS call a professional for internal microwave repairs. Microwave capacitors retain lethal charge even when unplugged.
error_codes.models_affected: LG countertop and over-the-range microwaves that use a cavity thermistor for temperature monitoring
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F-3 appearing after longer cooking sessions or on over-the-range units where grease-clogged vents trap heat. A frequent quick win is simply clearing obstructions and letting the unit cool while unplugged; if the code returns immediately even when cold, the thermistor itself is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad/touchpad failure
error_codes.detail: F-6 fires when the control board's key-input scan detects an invalid or continuous signal from the touchpad matrix — typically a key reading as pressed when no one is touching it. The board interprets a stuck or shorted contact as a held button and locks out normal command entry. This distinguishes it from a dead display or relay fault: the panel usually still lights, but keys respond erratically or not at all. Membrane wear, grease, and steam bridging the contacts are the usual culprits.

fixes.title: Clean and dry the touchpad
fixes.description: Wipe the keypad with a barely-damp cloth, then dry it completely so moisture cannot bridge the membrane contacts.

fixes.title: Power cycle the microwave
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes to reset the control board, then plug back in and test every key one at a time.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad
fixes.description: If F-6 persists after cleaning and reset, replace the keypad/touchpad assembly for the exact model.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes after cleaning and drying the keypad, then restore power and test all buttons; if F-6 returns, the membrane has failed and the touchpad must be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if a new touchpad does not clear F-6, because the control board's key-input circuit is then suspect and opening a microwave cabinet exposes high-voltage capacitors that can remain dangerous.
error_codes.models_affected: LG microwaves with membrane touchpad controls, including countertop, over-the-range, and built-in models
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F-6 appearing after cooking steamy or greasy foods, or when a button feels sticky. A thorough cleaning and drying followed by a two-minute power cycle often clears it temporarily; recurring codes usually point to a worn membrane needing replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted key on control panel
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected a short circuit on one of the touch keys, reading it as permanently pressed. The microwave will refuse input or behave erratically until the short clears. Moisture or grease that has worked under the membrane keypad is the most common cause, followed by membrane wear.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace control panel/touchpad
fixes.description: F-11 indicates membrane failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 2 minutes. Replace touchpad if F-11 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F-11 persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water fill detected — water inlet problem
error_codes.detail: The control board times how quickly the tub reaches its target water level using the pressure sensor and, on many models, a flow meter on the inlet. If the expected volume does not arrive within a set window, F8E1 (displayed as FH on some panels) is set. It points to inadequate incoming water rather than a drain or overflow problem, which distinguishes it from F8E2 and from SUD, a suds-detection fault.

causes.description: Inlet hose screen clogged with sediment or debris

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Ensure both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Disconnect the inlet hoses at the back of the washer. Inside each port are mesh screens — remove and clean them.

fixes.title: Check water pressure
fixes.description: A minimum of 20 PSI is required. Test by running a faucet while the washer fills.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Disconnect wires from the valve solenoids and test continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms. Replace if open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After resolving the water supply issue, press and hold the Start button for 3 seconds to clear and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good and inlet valve is new but error persists, the flow meter or pressure switch needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool front-load and high-efficiency top-load washers with electronic controls and flow monitoring; the code appears across the current lineup including models such as WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW5000DW, and WTW8127LC.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after a plumbing repair or a move, when a supply valve was left only partly open, or gradually as inlet screens collect sediment in hard-water areas. A quick win many describe is simply reopening both valves fully and rinsing the mesh screens, after which the fill returns to normal.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle paused, water not draining
error_codes.detail: F9E1 fires when the control board runs the drain pump but the pressure sensor still detects water in the tub after the allotted drain time elapses. The board interprets the failure to reach an empty state as a drainage fault rather than a fill or sensing problem. Unlike SUD, which flags excess suds slowing the drain, F9E1 signals an actual flow restriction — a clogged filter, kinked hose, blocked standpipe, or a pump that no longer moves water.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter behind the access panel on the lower front of the washer. Prepare towels and a shallow pan. Turn cap counterclockwise slowly — water will flow out. Remove and clean the filter.
fixes.warning: Have several towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the drain hose is not kinked and has a proper high loop. The hose should not be inserted more than 8 inches into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain isn't blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear but the washer doesn't drain, test the pump motor for continuity and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Drain & Spin cycle. The F9E1 code clears once draining completes successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump replacement doesn't resolve the issue, the main control board drain relay may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load models with a drain pump filter behind the lower access panel (such as the WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW), plus top-load washers that monitor drain time; the F9E1/DR indication is common across recent Whirlpool washer lines
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing at the end of a wash with standing water left in the drum, often after washing items that shed lint, coins, or small debris into the pump filter. Many say a quick filter clean-out resolves it; occasionally a partially kinked hose behind the machine is the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — latch cannot engage
error_codes.detail: F5E2 fires when the control board commands the door lock but never receives confirmation that the latch mechanically engaged. Unlike F5E1 (door not closed) or F5E3 (lock stuck locked), F5E2 specifically flags a failure to reach the locked state — the solenoid may pull but the hook fails to catch, or the switch never confirms lock. The washer refuses to start without a confirmed lock and aborts mid-cycle if confirmation is lost, protecting against water release with an unsecured door.

causes.description: Worn door latch hook or strike plate
Laundry caught between door and gasket
Wiring harness to door lock damaged

fixes.title: Check for items in door seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing or small items lodged in the door gasket preventing full closure.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Check the door latch (on the door) and the strike on the machine for wear or damage.

fixes.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixes.description: Remove the door lock assembly and test the solenoid for continuity.

fixes.title: Check wiring
fixes.description: Inspect wiring harness from control board to door lock for damage or loose connectors.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and restart. If error persists, the door lock assembly needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door lock assembly replacement is a straightforward repair, but if the problem is a wiring harness issue, professional diagnosis is recommended.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load washers with a door interlock, including models such as the WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, and WFW6620HW; top-load models with a locking lid may report the same family of F5E codes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing when a stray sock or fabric edge is pinched in the gasket, so a careful check around the seal often clears it. It may also show up intermittently as the latch hook wears, and some find the code returns until the latch or strike plate is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: F1E1 indicates the main control board has detected an internal fault during its self-diagnostic routine—typically a failed onboard component, a corrupted read of its own memory, or an error in core processing that prevents reliable operation. Unlike F1E2, which points to a specific control communication or memory fault, F1E1 flags a general primary-board failure. Power surges, degraded relays or capacitors, and moisture-related corrosion are common triggers. It differs from motor or drive faults like F6E1, which involve peripheral circuits rather than the board itself.

causes.description: Voltage spike or power surge damaging control board
Failed component on control board (capacitor, relay)
Moisture or corrosion on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10–15 minutes and restart. Some transient control board faults clear on power cycle.

fixes.title: Check for moisture
fixes.description: Inspect the control board area for signs of water intrusion or corrosion.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the error persists after power cycling, the main PCB needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire positions before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F1E1 returns immediately, the board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is straightforward but if error returns on new board, there may be a power supply issue causing the failure.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers using the F#E# diagnostic scheme, roughly 2010 onward; includes models such as WTW5000DW, WFW5620HW, WTW4816FW, WTW8127LC, WFW9620HC, WTW5057LW, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F1E1 appearing suddenly, often after a storm or power interruption, sometimes with the cycle freezing mid-wash. A significant number find that a 10–15 minute unplug clears a one-time transient fault; if it returns right away, most end up replacing the board.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected — too much detergent
error_codes.detail: During wash and spin, the control monitors motor load, drum behavior, and pressure/level feedback to infer foam. When excess suds cushion the drum and prevent proper agitation or spin ramp-up, the SUD (5D/Sd) code fires and the machine pauses to run extra rinses or dwell until foam subsides. Unlike a drain fault, water still evacuates; the issue is foam interfering with sensing and spin, not a blockage or pump failure.

causes.description: Too much detergent — using more than manufacturer's minimum recommended dose
Non-HE detergent used in HE front-load washer
Detergent residue buildup in dispenser and drum

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: The washer will continue automatically once suds subside. This may take up to 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Run Rinse+Spin
fixes.description: If cycle doesn't resume, run a Rinse+Spin only cycle to clear suds.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent going forward
fixes.description: Use only HE-labeled detergent. Use no more than 1 tablespoon per load. Less is more with HE washers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: SUD clears automatically. No manual reset needed. If it recurs, reduce detergent amount significantly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is not a mechanical fault. Persistent SUD with minimal detergent may indicate a water level sensor issue.
error_codes.models_affected: HE washers with suds sensing, both front-load and high-efficiency top-load models — including examples like WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW5000DW, and WTW8127LC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report SUD appearing late in a cycle right before or during spin, often after switching detergents or overfilling the cup. A frequent false trigger is using regular (non-HE) detergent or leftover residue in the drum; many say cutting the dose sharply and running an empty hot rinse clears it up.

error_codes.meaning: Dispenser cannot dispense detergent or additive
error_codes.detail: F0E2 fires when the control board expects detergent or an additive to be released but the dispenser system fails to complete the action. On automatic dispensers, the board drives an actuator motor or solenoid to route water through the correct compartment; if that mechanism stalls or the flow path is blocked, the fault sets. Unlike water-supply or drain codes, F0E2 points specifically at product delivery, and on many models the cycle continues even after the code appears.

causes.description: Dispenser clogged with hardened detergent or fabric softener

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser drawer
fixes.description: Remove the dispenser drawer completely and clean thoroughly with warm water, removing all hardened detergent and fabric softener buildup.

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser housing
fixes.description: Use a small brush to clean the compartment where the drawer sits, including the spray nozzles.

fixes.title: Inspect dispenser actuator
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve it, the dispenser actuator motor or solenoid may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, restart the cycle. Run a Rinse+Spin to confirm the dispenser is now functioning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the dispenser is clean but still doesn't function, the dispenser solenoid or motor needs professional testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load and select high-efficiency top-load washers with an automatic multi-compartment dispenser, roughly 2015 onward (for example WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW8127LC)
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after periods of using thick liquid detergent or fabric softener that hardens in the drawer and nozzles. A thorough drawer and housing cleaning often clears it, and false triggers sometimes trace to an overfilled or improperly seated dispenser drawer.

error_codes.meaning: Motor speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: F7E1 fires when the motor control module commands a target RPM but feedback from the motor's position sensor shows the drum isn't reaching that speed. The board compares expected versus actual rotation and flags the mismatch, most often during spin. This distinguishes it from door-lock or drain codes: the fault lies in the motor drive chain — the control module, stator windings, or position sensor — or simply too much load resisting acceleration, rather than a plumbing or latch issue.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: An overloaded drum prevents the motor from reaching speed. Remove half the laundry.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test motor and control board
fixes.description: If error persists, professional testing of motor control module is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, reduce load, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor and motor control board diagnosis requires professional testing if basic steps don't resolve the error.
error_codes.models_affected: High-efficiency top-load and front-load washers, primarily those using an electronically controlled variable-speed drive motor
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing at the start of the spin cycle, often with clothes left soaking wet. A heavy or unbalanced load, such as bulky towels or bedding, is a frequent false trigger, and many find that removing half the laundry and running a power cycle clears it before any parts are needed.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet water supply problem — excessive water or suds overflow
error_codes.detail: F0E1 fires when the control board reads a water level or pressure that exceeds the expected fill target, interpreting it as an overflow or excessive-suds condition. The pressure sensor reports too much water, which can come from real overfill (inlet valve leaking through) or from foam padding the sensor tube. Unlike a straight SUD code that flags suds during a cycle, F0E1 ties the over-water reading to the fill/supply logic, prompting the machine to stop before overfilling.

causes.description: Water inlet valve stuck open allowing overfill

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only, max 1 tablespoon. This is the cause in over half of F0E1 cases.

fixes.title: Check inlet valve
fixes.description: If no suds, test whether the inlet valve stays closed when not commanded.

fixes.title: Test pressure sensor
fixes.description: If water level seems fine, the sensor may be misreading.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the cause, run Rinse+Spin to clear suds, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If overfill continues after inlet valve replacement, pressure sensor needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool front-load and high-efficiency top-load washers that use a pressure sensor and electronic water-level control, including models such as WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW8127LC, and WTW5057LW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this after using too much or non-HE detergent, and simply cutting back the dose plus running an extra rinse often clears it. It can also appear falsely when suds fool the sensor, so many find a Rinse+Spin resets things without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad disconnected or key panel failure
error_codes.detail: F2E1 fires when the main control board detects that a keypad button appears continuously pressed or when it cannot establish valid communication with the user interface panel. The board flags a stuck key or lost UI signal and blocks cycle start to avoid unintended operation. It differs from F6E1, which points to a general communication bus fault, and from F1E1, which indicates a main control board fault rather than the keypad link itself.

causes.description: Wire harness between UI and main board loose

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check wire harness connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector between the UI panel and main board.

fixes.title: Replace UI board
fixes.description: If connector is secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F2E1 returns, check wiring connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't fix F2E1, main board communication port has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with electronic touch/keypad interfaces, including models such as WTW5000DW, WFW5620HW, WTW8127LC, WFW9620HC, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing right after a power flicker or when trying to start a cycle, with buttons feeling unresponsive. A simple 10-minute unplug often clears a transient stuck-key reading, and many find that reseating the ribbon or harness connector behind the console resolves it without new parts.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor failure — water level sensing fault
error_codes.detail: F3E1 indicates the control board is receiving an out-of-range or absent signal from the water-level pressure sensor, so it cannot confirm fill level. The sensor reads air pressure in a hose connected to the tub's pressure dome; a kinked, blocked, or leaking hose skews the reading, while an internal sensor fault sends invalid data. Unlike F8E1 (a water-inlet or fill-time problem) or F0E1 (load/basket sensing), F3E1 points specifically at the level-sensing circuit itself.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Inspect hose from tub to sensor for kinks or blockages.

fixes.title: Blow-test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect hose, gently blow into port — should hear click.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor fails blow test.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and clear hose don't resolve F3E1, control board pressure circuit failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top- and front-load washers using an electronic pressure (analog) level sensor, roughly 2015 onward — including models such as WTW5000DW, WFW5620HW, WTW8127LC, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing at the start of a cycle or mid-fill, sometimes after moving the machine, which can pinch the pressure hose. A frequent quick win is reseating or clearing that thin hose before assuming the sensor itself has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between main control board and motor control board
error_codes.detail: The main control board continuously exchanges serial data with the motor control module. F6E1 (and the F6E2/F6E3 variants) fires when the main board sends a command but receives no valid reply within its timeout window, so it halts spin and agitate. This is a communication-link fault, distinct from motor-drive faults like F7E1 that report an actual motor speed or sensing failure. Most often the harness, connector, or one board's comm port is the break in the link.

causes.description: Wire harness between main and motor boards loose or damaged
Main control board communication port failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Check motor board wire harness
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat the harness connecting main board to motor control board.

fixes.title: Replace motor control board
fixes.description: Usually cheaper to replace than main board.

fixes.title: Replace main control board
fixes.description: If motor board is new but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F6E1 returns, check all board connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-to-board communication failures can affect either board. Test motor board first.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool washers using paired main and motor control boards, including many recent front-load and high-efficiency top-load models such as the WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW8127LC, and WTW5057LW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle when the drum stops spinning, sometimes after the machine has been moved or vibrated loose a connector. A power cycle and reseating the board-to-board harness resolves many cases; persistent returns usually point to a failing motor control board.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure switch failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: F3E2 fires when the control board reads the pressure switch (water-level sensor) circuit as electrically open — no continuity in any position. Unlike F3E1, which flags an out-of-range or shorted analog signal, F3E2 means the board sees no valid connection at all. Common triggers are burned-out switch contacts, a loose or corroded connector, or a split or disconnected pressure hose that leaves the sensor unable to register any air-pressure change during fill.

fixes.title: Inspect pressure hose
fixes.description: Check hose connection at both tub and switch.

fixes.title: Test switch
fixes.description: Should change continuity at different air pressure levels.

fixes.title: Replace pressure switch
fixes.description: If switch has open circuit in all positions.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new switch and clear hose don't resolve F3E2, control board pressure input failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers using an electronic analog pressure sensor (water-level sensor), broadly mid-2010s onward; documented on models across the WTW and WFW series.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing at the start of a cycle, before or during fill, sometimes with the machine refusing to add water. A loose connector or a kinked or slipped pressure hose is a frequent false trigger, so many find that reseating the plug or reconnecting the hose clears it without a new part.

error_codes.meaning: Flow meter error — water volume sensor fault
error_codes.detail: F8E3 fires when the control board expects pulses from the inline flow meter on the water inlet but the signal is missing, erratic, or out of range while filling. The meter's spinning impeller generates pulses the board counts to gauge exact water volume. Unlike F8E1 (no water detected/supply issue) and F8E2 (dispenser or detergent flow fault), F8E3 points specifically at the volume sensor itself — a jammed impeller, failed sensor coil, or broken wiring to it.

causes.description: Flow meter impeller stuck or broken

fixes.title: Check flow meter for scale or debris
fixes.description: The flow meter is inline on the cold water inlet. Scale buildup can jam the impeller.

fixes.title: Test flow meter
fixes.description: Disconnect and check continuity of the sensor coil.

fixes.title: Replace flow meter
fixes.description: If impeller is stuck or electrical test fails.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If flow meter replaced and error persists, control board flow meter input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool washers that use inline flow-meter water metering rather than a simple pressure switch — largely high-efficiency front-load and newer top-load models. From KNOWN_MODELS, series such as WFW5620HW, WFW6620HW, WFW9620HC, WTW8127LC and WTW5057LW are representative; older basic models may not use this code.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-fill or shortly after a cycle starts, sometimes after a period of hard-water use. A frequent quick win is clearing scale or debris that has jammed the impeller; some find checking the connector at the meter resolves an intermittent trigger.

error_codes.meaning: Control lock active — all buttons disabled
error_codes.detail: LOC (sometimes shown as LC) indicates the Control Lock, or child lock, is active. This is a user feature, not a fault. When enabled, the control board disables every button except Power to prevent accidental setting changes or a child starting a cycle. Because it is intentional, no error is logged and no cycle interruption occurs. It is distinct from keypad or user-interface fault codes, which point to a stuck button or failed control board rather than a deliberately engaged lock.

fixes.title: Disable control lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Control Lock button for 3 seconds. On some models, hold the End of Cycle button for 3 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your manual
fixes.description: If the above doesn't work, consult your model's manual for the specific button combination.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Control Lock button for 3 seconds to unlock.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: LOC is not a fault. No service required.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with an electronic control panel and a Control Lock feature; the code appears across most recent digital-display models.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report LOC appearing after cleaning the panel or when a button was held down by accident, and the washer simply won't respond to any press. The usual quick win is holding the Control Lock (or End of Cycle) button for about three seconds. If it returns immediately, a stuck or moisture-fouled key may be the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch failure — lid/door switch not actuating
error_codes.detail: F5E1 fires when the control board expects the door or lid switch to actuate at cycle start but never sees the closed-contact signal. The board watches the switch circuit to confirm the door is physically shut before allowing fill or spin. It differs from lock-related codes (F5E2/F5E3) that involve the motorized latch failing to engage or disengage; F5E1 points specifically to the switch itself not making contact, a broken strike tab, or damaged switch wiring.

causes.description: Door switch contacts worn or burned

fixes.title: Check door closure
fixes.description: Make sure the door is fully closed.

fixes.title: Inspect door strike
fixes.description: The strike tab on the door that presses the switch may be broken.

fixes.title: Test door switch
fixes.description: Test switch continuity with door open and closed.

fixes.title: Replace door switch/lock assembly
fixes.description: If switch contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed. Restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F5E1 on front-loads often means the door latch hook or catch needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool front-load and top-load washers with an electronic door/lid switch, including models such as WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW, WTW5000DW, WTW8127LC, WTW5057LW, and WTW4816FW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appears right at cycle start when the door seems closed but won't latch, and a firmer push or a quick door open-and-reclose often clears it. A cracked or bent strike tab on the door is a frequently reported quick win before replacing the switch assembly.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock not engaging — lock timeout
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the door lock solenoid to engage but never received confirmation that the lock completed within the allowed time, so the wash cannot start. A misaligned latch, a worn solenoid, or a broken wire to the lock is the usual root cause.

causes.description: Door lock solenoid worn or failed
Door latch or strike plate misaligned
Wiring or connector to the lock damaged

fixes.title: Check latch alignment
fixes.description: Open and firmly close the door — confirm the latch hook lines up with the strike and the door isn't sagging on its hinge.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 1 minute, then restore power; this clears a transient lock-timeout fault on the control.

fixes.title: Test the door lock solenoid
fixes.description: With the lock removed, apply power and confirm it engages, then test the solenoid coil for continuity and replace if open.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixes.description: Swap the lock if it fails to engage or the latch is cracked — a straightforward bolt-in part.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly so the latch fully seats, then unplug the washer for 1 minute and restore power. Press Start — F5E3 clears once the control confirms the door locks.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new door lock assembly is installed and the latch is correctly aligned but F5E3 keeps returning, the lock relay on the main control board has failed and the board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Motor control board failure
error_codes.detail: The motor control unit (MCU) — separate from the main control board on many Whirlpool front-loaders — detected an internal fault and can no longer drive the drum motor reliably. A failed power component, surge damage, or overheating from poor ventilation is the usual root cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 15 minutes to let the MCU reset, then restore power and start a short cycle to see if F1E2 clears.

fixes.title: Reseat the motor connectors
fixes.description: Unplug the washer and check the MCU's wiring connectors for looseness or corrosion, then reseat any that are loose.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Inspect for overheating
fixes.description: Confirm the washer has clearance for airflow and the MCU heatsink isn't caked in dust or lint.

fixes.title: Replace the motor control board
fixes.description: Swap the MCU, located behind the drum or in the lower cabinet, if the fault returns.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 15 minutes so the motor control board fully powers down, then restore power and run a Rinse & Spin. If F1E2 returns immediately, the board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new motor control board returns F1E2 immediately, the main control board may be sending incorrect commands or the motor itself is shorted — both need a technician to diagnose in service mode.

error_codes.meaning: No water detected entering the machine
error_codes.detail: F20 (displayed as FH on older Duet units) fires when the control board expects water in the tub but the pressure switch reports no rise in level within a set fill window. The board compares elapsed fill time against the pressure sensor's frequency signal; if the tub does not reach the target level, it aborts and flags F20. This distinguishes it from F21 (drain/pump problems) and F8E1 (long-fill or flow-metering faults), which involve draining or slow flow rather than an outright absence of incoming water.

causes.description: Hot or cold supply valve closed / partly open
Inlet hose screens clogged with sediment
Low household water pressure or kinked hose

fixes.title: Confirm both faucets are on
fixes.description: Both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer must be fully open — the washer blends both even on cold cycles.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screens
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill hoses, and clean the mesh screens inside the inlet ports.
fixes.warning: Shut off the supply valves before disconnecting hoses.

fixes.title: Check hoses and pressure
fixes.description: Verify hoses are not kinked and household pressure is at least 20 PSI.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Test the valve solenoids for continuity (about 200–500 ohms) and replace if open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press PAUSE/CANCEL twice, then restart. To clear, press Pause or Cancel twice and the Power button once.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and inlet valve are confirmed good and F20/FH persists, the pressure switch or main control board needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool front-load washers (the older Duet series shows FH; models such as WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, WFW6620HW) and some top-load models like WTW5000DW, WTW8127LC, WTW5057LW that monitor fill via a pressure switch
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F20 or FH appearing right at the start of a cycle, often after the water supply was shut off for plumbing work or after a move. A frequent false trigger is a supply valve that looks open but is only partly turned; many report the quick win is simply fully opening both hot and cold faucets and clearing sediment from the inlet screens.

error_codes.meaning: Long drain — water not draining in the expected time
error_codes.detail: F21 fires when the control board senses water remains in the tub after the drain pump has run for the expected interval, indicating flow is restricted rather than absent. The board times the drain phase and, when the pressure or level sensor still reads water at timeout, logs a long-drain fault. This differs from F20 (no water detected/fill issue) and F9E1, a closely related long-drain variant; F21 specifically points to restricted outflow through the filter, hose, or pump.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Open the lower-front access panel, place towels and a pan, and slowly remove the filter cap to drain water and clear debris.
fixes.warning: Water will spill — have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose is not kinked and is inserted no more than 4.5 inches into the standpipe with a proper high loop.

fixes.title: Run a clean cycle with HE detergent
fixes.description: If suds are the cause, run a rinse/drain and switch to HE detergent.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hose are clear, test the pump for continuity and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the blockage, then run a Drain & Spin. F21 clears once draining completes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump and hose are clear and F21 persists, the main control board drain relay may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load Whirlpool washers with a drain pump filter, notably models such as WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, and WFW6620HW; some top-load HE models can display equivalent long-drain faults.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F21 appearing mid-cycle with a tub full of standing water, often after washing bulky or linty items. A clogged coin trap or a wad of debris in the pump filter is the frequent quick win, and excessive suds from regular (non-HE) detergent can falsely trigger it.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — door did not lock
error_codes.detail: F22 fires when the control board commands the door lock but never receives confirmation that the door latched, typically after about six failed attempts. The board monitors the lock's switch feedback; without a locked signal it blocks the cycle for safety, since the drum cannot spin or fill with an unsecured door. This differs from F5E1/F5E2, which point to the lock staying latched or unlatching unexpectedly rather than failing to engage in the first place.

causes.description: Door not fully closed / item caught in seal
Worn or failed door lock assembly

fixes.title: Confirm the door is shut
fixes.description: Close the door firmly and check for laundry caught between the door and gasket.

fixes.title: Inspect the door strike and latch
fixes.description: Check the strike on the door and the lock mechanism on the frame for wear or damage.

fixes.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixes.description: Remove and test the lock solenoid/switches for continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before accessing the door lock.

fixes.title: Check the wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness and connectors from the control board to the door lock.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure the door is fully closed and restart. If F22 persists, the door lock assembly needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door lock assembly is new and the wiring is sound, the main control board lock circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load washers with an electronic door lock assembly, such as the WFW5620HW, WFW9620HC, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appears when laundry or a bit of the gasket gets pinched at the door edge, preventing a full latch. Many find that firmly reclosing the door or clearing an obstruction resolves it quickly, though a persistent code often points to a worn latch or loose connector.

error_codes.meaning: Hot and cold water hoses reversed
error_codes.detail: The control board reads incoming water temperatures at the hot and cold inlet valves during fill and compares them against expected values. When the sensed hot inlet runs cold and the cold inlet runs hot, the board flags HC at cycle end. It signals a swapped plumbing connection rather than a valve or sensor failure, which distinguishes it from fill or drain faults like F20 that indicate no water reaching the tub at all.

causes.description: Hot and cold fill hoses connected to the wrong inlets
Hot and cold house plumbing reversed at the faucets

fixes.title: Swap the fill hoses
fixes.description: Shut off both supply valves and reconnect the hot hose to the hot (typically left) inlet and the cold hose to the cold inlet.
fixes.warning: Shut off the supply valves before disconnecting hoses.

fixes.title: Verify faucet temperatures
fixes.description: If the hoses are correct, run each faucet to confirm the house hot/cold lines are not themselves reversed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Correct the hose connections, then run a cycle. HC clears once the inlet temperatures match.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hoses and house plumbing are confirmed correct and HC persists, the inlet temperature sensing on the control board needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load washers with internal temperature sensing, commonly newer high-efficiency models such as the WTW5000DW, WTW4816FW, WTW5057LW, WTW8127LC and comparable series
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report HC right after installing a new washer or moving it, when hoses get crossed at the inlets. A frequent false trigger is house plumbing itself being reversed at the taps, so checking faucet temperatures often resolves cases where the hoses look correct.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — a power interruption stopped the cycle
error_codes.detail: PF is logged when the control board detects that main power was interrupted mid-cycle rather than a normal shutdown. On the next power-up the board recognizes the cycle did not complete and flags PF so the load can be resumed. It reflects loss of input voltage, not a defective motor, valve, or sensor, which distinguishes it from component-specific fault codes. A momentary brown-out, an unseated cord, or a weak breaker are enough to trigger it.

causes.description: Loose power cord or outlet connection

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press START to resume, or press POWER/PAUSE-CANCEL to clear the display.

fixes.title: Check the outlet and cord
fixes.description: Confirm the cord is fully seated and the outlet is sound.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting the cord.

fixes.title: Reset and monitor the breaker
fixes.description: Reset the circuit breaker and watch for one minute to confirm PF does not return.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press START to resume, or POWER to clear. Reset the breaker if power was lost.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PF returns repeatedly with stable house power, the cord, outlet, or control board power input needs inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with digital control displays, including models such as WTW5000DW, WFW5620HW, WTW8127LC, WFW9620HC, WTW5057LW, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PF appearing after a storm, brief outage, or when several appliances share a circuit, and it usually clears with a simple restart. If it keeps returning while house power seems steady, checking that the cord is fully seated in the outlet is a frequent quick win.

error_codes.meaning: User interface communication / keypad fault
error_codes.detail: F2E2 fires when the main control board fails to establish or maintain valid serial communication with the user-interface (keypad) board, or when the control detects a button reading as continuously pressed. The distinguishing factor from F1E1/F1E2 main-control faults is that the display and keypad specifically stop responding while the machine otherwise powers up. It points at the UI board, its ribbon/connector, or a physically stuck membrane key rather than the main control's own memory or supply.

causes.description: Loss of communication between main control and UI board

fixes.title: Power cycle the control
fixes.description: Unplug the washer (or turn off the breaker) for one minute, then restore power.

fixes.title: Reseat the UI wiring
fixes.description: Open the console and reseat the ribbon/connectors between the main board and the user interface.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Inspect the keypad
fixes.description: Check for a physically stuck button and clean around the keys.

fixes.title: Replace the user interface
fixes.description: If a key is stuck or communication does not restore, replace the UI board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cycle power for one minute. If F2E2 returns, reseat the UI wiring, then replace the user interface.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If wiring is seated and no key is stuck, the user interface board or main control board needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with a separate electronic user-interface board, roughly 2010 onward (for example WTW5000DW, WTW4816FW, WTW8127LC, WTW5057LW, WFW5620HW, WFW6620HW, WFW9620HC)
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report the buttons or display going dead mid-cycle or at startup, and a one-minute power cycle often clears it temporarily. A key held down by spilled detergent, a cleaning cloth, or a leaning laundry basket is a frequent false trigger, so check for stuck buttons before assuming a board failure.

error_codes.meaning: Off-balance load detected
error_codes.detail: F0E5 fires when the control board, reading feedback from the drive motor and balance sensor during the spin ramp-up, sees that the basket cannot accelerate smoothly because weight is distributed unevenly. The washer halts or slows the spin to avoid excessive drum wobble and drive-system stress. Unlike F0E2, which flags oversudsing from too much detergent, F0E5 is purely a mechanical distribution problem — the load itself, load size, leveling, or worn suspension components are what the sensor reacts to.

causes.description: Load bunched to one side or tangled
Load too large or too small

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the lid, separate tangled items, and spread the load evenly around the basket.

fixes.title: Right-size the load
fixes.description: Avoid washing a single bulky item alone or overstuffing the basket.

fixes.title: Level the washer
fixes.description: Confirm all four feet are firm on the floor and the cabinet does not rock.

fixes.title: Inspect the suspension
fixes.description: If balancing does not help, check the suspension rods/shock absorbers for wear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute the load, close the lid, and press START to resume the spin.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the load is balanced and the washer is level but F0E5 persists, the suspension or the off-balance sensor needs inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with electronic off-balance sensing, including models such as WTW5000DW, WTW4816FW, WTW8127LC, WTW5057LW, WFW5620HW, WFW6620HW, and WFW9620HC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing mid-cycle when washing a single heavy item like a comforter or a small, light load that flings to one side. Redistributing the items and confirming the machine sits level usually clears it; persistent codes on a balanced, level washer often point to tired suspension rods.

error_codes.meaning: Long fill — washer taking too long to fill
error_codes.detail: Per Whirlpool, LF (also shown as F8E1 or Lo FL) means the washer is not detecting the correct amount of incoming water within the allowed time, so the fill is taking too long. A closed or low-pressure supply, kinked or clogged inlet hoses, or a weak inlet valve are the usual causes. Both hot and cold valves must be open for the washer to fill correctly.

causes.description: Supply valves not fully open / low water pressure
Inlet hoses kinked or screens clogged with debris
Water inlet valve weak or failing

fixes.title: Check the water supply
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold supply valves are fully open and household pressure is adequate.

fixes.title: Inspect the fill hoses and screens
fixes.description: Make sure the inlet hoses are not kinked, and clean the inlet screens of mineral build-up or debris.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water before disconnecting hoses.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply and hoses are good but fill stays slow, replace the inlet valve.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Pause/Cancel twice and Power once to clear. Confirm both valves are open and hoses are clear, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the supply, hoses, and inlet valve all check out and LF persists, the flow meter or control board needs diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers with electronic fill control, including models such as WTW5000DW, WFW5620HW, WTW8127LC, WFW9620HC, WTW5057LW, and WFW6620HW; the code also appears as F8E1 or Lo FL on many recent Whirlpool washers.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report LF appearing early in a cycle right after installation or after the water was shut off for plumbing work, often because a supply valve was left partly closed or a hose stayed kinked behind the machine. A frequent quick win is simply opening both hot and cold valves fully and straightening the hoses; clearing sediment-clogged inlet screens also resolves many cases without new parts.

error_codes.meaning: Items detected during Clean Washer cycle — remove load
error_codes.detail: The rL code (also shown as F-34) fires when the control's load-detection routine senses mass or movement in the drum after the Clean Washer cycle begins. Because this maintenance cycle is designed to run empty with cleaner only, any detected item halts the cycle and prompts removal. Unlike imbalance or motor faults, rL is purely informational and reflects the sensor reading during startup, not a failed component. Occasionally residue, suds, or a slight imbalance can cause a false read.

causes.description: Clothing or items left in the drum during the Clean Washer cycle
Load-detect sensor misreading a residue or imbalance

fixes.title: Remove all items
fixes.description: Take all clothing and items out of the drum, then restart the Clean Washer cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle the control
fixes.description: If the drum is empty but rL persists, unplug the washer for 5 minutes to reset the control.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Remove everything from the drum and restart the Clean Washer cycle. If rL persists with an empty drum, unplug for 5 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: rL is a user prompt, not a fault. Service is only needed if it persists with a genuinely empty drum after a reset.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool top-load and front-load washers equipped with a dedicated Clean Washer / affresh cycle, roughly 2010 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report rL when they forget to empty the drum or leave a single stray item before starting the Clean Washer cycle. It can occasionally appear on a truly empty drum, in which case a quick unplug-and-restart usually clears it.

error_codes.meaning: Detergent cartridge not detected — Load & Go dispenser
error_codes.detail: dET is a status message specific to the Load & Go bulk-dispensing system. During its detergent-detection routine the control checks whether a filled dispenser cartridge is present and properly seated. When it cannot confirm the cartridge — usually because it is missing, loosely seated, or the dispenser door has not cycled recently — it displays dET. Unlike a hard fault code, dET does not halt operation; the machine washes normally with detergent added manually while detection completes.

causes.description: Dispenser cartridge not installed or not seated
Dispenser door not opened/closed for several cycles

fixes.title: Seat the cartridge
fixes.description: Make sure the Load & Go cartridge is installed and fully seated, then open and close the dispenser door.

fixes.title: Run a few cycles
fixes.description: The detection can take up to three cycles of opening and closing the dispenser door to register.

fixes.title: Check the dispenser sensor
fixes.description: If dET persists with a seated cartridge, inspect the dispenser sensor and its wiring.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Seat the Load & Go cartridge, open and close the dispenser door, and run a cycle. dET clears once detection succeeds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: dET is informational. Service is only warranted if a seated cartridge is still not detected after several cycles.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool washers equipped with the Load & Go bulk-detergent dispenser, including certain top-load and front-load models such as WTW8127LC, WFW9620HC, and WFW6620HW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report dET during the first few loads after installing or refilling the Load & Go cartridge, and it often clears on its own once the dispenser door has been opened and closed over a couple of cycles. A frequent quick win is simply reseating the cartridge firmly and confirming the door latches.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: F01 indicates the main electronic control board has detected an internal fault it cannot recover from — typically a failed relay, blown capacitor, or corroded trace. Because the board governs every function, the dryer usually becomes unresponsive. It differs from motor or sensor codes in that the fault originates on the board itself rather than an external component. A power cycle can clear transient faults, but an immediately returning code points to genuine board failure often driven by surges or steam-related moisture.

fixes.title: Check for a tripped circuit breaker
fixes.description: Before replacing the board, confirm the dryer is getting full 240V power. A partially tripped breaker can cause board errors.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. Some transient board faults clear this way.

fixes.title: Inspect the control board visually
fixes.description: Access the board (usually behind the front or back panel). Look for burn marks, blown capacitors, or corrosion. If visible damage exists, replacement is needed.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: Remove the old board, photograph all wiring connections, and install new board in reverse order.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F01 reappears immediately, the board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 returns on a new board, there may be an underlying voltage problem (power surge, bad wiring) causing premature board failure.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electronic-control dryers, including models such as WED4815EW, WGD4815EW, WED5620HW, and WED6620HW, along with similar late-model top-load dryers using a main control board.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F01 appearing suddenly, sometimes after a storm or power flicker, with the dryer refusing to start. A 15-minute unplug occasionally clears it as a quick win, but if the code returns right away most find the board itself has failed and needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: F22 fires when the control board reads the outlet (exhaust) thermistor as out of its expected resistance range — typically an open or shorted circuit rather than a plausible temperature value. Because the board relies on this sensor to regulate the heating cycle, it halts or limits heat and flags the fault. This differs from F30/F31, which point to the inlet thermistor or its circuit; F22 isolates the fault to the exhaust-side sensor or its wiring.

fixes.title: Locate the thermistor
fixes.description: The outlet thermistor is typically located in the exhaust duct inside the dryer cabinet.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 10,000–12,000 ohms. Test with a multimeter.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect connector and remove sensor mounting screw. Install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug for 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and error persists, the control board thermistor input circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with an outlet thermistor, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F22 appearing shortly after startup or when a cycle won't heat properly, sometimes intermittently as the dryer warms. A loose or heat-degraded connector at the thermistor is a frequent false trigger, and reseating that plug before replacing the sensor is a quick win many find worthwhile.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — vent blockage
error_codes.detail: F30 fires when the control board reads a temperature-rise profile or thermistor response consistent with restricted exhaust airflow. During a heat cycle the board expects the drum to reach and shed heat at an expected rate; when air moves too slowly, temperatures climb abnormally and the board triggers a safety shutdown before the high-limit thermostat trips. Unlike sensor-fault codes tied to open or shorted thermistors (such as F31), F30 points to a physical airflow blockage rather than an electrical failure.

causes.description: Duct run too long or too many bends

fixes.title: Clean lint filter
fixes.description: Clean after every load.

fixes.title: Clean exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect and clean full duct length with a dryer vent brush.

fixes.title: Clear exterior vent
fixes.description: Check exterior vent for lint, debris, or animal nesting.

fixes.title: Upgrade to rigid duct
fixes.description: Replace flexible foil or plastic duct with rigid metal for any run over 3 feet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the duct, run a 20-minute Air Only cycle. The F30 code clears once adequate airflow is detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is completely clear and code persists, the airflow sensor or high-limit thermostat may need replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with an airflow/thermistor monitoring circuit, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after loads start taking much longer to dry or the cabinet feels unusually hot, and clearing the exhaust duct and lint screen often resolves it quickly. A partially crushed flexible duct behind the dryer or a lint-clogged exterior vent cap is a frequent false-alarm trigger even when the internal filter looks clean.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: F26 fires when the control board commands the drum drive motor but does not sense proper motor rotation or run feedback within the expected window. The board detects an open winding, failed start capacitor, or a tripped thermal overload interrupting the circuit. The drum stays still while the console may still light up, distinguishing F26 from a broken belt or a stuck door switch. Unlike thermistor faults (F01-type codes), F26 points squarely at the motor circuit rather than heating or sensing.

fixes.title: Test the drive motor
fixes.description: Access the motor (inside the drum cabinet). Test motor windings for continuity. No continuity = burned motor.

fixes.title: Check the thermal overload
fixes.description: Some motors have a thermal overload reset button. Press it and try restart.

fixes.title: Replace the drive motor
fixes.description: Remove the drum to access the motor. Disconnect wiring and drive belt. Install new motor.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart. Error clears once motor runs successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly. If not experienced with appliance repair, professional service is recommended.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers using the F-series fault codes, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report the code appearing at the start of a cycle when the drum refuses to turn, sometimes accompanied by a faint hum. A frequent quick win is pressing the motor's thermal overload reset button after letting the unit cool, which can clear a temporary trip; if it returns immediately the motor or capacitor is likely the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor bar failure — primary sensor
error_codes.detail: The primary moisture sensor bars in the drum have failed. Auto-dry cycles will not work correctly.

causes.description: Sensor bars coated with fabric softener residue
Sensor bar corrosion or physical damage

fixes.title: Clean sensor bars
fixes.description: The bars are two metal strips inside the front of the drum. Clean with rubbing alcohol.

fixes.title: Test sensor bars
fixes.description: Check continuity between bars — should vary with moisture.

fixes.title: Replace sensor bar assembly
fixes.description: If bars are physically damaged or corroded.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run Auto-Dry cycle to test.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If cleaning doesn't fix F28, sensor assembly replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor bar failure — secondary sensor
error_codes.detail: F29 fires when the control board reads the resistance across the secondary moisture sensor bars and gets an implausible or out-of-range value, so auto-dry cycles lose their reference for wetness and either shut off with damp loads or run excessively long. Fabric softener and dryer-sheet residue insulating the metal bars is the usual trigger. It differs from F28, which flags the primary sensor, and from F31, a broader sensor-circuit fault.

fixes.title: Clean sensor bars
fixes.description: Clean both sets of sensor bars with rubbing alcohol.

fixes.title: Replace sensor assembly
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run Auto-Dry cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Sensor bar cleaning resolves F29 in most cases.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with electronic auto-dry moisture sensing, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing after frequent use of dryer sheets or liquid softener, and note that loads finishing damp is often the first clue. A quick wipe of both bar sets with rubbing alcohol reportedly clears it in many cases before any part is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open or shorted circuit from the thermistor that measures exhaust air temperature, so it cannot regulate heat safely and stops the cycle. Most often the thermistor itself has drifted out of spec or failed; a loose connector is the next suspect.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat at thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board thermistor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low or missing voltage on one leg of 240V supply
error_codes.detail: L2 fires when the control board senses that one of the two 120V legs feeding the 240V supply is low or absent. The motor circuit still gets power from a single leg, so the drum tumbles, but the heating element needs both legs and stays cold. This distinguishes L2 from a genuine heater or thermostat failure: the board is reporting an incoming power problem, not an internal component fault, which is why the fix lies at the breaker, outlet, or panel.

causes.description: Loose wire connection in dryer outlet or at panel

fixes.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Go to the panel and find the double-pole breaker for the dryer. Toggle it fully OFF then back ON.

fixes.title: Check the outlet voltage
fixes.description: With a multimeter on the outlet, between both hot terminals: should read ~240V. Each hot to neutral: ~120V. If one reads 0V, that leg has no power.

fixes.title: Have an electrician check the wiring
fixes.description: If the breaker reset and outlet checks don't resolve L2, the outlet wiring or breaker needs professional inspection.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset the circuit breaker. L2 clears when full 240V is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: L2 is almost always an electrical supply problem, not a dryer fault. An electrician should check the outlet and panel wiring if the breaker reset doesn't fix it.
error_codes.models_affected: Electric 240V Whirlpool dryers with digital fault display, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW and WED6620HW; gas models like WGD4815EW are less likely to show a heating-related L2 since they don't rely on both 240V legs for heat.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing suddenly as clothes tumbling but never getting warm, often after a breaker has half-tripped. A frequent quick win is toggling the double-pole breaker fully off then on, since a partially tripped leg can look normal at a glance.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — vent partially blocked
error_codes.detail: The control monitors exhaust temperature rise and drying time through a thermistor and thermal sensing to estimate airflow. When exhaust heat climbs too high or clears too slowly relative to expected airflow, the board flags AF, indicating a partial vent restriction rather than a total blockage. Unlike F30 or thermistor open/short faults that point to sensor circuit failure, AF specifically signals inadequate exhaust airflow while the heating and sensing hardware still read plausibly.

causes.description: Exterior vent cap flap stuck closed or clogged

fixes.title: Clean lint filter
fixes.description: After every load.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean exhaust duct
fixes.description: Full length cleaning with vent brush kit.

fixes.title: Check exterior vent
fixes.description: Clear any lint or debris from exterior vent cap.

fixes.title: Replace flexible duct with rigid metal
fixes.description: For any run over 3 feet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing duct, run Air Only cycle for 20 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is clear and AF persists, the airflow sensor may have failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dryers with airflow/thermal monitoring, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW and the gas WGD4815EW, plus similar recent top-load electric and gas models
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report AF appearing after loads start taking noticeably longer to dry or the drum feels unusually hot, and the quick win is often simply cleaning the lint filter and clearing the exhaust duct and exterior vent cap. A stuck exterior flap on windy days or a kinked flexible duct behind the dryer can trigger it even when the filter looks clean.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — a power interruption stopped the cycle
error_codes.detail: PF is a logged event, not a fault: the control board detected that supply voltage dropped or was interrupted mid-cycle, so it stored the state and displayed PF on the next power-up. It fires on brief brown-outs, a loose cord or outlet, or a weak breaker, and can also appear if a button is pressed during the roughly 10-second power-up delay. Unlike component codes, it points to the incoming power, not a heater, motor, or sensor.

causes.description: Loose power cord or outlet connection

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Touch and hold START to resume, or press POWER to clear the display.

fixes.title: Check the outlet and cord
fixes.description: Confirm the cord is fully seated and the outlet is sound; for electric dryers verify both legs of 240V.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting the cord.

fixes.title: Reset and monitor the breaker
fixes.description: Reset the circuit breaker and watch the dryer for one minute to confirm PF does not return.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Touch and hold START to restart, or press POWER to clear. Reset the breaker if power was lost.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PF returns repeatedly with stable house power, the cord, outlet, or control board power input needs inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electronic-control dryers with a digital display, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PF after a storm, a flickered outlet, or bumping a button right after switching the dryer on, and it usually clears once START is held again. If it keeps returning with steady house power, people often trace it to a partly unseated cord or a tired outlet.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad / user interface stuck key
error_codes.detail: F2E1 fires when the control board reads a continuously active keypad signal for longer than the firmware allows, interpreting it as a stuck button. It also triggers when the keypad ribbon (keytail) is loose or disconnected, so the interface sends no valid input. This differs from a full board failure in that the console still powers on; the control simply sees an invalid or held key state and blocks normal button response until the signal clears.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for one minute, then turn it back on.

fixes.title: Reseat the keypad ribbon
