fixes.description: Open the console and reconnect the keypad keytail/ribbon if it is loose.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/interface
fixes.description: If a key is stuck or the membrane has failed, replace the keypad/user interface.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F2E1 returns, reseat or replace the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the ribbon is seated and no key is stuck, the user interface board needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with electronic touch/membrane keypad consoles, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F2E1 appearing right after a cycle finishes or on power-up, sometimes locking out the controls entirely. A brief breaker power cycle often clears a one-off false trigger, but if it returns quickly, a loose keytail or a physically sticking button is the usual culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad / user interface fault — stuck key detected
error_codes.detail: F02 fires when the control board reads a key as continuously active on the user interface for longer than the firmware's stuck-key threshold. Rather than a momentary press, the board sees uninterrupted contact, which it interprets as a jammed or shorted button. This distinguishes it from broader interface-communication faults like F2E1, which point to the keypad-to-control connection or board itself rather than a single held key. Causes range from a sticky membrane button to a loose ribbon or a failed UI board.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for one minute, then restart.

fixes.title: Inspect the keypad
fixes.description: Check for a physically stuck or sticky button; clean around the keys.

fixes.title: Reseat or replace the interface
fixes.description: Reseat the keypad ribbon; if a key remains stuck, replace the keypad/UI.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F02 returns, reseat or replace the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no key is stuck and the ribbon is seated, the user interface board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with membrane keypad consoles, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F02 appearing after a spill or buildup around the buttons, or when a key feels tacky or slow to release. A simple breaker power cycle and cleaning around the keys often clears it; if a button stays physically depressed or the code returns quickly, the keypad or interface usually needs reseating or replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet (exhaust) thermistor open or shorted
error_codes.detail: F3E1 fires when the control board sees the outlet (exhaust) thermistor circuit reading open or shorted, meaning the resistance falls outside the expected range (near 10k ohms at room temperature). Without a valid exhaust temperature signal, the control cannot regulate the heating element or gas valve safely, so cycles may run cool, run long, or stop. It differs from F3E2, which points to the inlet thermistor, and from cycling-thermostat faults.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for one minute, then restart.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure the outlet thermistor resistance against spec (roughly 10k ohms at room temperature). An open reading means a failed thermistor.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Reconnect the thermistor plug if it is loose.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Replace the outlet thermistor if it tests open or out of range.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F3E1 returns, test and replace the outlet thermistor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but F3E1 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers with electronic controls and F#E# fault codes, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle or shortly after startup, sometimes alongside clothes that stay damp. A loose or corroded connector at the thermistor can falsely trigger it, and many find that reseating the plug or swapping the inexpensive thermistor clears the fault.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor circuit fault
error_codes.detail: F3E2 fires when the control board reads the moisture-sensor circuit as out of range or open — typically the readings drift too high or fail to change across loads, so the board flags an unreliable circuit. It differs from F3E1, which points at a shorted or grounded sensor input. Because the board can no longer trust dryness feedback, sensor-based cycles may end prematurely or run long, while timed cycles usually still complete normally.

fixes.title: Clean the moisture sensor bars
fixes.description: Wipe the metal sensor strips inside the drum with a little rubbing alcohol to remove softener residue.

fixes.title: Run a timed-dry test
fixes.description: Use a timed cycle to confirm the dryer heats and dries normally apart from the sensor.

fixes.title: Check the sensor wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the connector and wiring from the moisture strips to the control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the wiring.

fixes.title: Replace the moisture sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning and wiring checks fail, replace the sensor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the sensor bars and power cycle. If F3E2 returns, check the sensor wiring.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the bars are clean and wiring is good, the moisture sensor or its board input needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dryers with electronic moisture-sensing (Auto/Sensor Dry) controls, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW and the gas WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this after using liquid fabric softener or dryer sheets, which leave an insulating film across the metal sensor bars and mimic a circuit fault. A quick wipe of the bars with rubbing alcohol often clears it, and many find timed-dry cycles keep working in the meantime.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet/exhaust thermistor (temperature sensor) out of range
error_codes.detail: F24 fires when the control board reads the inlet/exhaust thermistor resistance outside its expected window — typically because the sensor is open, shorted, or has drifted far from the roughly 10k-ohm room-temperature spec. Without a valid signal, the board cannot regulate drum temperature, so it flags the sensor rather than the heat source. This distinguishes F24 from cycling-thermostat or heater faults: the code points at the temperature-reading path, not at actual overheating or a failure to heat.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for one minute, then restart.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance against spec (about 10k ohms at room temperature); replace if open or shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Reseat the connector and check the harness to the thermistor for damage.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F24 returns, test and replace the thermistor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but F24 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas dryers that use a thermistor-based temperature sensing circuit, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F24 appearing at cycle start or shortly into drying, sometimes after moving the dryer, which can unseat the thermistor connector. A quick reseat of the connector or a simple power cycle resolves some cases, but a persistent code usually traces back to the sensor itself.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — no signal between control boards
error_codes.detail: F70 fires when the central control unit stops receiving valid data from the user interface (or motor control) board over their communication link. The control board watches for expected handshake signals; when they drop out or go corrupt, it logs a communication fault. Unlike F71, which points more specifically at the interface side, F70 flags a general loss of signal between boards — most often a loose or damaged inter-board harness rather than an outright board failure.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for one minute, then restart.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Open the console and reseat the connectors between the central control and user interface boards.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the faulty board
fixes.description: If reseating fails, replace the user interface or central control board as diagnosed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F70 returns, reseat the inter-board harness.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness is sound, board-level diagnosis is best handled by a technician.
error_codes.models_affected: Electronic-control Whirlpool dryers with separate central control and user-interface boards, including Duet/front-load models and console-control top-load units roughly 2010 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing suddenly after a power blip or when the console is bumped, sometimes with an unresponsive display. A simple breaker power-cycle for a minute clears it in many cases; if it keeps returning, reseating the harness connectors is the usual quick win before suspecting a board.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — user interface not responding
error_codes.detail: F71 fires when the central control unit sends data to the user interface board but fails to receive a valid response within the expected window. The board interprets the missing or corrupted acknowledgment as a communication breakdown. Unlike F70, which flags a broader loss of link, F71 points specifically at the UI board being unresponsive — often a loose interconnecting harness, a failed interface board, or, less often, the central control itself following a power surge.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for one minute, then restart.

fixes.title: Reseat the harness
fixes.description: Reseat the connectors between the central control and the user interface board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the user interface board
fixes.description: If communication does not recover, the UI board is the usual culprit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power down at the breaker for one minute. If F71 returns, reseat the harness then replace the UI board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness and UI board are good, the central control board needs service.
error_codes.models_affected: Electronic-control Whirlpool dryers with a separate user interface board, including models such as WED4815EW, WED5620HW, WED6620HW, and WGD4815EW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F71 appearing at power-up or shortly after selecting a cycle, sometimes with an unresponsive or frozen control panel. A simple breaker power-cycle occasionally clears it if the trigger was a transient glitch, but a code that returns after reseating the harness usually points to the interface board.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: F9E1 fires when the control board does not detect the expected drop in water level after the drain pump is commanded on, meaning the tub fails to empty within the allotted drain time. The board infers a blocked drain path or a non-functioning pump rather than a fill problem. Unlike inlet or float codes, F9E1 is specifically triggered by residual standing water at cycle's end, most often from a clogged filter, kinked hose, or blocked disposal connection.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the cylindrical filter in the bottom of the tub. Rinse and replace.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure the drain hose has a high loop and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Check disposal knockout
fixes.description: If connected to disposal, confirm knockout plug is removed.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clean, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press Cancel then Start to run a new cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails continuity test, replace it. Persistent errors after replacement point to a control board relay fault.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code format, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing at the end of a cycle with a puddle of standing water in the tub. A recently installed garbage disposal with an intact knockout plug is a frequent false trigger, and many find that simply cleaning the bottom filter clears it.

error_codes.meaning: Wash heater or heating element failure
error_codes.detail: F6E4 fires when the control board commands the heater but does not see the wash water reach the expected temperature within the allotted time, based on feedback from the NTC thermistor. This points to an open heating element, a drifting or failed temperature sensor, or a heater relay on the board that won't close. Unlike drain or fill faults, F6E4 is strictly a heating-loop problem, so the machine still fills and cycles but cannot sanitize or dry properly.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect wires and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test the NTC temperature sensor
fixes.description: Resistance at room temp should be approximately 6k ohms.

fixes.title: Check control board
fixes.description: If both element and sensor are good, the relay on the control board controlling the heater has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle. Error clears after a successful heated wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If heating element replacement doesn't resolve F6E4, the control board needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code format with an exposed-element or in-line heating circuit, roughly 2015 onward; includes models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle alongside cold or lukewarm water and dishes that stay wet at the end. It can occasionally be a false trigger from a loose element or sensor wire, so reseating connections before ordering parts is a frequently mentioned quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad error — control panel not communicating
error_codes.detail: F2E2 fires when the main control board fails to establish or maintain serial communication with the keypad/user-interface board. The board sees no valid handshake or button data, so selections and start commands don't register. Unlike F7E1, which points to a stuck or shorted key, F2E2 indicates a broken link—most often a loose ribbon or harness connector between the door-mounted UI and the main board, less often a failed UI board or main-board communication port.

causes.description: Wire harness connector loose between UI and main board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat UI connector
fixes.description: Check and firmly reseat the connector at the UI panel.

fixes.title: Replace UI board
fixes.description: If connector is secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F2E2 returns, replace UI board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't fix F2E2, main board UI port failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers with an electronic touch-panel control interface, including console and top-control models such as the WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, WDF520PADM, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report the panel going dark or unresponsive mid-cycle, or buttons that beep but do nothing. A simple unplug-and-wait power cycle sometimes clears it, and many find that reseating the connector behind the door panel is the quick win before ordering parts.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — low resistance reading
error_codes.detail: The NTC temperature sensor is reading a resistance that indicates it has shorted.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Should read ~6k ohms at room temperature. Near 0 ohms = shorted.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Simple replacement near heating element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and let the 2-minute drain finish, then unplug the dishwasher (or flip the breaker) for 60 seconds. After replacing the thermistor, run a normal cycle to confirm F3E1 stays clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor shows F3E1, control board thermistor input is shorted.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — high resistance / open circuit
error_codes.detail: F3E2 fires when the control measures an open circuit or abnormally high resistance from the NTC temperature sensor, meaning the reading falls above the valid range the board expects. Without a trustworthy temperature signal, the control cannot confirm water heating and may drop the heated-wash or sanitize phases. This distinguishes it from F3E1, which flags a shorted or too-low resistance reading, and from heater-circuit faults rather than the sensor itself.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Should read ~6k ohms at room temp. Infinite = open circuit.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat firmly.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and allow the 2-minute drain to complete, then cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds. After reseating the connector or fitting a new thermistor, run a normal cycle to verify the code is gone.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, wiring harness to sensor has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using an NTC thermistor for water-temperature sensing, common across the WDF, WDT, and WDTA series (e.g. WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, WDF330PAHS)
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle or when a heated or sani option is selected, often alongside dishes coming out cooler than expected. A loose or corroded connector at the sensor is a frequent false trigger, and many find that simply reseating the thermistor plug clears it before any part is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Water heating failure — not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the heater but water temperature never reached the programmed target within the allowed time. Dishes come out poorly washed and the cycle runs long or aborts. A burned-out heating element is the most common root cause, followed by a bad thermistor.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test continuity — 15–30 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: ~6k ohms at room temp.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor good, control board heater relay failed.

error_codes.meaning: Circulation pump motor error
error_codes.detail: F7E1 fires when the control board detects that the main wash circulation pump motor is not turning at the expected speed or is drawing abnormal current during the wash phase. The board monitors motor feedback and flags a fault when the impeller is jammed by debris, the motor windings have failed, or a drive relay on the board misbehaves. Unlike F9E1, which points to drainage problems, F7E1 concerns the circulation (wash) pump that sprays water through the arms, not the drain pump.

fixes.title: Check for debris
fixes.description: Remove filter and check pump impeller area for hard debris.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and let the 2-minute drain run, then unplug or switch off the breaker for 60 seconds. Remove the filter, clear any debris around the pump impeller, and start a short cycle to test.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement is complex. Professional service recommended.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code system, including tall-tub and pocket-handle models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code mid-cycle, often after a glass shard or bit of hard food lodges in the impeller, and clearing debris around the filter and pump sometimes resolves it. A power cycle can clear a false trigger, but if the code returns quickly the pump motor itself is usually the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Calibration needed — sensor recalibration required
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher's sensors need to be recalibrated. This typically appears after a power outage or when settings have been reset.

fixes.title: Let the calibration cycle finish
fixes.description: CAL is a status display, not a fault. Run the dishwasher empty and let the calibration complete uninterrupted — typically 10–20 minutes.

fixes.title: Power cycle if stuck
fixes.description: If CAL stays on the display for more than an hour, switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then run an empty Heavy cycle to recalibrate.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a heavy cycle empty. CAL clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: CAL is not a fault. No parts needed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board / thermistor stuck — electronic control fault
error_codes.detail: F1E1 fires when the main control board detects an internal fault it cannot recover from — typically a relay that reads stuck (won't open or close on command) or a thermistor signal that is electronically impossible, such as an open or shorted reading outside any valid temperature range. Unlike F3E1, which points to the thermistor sensor itself, F1E1 flags the board's own logic and relay circuitry, so cycles become unreliable or won't start.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore.

fixes.title: Check thermistor wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the thermistor harness for shorts or pinched wires to the board.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F1E1 returns, the main control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds. If F1E1 persists, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E1 after a power cycle indicates the control board must be replaced.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code system, roughly 2015 onward, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing suddenly, sometimes right after a power flicker or surge, with the dishwasher refusing to advance through a cycle. A 60-second power-cycle at the breaker occasionally clears a one-off glitch, but many report F1E1 returning, which usually points to the control board rather than a simple reset issue.

error_codes.meaning: User interface stuck key — button held or touchpad fault
error_codes.detail: The control monitors each button on the user interface and expects presses to be momentary. F2E1 fires when the board reads a key as continuously pressed beyond a set time window, which it interprets as a stuck membrane button, a shorted touchpad, or a failing UI board. Moisture bridging contacts behind the panel can mimic a held key. Unlike F2E2 (UI-to-main communication loss) or F1E1 (main control fault), F2E1 points specifically at the button-sensing circuit on the interface itself.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Press each button
fixes.description: Work each button to free a stuck key, and dry any moisture behind the panel.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If a button stays stuck, replace the user interface board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power 60 seconds. Work each button. If F2E1 persists, replace the UI board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new UI board and F2E1 persists, the main board UI port has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers with membrane touchpad or touch-control user interfaces, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after steam or a spill reaches the console, or mid-cycle when a button seems unresponsive. A power cycle plus working each button and drying the panel edge often clears it; if a single button keeps triggering it, the touchpad or UI board is usually the culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch fault — control cannot confirm door state
error_codes.detail: F5E1 fires when the control board reads an invalid or unchanging signal from the door latch switch and cannot confirm the door is securely closed. Because the control interlocks the cycle on this input, it refuses to start or halts partway through as a safety measure. The fault points specifically to the door-state switch or its wiring, distinguishing it from F5E2, which typically indicates a door-latch motor or drive-mechanism fault rather than a switch-reporting problem.

fixes.title: Check the door latches firmly
fixes.description: Ensure the door clicks shut and the latch engages.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Test continuity of the door latch switch as the door is latched.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power 60 seconds. Check the door latches firmly. Replace latch switch if F5E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch switch is good and F5E1 persists, the control board door input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F-E fault code format, including tall-tub and pocket-handle models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code right after loading, especially if a tall item or the utensil basket keeps the door from clicking fully shut. Re-seating the door and confirming a firm latch resolves it in many cases; if it returns even with the door properly closed, the latch switch or its wiring is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Door will not latch / lock — latch mechanism fault
error_codes.detail: F5E2 fires when the control board cannot confirm the door latch has fully engaged and locked. A microswitch inside the latch assembly signals the closed-and-locked state; if that signal is missing or intermittent, the control blocks cycle start to avoid running with an unsecured door. Unlike F5E1, which relates to unexpected latch-state changes, F5E2 specifically flags a failure to reach the latched condition — usually a mechanical latch problem, misalignment, or an open switch circuit.

causes.description: Door latch mechanism stuck or broken

fixes.title: Check for obstructions
fixes.description: Make sure no rack, dish, or item blocks the door from latching.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Look for a broken or sticking latch.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch is broken.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear obstructions and cut power 60 seconds. Replace the latch if F5E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch is sound and F5E2 persists, the control board latch input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F-E fault code format, including built-in models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code when the door seems shut but won't start, often after a rack or tall item nudges the door out of alignment. A firm, deliberate push to fully close the door and clearing any obstruction resolves it in many cases; a worn or cracked latch tends to be the culprit when the code keeps returning.

error_codes.meaning: No water detected — inlet / fill problem
error_codes.detail: F6E3 fires when the control does not sense water reaching the tub within its programmed fill window, so it stops the cycle to protect the pump from running dry. The board watches the inlet valve energizing against feedback from the flow meter or fill sensor. Unlike F8E1, which flags a fill or overfill/leak fault once water is moving, F6E3 specifically means essentially no incoming flow was detected — pointing at supply, valve, or line problems rather than a sensor reading mid-fill.

causes.description: Water inlet valve clogged or failed
Supply shutoff closed or low pressure

fixes.title: Check the water supply
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink supply valve is fully open and the fill line is not kinked.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet valve screen
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill line, and clean the inlet valve screen.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve is clogged or not opening.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm supply valve open, clean the inlet screen, cut power 60 seconds. Replace inlet valve if F6E3 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water supply and inlet valve are good and F6E3 persists, the flow meter or control has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code scheme, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after the under-sink shutoff was left partly closed following plumbing work, or when the fill line got kinked when the unit was pushed back. A frequent quick win is simply opening the supply valve fully and clearing the inlet screen; a slow or clogged inlet valve can mimic a supply problem, so it's worth checking flow before replacing parts.

error_codes.meaning: No / low water fill — slow or insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: F8E1 fires when the control board does not sense the expected water volume within its timed fill window, as measured by the flow meter and monitored against the float switch. It flags slow or insufficient fill rather than an overfill or drain problem, which distinguishes it from float-overfill and drain-related faults. Typical triggers are a partially closed or low-pressure supply, a clogged or weak inlet valve, or a flow-meter/float sensing error that misreports incoming water.

causes.description: Water inlet valve partially clogged or weak

fixes.title: Check water pressure and supply
fixes.description: Confirm the supply valve is fully open and pressure is adequate.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet valve screen
fixes.description: Shut off water and clean the inlet valve inlet screen.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve is weak or clogged.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm supply pressure, clean the inlet screen, cut power 60 seconds. Replace inlet valve if F8E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and valve are good and F8E1 persists, the flow meter or control has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code system, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code early in a cycle, often after plumbing work or when a supply shutoff was left partly closed. A frequent quick win is fully opening the water valve and clearing a clogged inlet screen; if it returns after that, the inlet valve or flow meter is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Diverter motor fault — diverter stuck or not detected
error_codes.detail: F9E2 fires when the control board commands the diverter motor to switch spray between the upper and lower wash arms but cannot confirm the expected position change within its timing window. The board monitors the diverter's rotation or position feedback; a stalled disk, failed motor, or interrupted sensor/wiring signal triggers the code. Unlike F7E1, which points at flow or fill sensing, F9E2 specifically flags the diverter's mechanical actuation and position detection, so spray distribution rather than water volume is the underlying concern.

fixes.title: Clean the sump and diverter area
fixes.description: Remove the filter and clear debris around the diverter disk.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Replace the diverter motor
fixes.description: If the diverter does not actuate.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the diverter area and cut power 60 seconds. Replace the diverter motor if F9E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the diverter is clean and F9E2 persists, the diverter motor or control has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers with a diverter-managed wash system that reports two-digit fault codes, including tall-tub models such as WDT730PAHZ and WDTA50SAKZ, and generally applies across similar Whirlpool builds from the mid-2010s onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code alongside dishes coming out poorly cleaned on either the top or bottom rack, and it often surfaces mid-cycle. Debris or a stray piece of glass jamming the diverter disk in the sump is a frequent false trigger, and clearing the filter area plus a 60-second power cycle sometimes clears it without a part swap.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board / sensor circuit fault — electronic control failure
error_codes.detail: F1E2, shown as the 1-2 blink pair, is set when the main control board's microprocessor fails an internal self-check or detects a sensor circuit it cannot read or recover from. Unlike F1E1 (a control fault) or F2E1 (a stuck-key/user-interface issue), F1E2 points to the board's own logic or its sensor input path. The harness between the board and the temperature/turbidity sensor is checked first, but a sound harness with a persistent code indicates the board itself.

causes.description: Sensor harness between control and sensor damaged

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor harness
fixes.description: Check the harness between the control board and the temperature/turbidity sensor for damage, corrosion, or a loose connector.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: When the harness is sound and F1E2 returns, replace the main control board.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds. If F1E2 persists, the main control board needs to be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F1E2 that returns after a power cycle and a good harness check means the main control board must be replaced.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# / blink-pair fault system, including LED-display models such as WDF520PADM and WDF330PAHS and digital-display models like WDT730PAHZ and WDTA50SAKZ
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing suddenly, sometimes after a storm or power flicker, with the cycle stopping mid-wash. A 60-second power-down at the breaker occasionally clears it as a false trigger, but if it returns after a good harness check the board typically needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet valve electrical fault — valve circuit problem
error_codes.detail: F8E2 fires when the control board monitors the water inlet valve circuit and sees a current draw outside the expected range — an open coil, a short, or a broken connection through the harness. Unlike F8E1, which flags an actual fill or flow problem detected during a cycle, F8E2 points specifically at an electrical fault in the valve or its wiring, so fill becomes unreliable and the tub may not reach the water level needed for a proper wash.

causes.description: Inlet valve coil open or shorted
Wiring / connector to the inlet valve faulty
Inlet valve stuck while still powered

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore.

fixes.title: Check the inlet valve wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness and connector at the water inlet valve for damage or corrosion, and confirm the valve is getting power.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve coil is open/shorted or stuck while powered, replace it.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power 60 seconds. If F8E2 persists, check the inlet valve wiring and replace the inlet valve.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the inlet valve and its wiring test good and F8E2 persists, the control board valve output has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool dishwashers using the F#E# fault-code system, including models such as WDF520PADM, WDT730PAHZ, WDTA50SAKZ, and WDF330PAHS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F8E2 appearing at the start of a cycle right as the machine tries to fill, sometimes with little or no water entering the tub. A brief power cycle occasionally clears a one-off glitch, but a loose or corroded connector at the valve is a frequently cited quick win before replacing the valve itself.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: SY EF fires when the control board monitors the freezer evaporator fan motor and detects no rotation or an out-of-range feedback signal, typically from a stalled blade, an open winding, or a disconnected harness. Because this fan pushes evaporator air into both compartments, the board flags it to prevent widespread warming. It differs from SY CF, which points to the condenser fan on the compressor side; SY EF is specific to the internal freezer air-circulation fan.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Frost buildup is very common. Unplug and let defrost completely before replacing the motor.

fixes.title: Access the evaporator fan
fixes.description: Remove freezer back panel. Fan is mounted on a bracket above or beside the evaporator coils.

fixes.title: Test the motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to test. If motor doesn't spin, replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew bracket, swap motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours for temperature stabilization. Code clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice accumulates again within weeks, the defrost heater is likely failing.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool refrigerators using the SY-series control diagnostics, commonly newer electronic-control side-by-side and French-door models such as the WRS325SDHZ and WRX735SDHZ; similar bottom-freezer and top-freezer units with a monitored evaporator fan may also display it
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code alongside a warming fridge but still-frozen icy sections, and frequently trace it to frost jamming the fan blade rather than a dead motor. A full manual defrost sometimes clears the fault temporarily, but repeat icing within weeks often points to a defrost-system problem worth investigating.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: SY CF is set when the control board detects that the condenser fan motor is not turning or not returning the expected feedback signal while the compressor is running. The board monitors fan operation and flags a failure if RPM or current is out of range. This differs from SY EF, which points to the evaporator fan inside the freeze compartment; SY CF specifically concerns the rear condenser fan near the compressor, whose loss causes overheating and a cooling shutdown.

causes.description: Debris or pet hair blocking fan

fixes.title: Clean the condenser area
fixes.description: Remove rear access panel. Vacuum out dust and pet hair from condenser fan and coils.

fixes.title: Spin fan blade by hand
fixes.description: Should rotate freely. Stiff = bearing failure.

fixes.title: Replace condenser fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew from bracket, install new motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow several hours for temperature recovery. Error clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If motor is clean and free but doesn't run with voltage applied, replace it. If new motor still shows error, control board relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool bottom-mount and side-by-side refrigerators with electronic diagnostics that display SY-series fault codes, including models such as WRS325SDHZ, WRX735SDHZ, and WRF555SDFZ
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing alongside a warm refrigerator and freezer, sometimes after the fan collects heavy dust or pet hair. A false or premature trigger can occur when debris temporarily jams the blade, and many report that a thorough cleaning of the condenser area clears the code without needing a new motor.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The temperature sensor in the freezer compartment has failed. The control board cannot accurately read freezer temperature, which may cause over-cooling or under-cooling.

fixes.title: Locate the freezer thermistor
fixes.description: Usually clipped to the evaporator coil or mounted inside the freezer compartment.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: Should read approximately 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip off old sensor, install new one at same location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 5 minutes then restart. Code clears after normal temperature cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor tests good and code persists, the control board sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted keypad — stuck key on control panel
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control (ERC) continuously scans the keypad membrane matrix. When it reads the same key as pressed for roughly 60 continuous seconds, it interprets this as a shorted or stuck key and displays F2-E0. Unlike F2-E1, which points more directly at the touchpad-to-control interface, F2-E0 indicates the board sees an active key signal — often from grease bridging contacts rather than an outright board fault.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around all buttons with a barely-damp cloth. Dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Many F2E0 errors clear after power cycling.

fixes.title: Replace keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E0 returns after cleaning and cycling, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Wipe panel. If F2E0 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F2E0 persists, the Electronic Range Control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges with electronic membrane keypads, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after cooking spills or cleaning, when moisture or grease bridges a button. A quick win many describe is wiping the panel dry and cycling the breaker, which often clears it; if it returns immediately after every power-up, the keypad itself is usually suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad/touchpad disconnected from control board
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control board continuously monitors the signal lines running through the touchpad ribbon cable. F2-E1 fires when the board detects an open circuit or lost continuity on those lines, meaning it can no longer read keypad input. It differs from F2-E0, which typically indicates a stuck or shorted key rather than a fully disconnected panel. The fault points to the cable, its connectors, or the board's ribbon port rather than a specific button.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check the ribbon cable
fixes.description: The ribbon cable connects the touchpad to the ERC board, typically running behind the control panel. Reseat both connectors.

fixes.title: Replace keypad if cable is integral
fixes.description: On most models the keypad and cable are one assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Check ribbon cable connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new keypad and cable and F2E1 persists, ERC board connector port failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electronic-control ranges and wall ovens with a membrane touchpad, including models like the WFE525S0JZ; simpler manual-control gas ranges such as the WFG320M0BS lack the touchpad circuit and generally do not throw this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a power surge, oven cleaning, or panel service that jostled the ribbon cable, and often nothing on the touchpad responds. A breaker power-cycle and reseating both connector ends is the frequent quick win; a cable that looks cracked or creased usually means replacing the touchpad assembly.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: F3-E0 fires when the control board reads an open circuit across the oven's RTD temperature sensor, meaning no continuity between the two sensor pins. Without valid resistance feedback (normally near 1,080 ohms at room temperature), the board cannot regulate heat and disables the elements or burner for safety. This differs from F3-E1 and F3-E2, which flag a shorted sensor or out-of-range resistance rather than a fully broken connection, and from wiring-only faults you can reseat.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature, Whirlpool oven sensors read approximately 1,080–1,090 ohms. Open circuit = failed sensor.

fixes.title: Check connector at sensor
fixes.description: Reseat the 2-pin connector.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Located in the upper-back of the oven cavity.
fixes.warning: Let oven cool completely.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, turn on and test with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool, KitchenAid, and Maytag ovens sharing the same control platform, including electric ranges like the WFE525S0JZ; gas ranges such as the WFG320M0BS use an equivalent RTD probe
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and F3E0 persists, ERC board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing at power-up or when a bake cycle is started, often with no heat at all. A loose or heat-fatigued 2-pin connector at the rear of the cavity is a frequent false trigger, and many report that simply reseating it or swapping the inexpensive sensor clears the code.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor is reading near-zero resistance, indicating a short circuit. This can cause the oven to detect falsely high temperatures.

causes.description: Wiring pinched against chassis causing short

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Room temp should read ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring
fixes.description: Look for any pinch points along the sensor wiring.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: A shorted sensor cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, test with 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor is shorted too, wiring harness has a short to ground.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — temp exceeded safe limit
error_codes.detail: F3-E2 fires when the control board reads an oven cavity temperature above the maximum safe threshold (roughly 590°F/310°C) during a normal bake or cook cycle. The board compares the RTD sensor's resistance against expected values and shuts down on runaway heating. Unlike F3-E0 and F3-E1, which flag an open or shorted sensor circuit, F3-E2 indicates the temperature actually climbed past the safe limit — typically a stuck bake relay or a sensor drifting low and calling for continuous heat.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure causing runaway heating
Bake relay stuck on control board

fixes.title: Turn off circuit breaker immediately
fixes.description: Allow oven to cool completely — 1 full hour minimum.
fixes.warning: Do not open oven door. Stand clear.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor after cooling
fixes.description: Sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Failed sensor can cause runaway heating.

fixes.title: Replace sensor or control board
fixes.description: If sensor is good, the heating relay on the ERC board is stuck closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Do not use oven until diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F3E2 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required before further use.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric ovens and ranges with an ERC control board and RTD temperature sensor, including the WFE525S0JZ; gas ranges like the WFG320M0BS use the same control logic but reach this fault far less often since their oven heating is burner-driven.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing mid-bake or during self-clean, sometimes with the oven still noticeably hot. A cooled-down unit may seem fine briefly, but the code often returns; because it can signal a stuck relay that keeps heating, most report leaving the breaker off until it's checked rather than risking another runaway.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch failure — latch not sensing door position
error_codes.detail: The door switch is not detecting the door position correctly during a self-clean cycle or normal operation.

fixes.title: Check door closes flush
fixes.description: The door must close completely and evenly.

fixes.title: Test door switch
fixes.description: The switch is typically near the door latch. Test continuity with door open and closed.

fixes.title: Replace door switch
fixes.description: If contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Check door alignment and switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door switch replaced and F5E0 persists, the ERC board door switch input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Self-clean door latch failure — lock timeout
error_codes.detail: During a self-clean cycle the control board commands the motorized door latch to lock and monitors a switch that confirms latch position. F5-E1 fires when the latch fails to reach the locked or unlocked state within the allotted time, indicating a lock timeout. This differs from F5-E0, which flags a latch switch signal that disagrees with the commanded state rather than a failure to move at all. Causes range from a failed latch motor to a jammed mechanism or damaged wiring.

causes.description: Door latch mechanism stuck or jammed

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check latch for obstructions
fixes.description: Food debris can jam the latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test and replace latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch motor is at the top-rear of the range.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F5E1 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch and F5E1 persists, ERC board latch relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric ranges with self-clean, including WFE525S0JZ; gas models without a motorized self-clean latch are generally not affected
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing right after starting a self-clean cycle, or occasionally mid-cycle when the door won't unlock afterward. A brief power cycle and clearing any food debris around the latch sometimes resolves a stuck mechanism, but a recurring code usually points to a worn latch motor.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch during lock function — latch system fault
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the door latch to lock (usually for self-clean) but never received confirmation that the latch reached the locked position, so the cycle is locked out. A failed latch motor or a misaligned latch switch is the most common root cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door alignment and latch
fixes.description: Ensure door closes completely.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F9E0 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new latch and F9E0 persists, ERC board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM communication error — control board memory fault
error_codes.detail: F1-E0 fires when the Electronic Range Control runs its startup self-check and fails to read or verify data from the onboard EEPROM, the chip that holds calibration and user settings. Without valid memory contents the control cannot trust its operating parameters, so it halts and flags a memory fault. Unlike F1-E1 or F1-E2, which point to other control-side faults, F1-E0 specifically indicates the EEPROM communication or integrity check failed, often from a corrupted read rather than a sensor input.

causes.description: EEPROM chip or control board failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power. A surge-induced F1E0 often clears.

fixes.title: Check incoming voltage
fixes.description: Verify a stable 240V supply. Brownouts can corrupt EEPROM comms.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E0 returns, the control board EEPROM has failed and the board must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E0 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Maytag ovens sharing the same ERC platform
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If power cycling does not clear F1E0, the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F1-E0 appearing suddenly after a storm, power flicker, or brownout, and many find a five-minute breaker reset clears a one-time surge glitch. When it returns quickly or locks the panel with constant beeping, it usually points to a failed control board rather than a false trigger.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM checksum error — corrupted control memory
error_codes.detail: The data stored in the ERC board's EEPROM failed its checksum verification, meaning the stored calibration or configuration data has been corrupted.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. A one-time corruption can clear on reboot.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E1 returns, the EEPROM data cannot be restored in the field and the board must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E1 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E1 means corrupted control memory — replace the ERC board.

error_codes.meaning: UL A/D error — microprocessor analog-to-digital fault
error_codes.detail: The control board's microprocessor is unable to perform analog-to-digital conversions correctly (the 'UL' or upper-limit watchdog circuit). The board cannot reliably read its sensor inputs.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: An A/D conversion fault is internal to the control board and not field-repairable.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E2 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F1E2 is an internal control fault — replace the ERC board.

error_codes.meaning: Model ID error — control not configured for this model
error_codes.detail: F1-E4 fires when the ERC board's stored model identification cannot be matched to the appliance. The control reads a model ID resistor, jumper, or EEPROM value on startup; if that value is missing, out of range, or corrupted, the board cannot load the correct operating parameters and halts. Unlike F1-E0 (general EEPROM/control fault) or F1-E5, F1-E4 points specifically to a configuration mismatch, most often after a replacement board with the wrong part number is installed.

causes.description: Replacement board not configured for this model

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Verify correct replacement board
fixes.description: If F1E4 appeared after a board swap, confirm the part number matches your exact model.

fixes.title: Replace with correct ERC board
fixes.description: Install a board configured for your model number.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Verify the control board matches your model.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the correct board is installed and F1E4 persists, the model ID circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges/ovens with an electronic range control (ERC) board that reads a model ID resistor or jumper, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing right after a control-board replacement, when the substitute board isn't configured for the exact model. A brief power cycle at the breaker sometimes clears a one-off misread, but if it returns the usual quick win is confirming the board's part number matches the model exactly.

error_codes.meaning: Calibration shifted — stored temperature calibration out of range
error_codes.detail: F1-E5 fires when the control reads its stored oven temperature calibration values from EEPROM and finds them outside the factory-acceptable window, so it can no longer trust the calibration used to regulate bake and broil temperatures. Unlike F1-E0 (general EEPROM/control fault) or F1-E4 (calibration shift on a specific element circuit), F1-E5 points specifically to corrupted or drifted stored calibration data rather than a sensor or heating-circuit reading, often triggered by a power surge disturbing memory.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Re-run oven temperature calibration
fixes.description: If your model supports user calibration offset, reset it to 0 and re-test.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E5 returns, the calibration data is unrecoverable.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Reset calibration offset if persistent.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E5 after power cycling indicates the control board must be replaced.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electronic-control ranges and wall ovens using an ERC/EEPROM-based control, including models such as WFE525S0JZ; gas ranges with electronic controls like the WFG320M0BS may report the same family of F1 codes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a power outage, surge, or lightning event, sometimes accompanied by tones or an unresponsive keypad. A breaker power cycle of several minutes clears it in many cases, and resetting any user calibration offset to zero is a frequent quick win before it comes back and points to the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Latch signal mismatch — door latch feedback disagrees with control
error_codes.detail: F1-E6 fires when the control's latch-position feedback contradicts the commanded latch state — for example, the board drives the motorized latch to lock but the position switch never confirms it, or reports locked when unlocked. It points specifically to the door-latch switch, latch motor, or their wiring rather than to a bad locked-door condition. This distinguishes it from F5-series codes, which indicate the door is actually unlocked or self-clean cannot be started, and from control-board (F9) faults.

causes.description: Door latch switch failure or misalignment
Wiring between latch and board damaged

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch assembly
fixes.description: Check that the latch moves freely and its position switch actuates correctly.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch switch feedback is faulty.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F1E6 returns, inspect and replace the door latch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new latch and F1E6 persists, the control board latch input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool ranges and wall ovens equipped with a motorized self-clean door latch; among listed models the WFE525S0JZ electric range applies, while the WFG320M0BS gas range without a self-clean latch is generally not affected
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing when starting or ending a self-clean cycle, and a five-minute power cycle sometimes clears a one-off glitch. A latch that is sticky, misaligned, or slow to seat can falsely trigger it, and freeing or realigning the latch mechanism is a frequently reported quick win before replacing parts.

error_codes.meaning: Cancel key drive line open — control panel fault
error_codes.detail: F2-E5 fires when the control board runs its key-scan routine and finds the drive line dedicated to the Cancel/Off key reading as open, meaning that key can never register a press. The board flags a stuck or disconnected keypad matrix rather than a general control failure. Unlike F2-E0 or F2-E1, which indicate a broadly shorted or non-responsive keypad, F2-E5 isolates the single Cancel-key trace, usually pointing to a broken touchpad membrane or an unseated ribbon cable at the board header.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reconnect the touchpad ribbon cable to the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E5 persists, the membrane keypad has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable. If F2E5 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F2E5 persists, the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool ranges and wall ovens with an electronic membrane touchpad and F2-Ex diagnostic family, including models such as WFE525S0JZ; simpler gas ranges like the WFG320M0BS with rotary or mechanical controls generally do not report this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing on its own with the oven beeping and refusing button presses, often after a spill, steam exposure, or age-related wear on the touchpad. A power cycle and reseating the ribbon cable sometimes clears it temporarily, but many find the code returns until the membrane touchpad is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Cancel key drive line open — control panel fault (variant)
error_codes.detail: F2-E6 indicates the control detects an open drive line specifically for the Cancel/Off key during its key-scan routine, so it cannot confirm that key. Like F2-E5, it points to the touchpad, its ribbon cable, or the board's scanning circuit rather than a stuck key. It differs from F2-E0/E1 shorted-key faults, which report a continuously pressed key, whereas F2-E6 reflects a broken or unreadable Cancel line connection.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reconnect the touchpad ribbon cable to the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E6 persists, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable. If F2E6 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F2E6 persists, the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool ranges and wall ovens with an electronic membrane touchpad control, including models such as WFE525S0JZ; gas ranges with the same touchpad style like WFG320M0BS may report the same family of F2 codes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing after the oven finishes a cycle or randomly during standby, often with beeping that won't clear. A power cycle at the breaker sometimes resolves a one-time glitch, but if it returns, reseating or replacing the ribbon cable and touchpad is the fix most people report success with.

error_codes.meaning: Meat probe defect — temperature probe fault
error_codes.detail: F4-E1 fires when the control board reads the meat/temperature probe circuit and finds resistance outside the valid range, most often a shorted probe or a fault at the cavity jack. Because the reading is invalid rather than absent, the board flags a probe defect. This differs from F3-E0/F3-E1, which point to the oven's own cavity temperature sensor rather than the removable meat probe, so cooking generally continues once the probe is unplugged.

causes.description: Probe left plugged in or wiring fault

fixes.title: Remove the meat probe
fixes.description: Unplug the probe from the oven jack. If the error clears, the probe is faulty.

fixes.title: Inspect the probe jack
fixes.description: Check the receptacle inside the cavity for damage or food debris.

fixes.title: Replace the meat probe
fixes.description: Use a replacement probe matched to your model.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the meat probe. Power cycle 5 minutes. Replace probe if F4E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new probe and F4E1 persists, the probe jack or control input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric wall ovens and ranges equipped with a meat-probe jack, including models like the WFE525S0JZ; gas ranges without a probe circuit generally will not display this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a probe was left plugged in during self-clean or high-temp baking, or when food debris has collected in the jack. A frequent quick win is simply unplugging the probe and power-cycling; if it clears, a replacement probe usually resolves it.

error_codes.meaning: Return line not connected — control wiring fault
error_codes.detail: F6-E0 fires when the ERC cannot detect a valid keypad return (scan) line, breaking the key-scan matrix the control uses to read button presses. The board expects continuity across the ribbon connector; if the return line reads open, it flags the fault. Unlike F2-E1, which signals a stuck or shorted keypad, F6-E0 points to a disconnected or damaged ribbon cable or control harness rather than a held key, though a failed control board can also trigger it.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat all control connectors
fixes.description: Reconnect the keypad ribbon cable and verify control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If the wiring is sound and F6E0 persists, the control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable and control connectors.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If all connections are secure and F6E0 persists, replace the control board.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges/ovens with an electronic range control (ERC) and membrane keypad, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after a repair, cleaning, or a move that may have loosened the ribbon cable, and a power cycle plus firmly reseating the connectors often clears it. If it returns immediately, the harness or control board is usually the culprit rather than a one-time glitch.

error_codes.meaning: Common switch wire defective — function key fault
error_codes.detail: F7-E1 indicates the control board detects a fault on the common (shared) switch line that connects the function keys. When this wire shorts or reads as continuously closed, the board interprets it as a stuck function key. Unlike a plain F7 or the F2-E0 stuck-key code, F7-E1 points specifically to the shared common wire in the touchpad membrane or its ribbon connection rather than a single individual key or the clock/keypad interface as a whole.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat and inspect the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Look for cracks or damage in the touchpad ribbon cable.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F7E1 persists, the membrane keypad common wire has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F7E1 persists, replace the touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F7E1 persists, the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ovens/ranges with membrane touchpad control panels, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing on its own, sometimes with keys that stop responding or beep continuously. A brief power cycle at the breaker occasionally clears a transient trigger, but many find it returns until the touchpad ribbon is reseated or the membrane keypad is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: F0 is a general fault the Electronic Range Control flags during its own self-diagnostic routine when the board's internal logic or memory check fails. Unlike F1 (a specific control/watchdog fault) or F8 (analog/EEPROM-related errors), F0 is a catch-all indicating the ERC itself could not verify normal operation. It often traces to a degraded board, but a power surge or a loose control harness connection can also trigger the self-check to fail.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixes.title: Reseat control connectors
fixes.description: Check the control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F0 persists after power cycling, replace the control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F0 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F0 indicates the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool ranges and wall ovens using an Electronic Range Control (ERC) board, primarily older models with two-digit F-codes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F0 appearing suddenly, sometimes after a storm or brief power interruption, occasionally with beeping. A quick win many describe is cutting power at the breaker for several minutes, which can clear a one-time glitch; if it returns, the board usually needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: F1 is the Electronic Range Control's self-diagnostic flag for an internal fault it can't recover from — most often a corrupted EEPROM read/write or a stalled microprocessor. The board watches its own memory and logic during operation and startup; when checks fail, it latches F1 and usually beeps. On controls reporting sub-codes it maps to the F1-E# family. Unlike F0 (cancel/key-stuck) or sensor-specific codes, F1 points at the control electronics themselves rather than the oven's heating or probe circuits.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat control connectors
fixes.description: Check the control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1 persists, the control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1 indicates the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool ranges and ovens using an Electronic Range Control (ERC), spanning both older two-digit displays and newer boards that report F1-E# sub-codes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F1 appearing suddenly with continuous beeping, sometimes right after a power surge or storm. A breaker power-cycle often clears a one-time glitch, but many find the code returns within days, which usually points to a failing board.

error_codes.meaning: Control failure — door/latch circuit
error_codes.detail: F5 fires when the control board cannot verify the expected state of the door switch and self-clean latch circuit. During and after a clean cycle the board drives the motorized latch and reads its position and the door switch; if the latch position never confirms, sticks between states, or the switch signal is inconsistent, the board flags F5. This is a latch/door circuit fault, distinct from temperature-sensor codes (F3) or generic control faults, and often maps to the F5-E0/E1 sub-code family.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door and latch
fixes.description: Ensure the door closes fully and the latch moves freely.

fixes.title: Replace door latch or switch
fixes.description: If the latch or door switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Check door and latch operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch and switch are good and F5 persists, the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges with a self-clean latch, including models like WFE525S0JZ; basic non-self-clean models such as WFG320M0BS are less likely to show this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F5 appearing right after a self-clean cycle, when a stuck or overheated latch motor fails to return to its resting position. A power cycle at the breaker for several minutes often clears it if the door is fully closed and the latch moves freely; a latch jammed by warping or debris is a frequent false trigger.

error_codes.meaning: Function key stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F7 indicates a stuck or shorted function key on the control panel. The control detects a key held closed and cannot return to normal operation.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around all buttons with a barely-damp cloth and dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F7 returns, the membrane keypad has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean panel, power cycle 5 minutes. If F7 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F7 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F8 indicates a control board failure, often an EEPROM or analog reference fault detected during self-diagnostics. On sub-code controls this maps to internal control faults.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Switch the oven circuit breaker off for 5 minutes, then back on; this clears a transient EEPROM fault on many controls.

fixes.title: Check incoming voltage
fixes.description: With the breaker on, verify a stable 240V supply at the oven — unstable or low voltage can trip false control faults.

fixes.title: Inspect board connectors
fixes.description: Unplug the oven and reseat the ribbon and wiring connectors on the control board; corrosion here mimics board failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC control board
fixes.description: If F8 persists after a power cycle and the voltage is good, the electronic range control has failed and must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off the oven's circuit breaker for a full 5 minutes to let the control board discharge, then restore power. If F8 reappears within a cycle, the control board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a power cycle and connector check do not clear F8 and the 240V supply tests good, the electronic range control board needs replacement — a job best left to a technician on wired-in ovens.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature too high — over-temperature fault
error_codes.detail: F2 fires when the Electronic Range Control reads an oven temperature above its safe ceiling and shuts down heating. The RTD sensor's resistance drops as temperature rises; a shorted or low-reading sensor fools the control into either misreporting or the control detecting a genuine runaway from a relay welded closed that keeps an element powered. Unlike F3 (open sensor) or F4 (shorted sensor circuit), F2 reflects an actual over-temperature condition the board must abort to protect the appliance.

causes.description: Oven temperature sensor (RTD) shorted, reading low
Bake/broil relay welded closed on the control

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.
