fixes.title: Test the oven temperature sensor
fixes.description: Check the RTD sensor resistance — roughly 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature; a shorted/low reading causes over-temp.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the sensor is good but an element stays powered, a welded relay on the ERC board is the cause.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing — do not keep operating an oven that overheats.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. If F2 returns or the oven overheats, stop using it and have the sensor and control board checked.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: An oven that actually overheats is a safety hazard. If a power cycle does not clear F2, stop using the oven until the sensor and control board are diagnosed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges with an Electronic Range Control, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F2 appearing mid-bake or during self-clean, sometimes with the oven noticeably hotter than the setpoint. A single power cycle occasionally clears a one-off glitch, but if the code returns or the oven keeps heating, most find the RTD sensor or a stuck relay is the real culprit and stop using the oven until it's checked.

error_codes.meaning: Open oven temperature sensor — sensor circuit open
error_codes.detail: F3 fires when the Electronic Range Control reads an open circuit on the oven temperature sensor (RTD) input — resistance appears infinite rather than the expected ~1080 ohms at room temperature. Without a valid reading the control cannot regulate bake or broil heat and locks out. It differs from F4, which flags a shorted or out-of-range (low-resistance) sensor, and from F2, which relates to over-temperature or keypad faults rather than a broken sensor connection.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature sensor
fixes.description: Check the RTD resistance — about 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature; an open/infinite reading confirms a failed sensor.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor harness
fixes.description: Reconnect or repair a broken sensor connector at the back of the oven cavity.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F3 persists, test and replace the oven temperature sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor reads correctly and the harness is sound but F3 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges/ovens using a two-digit Electronic Range Control fault system, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F3 appearing at startup or mid-bake, sometimes after cleaning behind the range where the sensor connector can be bumped loose. A brief power cycle occasionally clears a one-off glitch, but a persistent code usually points to reseating the connector or swapping the inexpensive sensor as the quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted oven temperature sensor — sensor reading short
error_codes.detail: F4 fires when the Electronic Range Control reads oven-sensor (RTD) resistance far below the valid window — effectively a short. A healthy sensor measures roughly 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature, so a very low reading tells the board the temperature data is untrustworthy and it halts normal cooking. This is the mirror of the open-sensor fault: F4 indicates a shorted sensor or a harness chafed to ground, whereas an open circuit trips a different code in the same family.

causes.description: Sensor harness pinched / shorted to ground

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature sensor
fixes.description: Check the RTD resistance — about 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature; a very low reading confirms a shorted sensor.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor harness
fixes.description: Look for a pinched or chafed sensor wire shorting against the cavity or chassis.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F4 persists, test and replace the oven temperature sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor reads correctly and the harness is sound but F4 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges with an Electronic Range Control and RTD-type oven sensor, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F4 appearing at power-up or partway through preheat, often after the oven has been moved or a rear panel disturbed, which can pinch the sensor wire. A five-minute power cycle sometimes clears a transient fault, but a persistent code usually points to the sensor or its harness.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch fault — self-clean lock circuit
error_codes.detail: F9 fires when the Electronic Range Control cannot confirm the motorized self-clean latch has reached its commanded position within the expected time. During lock or unlock, the control watches the latch position switch; if the switch state never matches the command, it flags a latch circuit fault and blocks self-clean and often bake. Unlike overtemperature codes (F5 family), F9 is strictly about mechanical latch travel and its feedback switch, motor, or wiring, not sensor readings.

causes.description: Door latch motor or latch assembly failed

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Check that the self-clean latch moves freely and is not jammed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing the latch.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch motor or switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F9 persists, inspect and replace the door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch and its switch are good and F9 persists, the control board latch circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges with a motorized self-clean door latch, including models such as WFE525S0JZ; the F9 door-latch code applies broadly across freestanding ranges with a self-clean cycle
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F9 appearing right after starting or ending a self-clean cycle, sometimes with the door still locked. A jammed or slow latch is a frequent false trigger, and many find a five-minute power cycle clears it temporarily before the latch assembly needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Control board software fault — stack overflow
error_codes.detail: F1-E9 fires when the oven's electronic control board firmware encounters a stack-overflow condition — the processor exhausts its internal memory stack and can no longer execute reliably, so it halts and reports the fault. Unlike sensor-driven codes, this is an internal software/hardware fault rather than a reading from the oven cavity. It sits within the F1-E# board family; related codes like F1-E0 and F1-E1 point to other internal control-board failures, but E9 specifically flags a stack overflow.

fixes.title: Reset power
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for at least 5 minutes, then restore power and retest.

fixes.title: Reseat control connectors
fixes.description: Check the harness connections to the appliance control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F1-E9 returns after a power reset, replace the appliance control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. If F1-E9 persists, the control board needs to be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F1-E9 that returns after a power reset means the appliance control board must be replaced.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges/ovens using the F1-E# control-board diagnostic family, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing suddenly, sometimes with continuous beeping, and frequently after a power surge or brownout. A breaker reset of several minutes often clears it temporarily; when it keeps returning, most find the control board itself needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Wiring harness / cavity size mismatch
error_codes.detail: F1-E3 fires when the control compares the cavity-size configuration it reads from the model jumper or wiring harness against the value stored in its onboard memory and finds a mismatch. Unlike F1-E4 (a related configuration or memory error) or a generic F1, this code points specifically at a harness/jumper reading the board doesn't recognize. It most often follows a control board swap with an unconfigured or wrong part, or a damaged, disconnected configuration harness.

causes.description: Replacement control board not configured for this model/cavity

fixes.title: Reset power
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power and retest.

fixes.title: Check the configuration harness
fixes.description: Verify the model/cavity-size jumper or configuration harness is connected and undamaged.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

fixes.title: Use the correct control board
fixes.description: If a board was just replaced, confirm it is the correct part configured for this oven's cavity size.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes and confirm the configuration harness is connected. If F1-E3 followed a board swap, fit the correct configured board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the configuration harness is sound and the board is the correct part but F1-E3 persists, the control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool electric and gas ranges/ovens using the F1-E series fault reporting, including models such as WFE525S0JZ and WFG320M0BS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing right after a control board replacement, since the new board may not be configured for the oven's cavity size. A simple 5-minute power cycle sometimes clears a one-time glitch, but if it returns after a board swap, the wrong or unconfigured part is the usual culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Heater open circuit — heating element has failed
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the wash-water heating element's circuit resistance during hot, warm, and sanitize cycles. When it measures an open circuit rather than the expected 20–30 ohms, it fires E64, indicating the element has burned through, its wiring is broken, or the heater relay isn't completing the loop. Unlike E61, which points to a heater that runs but fails to reach temperature, E64 reflects a total loss of electrical continuity, so no heat is produced at all.

causes.description: Heater wiring harness damaged or disconnected

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect the two wires from the heater. Test with a multimeter for continuity. A good heater reads approximately 20–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed heater.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect both wires leading to the heater for damage, burned insulation, or loose connections.

fixes.title: Replace the heating element
fixes.description: The element is typically accessible from under the tub. Follow service manual for your specific model.
fixes.warning: Unplug and fully drain the washer before accessing the heater.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the heating element, unplug for 5 minutes and restart. The code clears after a successful heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element access requires significant disassembly. Professional service is recommended if you're not experienced.
error_codes.models_affected: GE front-load washers with an internal water heating circuit, such as the GFW550SSNWW, GFW650SSNWW, and GFW850SPNRS; top-load models without a heater (e.g. GTW465ASNWW) generally do not report this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E64 appearing only on hot or sanitize cycles while cold washes run normally, which can make it easy to overlook at first. Before condemning the element, it's worth checking the two heater wires, since a loose or corroded connection sometimes triggers the same code and is a much quicker fix.

error_codes.meaning: Water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The water entering the washer is below the expected temperature for the selected cycle. This can indicate a water heater issue in the home, a cross-connected supply hose, or a failing water temperature sensor.

causes.description: Home water heater set too low or needs time to deliver hot water
Hot and cold supply hoses connected in reverse
Water inlet valve not opening hot side

fixes.title: Check hot water supply
fixes.description: Run the hot water faucet at a nearby sink and feel the temperature. If cold, the home water heater has an issue.

fixes.title: Verify hose connections
fixes.description: Make sure the hot hose (usually red coded) connects to the hot supply valve and hot port on the washer.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: The thermistor reads incoming water temperature. Test resistance and compare to spec.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the supply issue or replacing the sensor, restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hot water is confirmed good at the tap but E61 persists, the NTC sensor or inlet valve hot side needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater relay failure on main control board
error_codes.detail: The relay on the main control board that switches power to the heating element has failed. The element may be physically fine, but the board cannot send power to it.

causes.description: Heater relay stuck open on control board

fixes.title: Confirm heater element is good
fixes.description: Test element first (see E64) to confirm the element itself is not the issue before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If element tests OK, the board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If E65 returns with a known-good heater element, the board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement for GE washers is complex. Professional diagnosis is recommended to confirm the board and not the element is the cause.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor (water temperature sensor) has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings to the control board. The washer's heating function is disabled.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect and test resistance with multimeter. Room temperature reading should be approximately 6k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat connector firmly at the thermistor mounting.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Install new thermistor and ensure it's properly clipped to the heater or drum housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and tests correctly but error persists, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Spin speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: The washer motor is not spinning the drum at the commanded speed. Clothes will exit the cycle still wet.

causes.description: Drum overloaded preventing motor from reaching speed

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items and retry with a smaller load.

fixes.title: Check drive belt
fixes.description: On belt-driven models, inspect the belt for wear, stretching, or breakage.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixes.description: On brushed DC motors, worn brushes cause speed loss. Replace if worn below 1/4 inch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a Spin Only cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If belt and brushes are fine and error persists, motor control board testing is required.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board detected that the drive motor failed to run or drew excessive current when commanded. The drum will not tumble or spin and the cycle halts. Worn motor windings or a broken drive belt letting the motor race are the most common root causes.

fixes.title: Check drive belt
fixes.description: Inspect for breakage or slipping.

fixes.title: Test motor windings
fixes.description: Test continuity of motor coils.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the control, then plug back in and run a short spin cycle. If E43 returns as soon as the drum tries to turn, the motor or motor control board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Motor speed sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board stopped receiving speed feedback from the motor tachometer, so it cannot regulate drum speed. The washer may agitate erratically or refuse to spin. A failed tach sensor on the motor or a chafed sensor wire is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test speed sensor
fixes.description: Check resistance per service manual.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes to clear the code, then restart on a spin cycle. If E44 reappears once the drum starts turning, test and replace the motor speed sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and error persists, control board input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The control board commanded the door lock to engage or release but never received confirmation from the lock switch. The cycle cannot start, or the door stays locked after it ends. A worn latch or failed lock solenoid is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Open and close the door checking for a worn, cracked, or sticking latch hook that won't seat in the strike.

fixes.title: Test the lock solenoid
fixes.description: With power off, check the door lock solenoid windings for continuity; an open coil won't engage the lock.

fixes.title: Check the lock wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness and connector between the control and the door lock for breaks or loose pins.

fixes.title: Replace the lock assembly
fixes.description: If the latch or solenoid is faulty, fit a new door lock assembly and confirm the door reports locked.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly until it clicks and restart the cycle. If E47 returns, unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then test the door latch and lock solenoid before running another load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door latches properly and the wiring checks good but E47 persists, the door lock solenoid or the control board's lock circuit has failed and needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The GE dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time. Standing water remains in the tub.

causes.description: Drain filter clogged with food particles
Drain hose kinked or improperly high-looped

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the filter at the bottom of the tub (twist counterclockwise) and rinse clean.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the hose runs up to the counter underside before dropping to the drain.

fixes.title: Check the disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal and confirm the connection point is clear.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If all above clear, test pump motor for operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run a rinse cycle to confirm drain.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump is running but water doesn't drain, the pump impeller may be broken requiring pump replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Flood switch activated — water in base tray
error_codes.detail: The flood protection switch has detected water in the base of the dishwasher. The unit will not run until the water is cleared and the leak source is fixed.

fixes.title: Tilt unit to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward (with power off) to let base water drain.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at breaker before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check entire perimeter of door gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply line and drain hose connections inside the unit.

fixes.title: Check detergent amount
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher-specific detergent, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base tray, fix leak source, and restart. Code clears once sensor is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, the tub itself may be cracked, requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor failure — circulation pump not running
error_codes.detail: The main circulation pump motor has failed or is drawing incorrect current. The dishwasher cannot perform a wash cycle.

causes.description: Foreign object jammed in pump impeller

fixes.title: Check for debris in pump
fixes.description: Remove filter and reach into sump to clear any debris from impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor is burned, full pump assembly replacement is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing jams, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement requires significant disassembly. Professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted by power outage
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detected a power interruption during a cycle. This is not a fault — it is an informational code. The cycle needs to be restarted.

causes.description: Power outage or interruption during cycle

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to resume or select a new cycle.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: If power interruptions are frequent, check the dishwasher's dedicated circuit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start to begin a new cycle. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PF does not require service unless power interruptions are frequent — in that case, check household wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — not draining properly
error_codes.detail: The control board did not see the water level drop during the drain period — the pump ran but water stayed in the tub, leaving standing water after the cycle. A clogged drain filter or a kinked or improperly looped drain hose is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the tub bottom.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure high-loop installation and no kinks.

fixes.title: Check disposal
fixes.description: Run disposal before cycle.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, test pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, cancel and restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main control board failed an internal self-check or stopped responding, so the dishwasher may go unresponsive or halt mid-cycle. Internal component failure on the board, often triggered by a power surge or moisture intrusion, is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Power-cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Cut power at the breaker or unplug for 15 minutes to let the board fully reset, then restore power and retry a cycle.

fixes.title: Check for moisture intrusion
fixes.description: Inspect the control housing in the door for water tracks or condensation that can corrupt the board's logic.

fixes.title: Inspect power connections
fixes.description: Verify the incoming supply and board connectors are tight; a surge or loose neutral can fault the board.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If C3 returns after a reset, the main board has failed internally and must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker (or unplug) for 15 minutes so the control board fully discharges, then restore power and start a short cycle. If C3 returns, the board has failed and needs replacing.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a power cycle doesn't clear C3 and there's no obvious moisture or surge damage, the main board has an internal fault — a technician should confirm and replace it to avoid wiring errors.

error_codes.meaning: OWI sensor failure — optical water indicator fault
error_codes.detail: The optical sensor that detects water clarity (how dirty the wash water is) has failed. The dishwasher may run shorter cycles than needed.

causes.description: OWI sensor lens dirty or coated

fixes.title: Clean the OWI sensor
fixes.description: Located on the sump assembly. Clean the lens with a soft cloth.

fixes.title: Test and replace OWI sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning or replacing, run a normal wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: C7 rarely causes complete failure — dishwasher still washes, just uses default cycle length.

error_codes.meaning: Water not hot enough — incoming water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detected that the incoming water temperature is below the minimum needed for effective washing.

causes.description: Home water heater temperature set too low (below 120°F/49°C)
Hot water run too long before cycle — hot water depleted

fixes.title: Run hot water at nearby sink
fixes.description: Run the kitchen hot water faucet for 30–60 seconds before starting the dishwasher. This clears the cold water in the supply line.

fixes.title: Check water heater temperature
fixes.description: Set to at least 120°F (49°C).

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: If water is hot at tap but HO persists, test the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run hot water at sink first, then restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water heater is hot and sensor replaced but HO persists, the inlet valve hot side is not opening.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor fault — water level sensing issue
error_codes.detail: The control board is receiving implausible or no readings from the water level pressure sensor, so it cannot verify the fill level. Fills may be wrong or the cycle may halt. A failed sensor or a blocked pressure hose from the sump is the usual cause.

causes.description: Air trap or hose to sensor blocked

fixes.title: Check air trap and hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose from the sump to the sensor should be clear.

fixes.title: Test and replace sensor
fixes.description: If hose is clear, test sensor and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and clear hose don't resolve PrS, control board pressure input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Slow drain — pump-out during drain exceeded ~2 minutes
error_codes.detail: The control timed the drain and the pump took longer than about 2 minutes to clear the water, indicating restricted drainage. On many newer GE/Profile/Café models with a touch UI, C1 instead reports a stuck control-panel key — clean the panel and power-cycle if no drain restriction is found.

causes.description: Air gap or drain hose partially clogged
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed / disposal blocked

fixes.title: Clean the air gap
fixes.description: Remove the air gap cap on the sink and clear any debris.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose and disposal
fixes.description: Reposition a kinked hose and run the disposal to clear it.

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the tub-bottom filter.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If drainage stays slow after clearing, test the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the restriction, then press RESET (or Start/Cancel) and run a new cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: GE, GE Profile, and GE Café dishwashers
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drainage is clear but C1 keeps returning, the drain pump or control board drain timing circuit is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — total pump-out cycle exceeded ~7 minutes
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the drain phase and expects standing water to clear within a set window. When the drain pump runs cumulatively past its maximum pump-out time (about 7 minutes) and the sensor still detects water in the tub, the board halts the cycle and flags C2. Unlike C1, which signals a slower or partial drain, C2 represents a full failure to evacuate water, pointing to a hard blockage in the drain path or a non-functioning pump.

causes.description: Drain hose or air gap clogged
Disposal blocked / knockout plug in place

fixes.title: Press RESET to stop the alarm
fixes.description: Press RESET to silence the beep before troubleshooting.

fixes.title: Clear hose, air gap, and disposal
fixes.description: Inspect the full drain path for blockages.

fixes.title: Clean filter and sump
fixes.description: Check the sump area at the tub bottom for debris.

fixes.title: Test and replace drain pump
fixes.description: If the path is clear, the pump is the likely failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the blockage, press RESET, then start a Rinse cycle to confirm drainage.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drain path is fully clear and water still won't pump out, replace the drain pump.
error_codes.models_affected: GE dishwashers using the C-series fault codes, including models such as GDF630PSMSS, GDT665SSNSS, and GDP630PYRFS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report C2 appearing at the end of a wash with standing water left in the tub, often after a new install where the disposal knockout plug was never removed or the drain hose is kinked. A frequent quick win is clearing the filter and sump or freeing the air gap, after which a Rinse cycle drains normally.

error_codes.meaning: Incoming water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The control detected that the incoming water temperature is below the minimum (about 120°F/49°C) needed for effective washing, so it may extend the cycle to add heat. On some newer GE/Profile/Café models C6 is reported as C7 for the same condition.

causes.description: Cold water sitting in the supply line
Hot water used elsewhere during the cycle

fixes.title: Run the nearby hot tap first
fixes.description: Run the kitchen hot faucet 30–60 seconds before starting to purge cold water from the line.

fixes.title: Raise water heater temperature
fixes.description: Set the water heater to at least 120°F (49°C).

fixes.title: Avoid competing hot-water use
fixes.description: Don't run showers or laundry while the dishwasher fills.

fixes.title: Use a heated/longer option
fixes.description: Select a heated-wash option so the heater adds the needed temperature.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run hot water at the sink first, then restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If incoming water is confirmed hot but C6 persists, the temperature sensor or heater circuit needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Detergent dispenser cup blocked from opening fully
error_codes.detail: The detergent cup could not open completely during the cycle, usually because an item in the lower rack is leaning against it. The wash runs without detergent being released at the right time.

causes.description: Tall item or pan blocking the dispenser door
Silverware or utensil fallen against the cup
Dispenser door spring or wax motor sticking

fixes.title: Remove the obstruction
fixes.description: Take out the silverware, pan, or tall item blocking the dispenser, then close and latch the door.

fixes.title: Wait for the cup to cycle
fixes.description: Close the door and wait ~30 seconds for the cup to open.

fixes.title: Inspect the dispenser door
fixes.description: If nothing blocks it, the door spring or wax motor may be sticking.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the obstruction, latch the door, and wait 30 seconds for the cup to open.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If nothing blocks the cup but C8 persists, replace the detergent dispenser assembly.

error_codes.meaning: Long fill — dishwasher not filling within the expected time
error_codes.detail: The control monitors fill progress and expects the tub to reach a target water level within a set window, typically judged by the flow meter or a timed fill. When that level isn't reached in time, the board halts the cycle rather than running the pump and heater dry, posting LE (or LF). Unlike a leak or overflow code, this fault points to insufficient incoming water — a closed valve, restricted inlet, or weak solenoid rather than drainage or float problems.

causes.description: Supply (saddle) valve closed or partly open
Inlet hose kinked or screen clogged

fixes.title: Open the supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink water valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check the fill hose and screen
fixes.description: Straighten the hose and clean the inlet screen.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is good, test the inlet valve solenoid for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the supply valve and clear any kink, then restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and LE persists, replace the water inlet valve.
error_codes.models_affected: GE built-in dishwashers with electronic fill monitoring, including models such as GDF630PSMSS, GDT665SSNSS, and GDP630PYRFS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after the supply valve was bumped or partly closed during nearby plumbing work, and simply reopening it often clears the fault. It can also appear falsely when household pressure drops temporarily or a fill hose gets kinked behind the unit after it's been moved for cleaning.

error_codes.meaning: Heater circuit failure — no continuity through the heating element
error_codes.detail: The control cannot confirm a working heating-element circuit, so water and dishes won't reach wash/dry temperature. A burned-out element or an open heater relay on the board is the usual cause.

causes.description: Heater wiring or terminal connection failed

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check the element for continuity at its terminals.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing the element.

fixes.title: Inspect heater wiring
fixes.description: Check the terminals and harness to the element for damage or corrosion.

fixes.title: Replace the heating element
fixes.description: Replace if the element reads open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 1 minute to clear the code. If HE returns, test the heating element.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element tests good but HE persists, the control board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — inlet valve not operating correctly
error_codes.detail: The control detected that the water inlet valve is not metering water as expected — either not opening, or flowing when it should be closed. Fills are wrong and the cycle may stall.

causes.description: Valve stuck open (overfill) or stuck closed
Control board inlet valve circuit fault

fixes.title: Check supply and inlet screen
fixes.description: Confirm the supply valve is open and the inlet screen is clear.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Test the solenoid for continuity and check that it closes fully.

fixes.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixes.description: Replace if the valve fails to open/close correctly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 1 minute, confirm the supply valve is open, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new inlet valve doesn't resolve IE, the control board valve circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor issue — freezer not defrosting correctly
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor (which monitors the evaporator temperature during defrost cycles) has failed or is reading incorrectly. This can lead to frost buildup on the evaporator coils and eventual cooling failure.

fixes.title: Defrost the refrigerator manually
fixes.description: Unplug and allow complete defrost (24–48 hours). Check if code clears after full defrost.

fixes.title: Locate and test the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Behind the freezer back panel, on or near the evaporator. Test resistance.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, allow 24 hours for normal operation to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is new and error continues, the defrost heater or defrost timer/board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The 1E code fires when the control board reads the freezer NTC thermistor as out of its valid resistance range — typically an open circuit or a value implying disconnection. The board expects roughly 16k ohms near freezing; a failed sensor, loose connector, or damaged harness pushes the reading beyond the accepted window. Distinct from 2E, which flags the fresh-food sensor, and dS, which relates to defrost. Losing accurate freezer data prevents proper compressor regulation.

fixes.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixes.description: In a 32°F/0°C environment: approx 16k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: The connector is often the issue — unplug and firmly reseat.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install new sensor in same clip location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 5 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both sensors fail simultaneously, the control board or wiring harness has a common fault.
error_codes.models_affected: GE refrigerators with electronic diagnostic control boards, including French-door, side-by-side, and bottom-freezer models such as GNE27JYMFS, GSS25GYPFS, GFE26JYMFS, and PVD28BYNFS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code alongside a freezer that runs too warm or too cold, since the board loses its temperature reference. A loose or corroded sensor connector is a frequent false trigger, and many find that simply reseating the plug before ordering parts clears the fault.

error_codes.meaning: Demo/showroom mode active — cooling is disabled
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has been placed in showroom display mode. All lights and controls work, but the compressor and cooling system are off. Food will spoil in this mode.

causes.description: Demo/display mode activated, often after a power interruption or self-test
Display board or main board communication fault keeping the mode stuck

fixes.title: Exit demo mode
fixes.description: On most GE models: press and hold 'Lock' and 'Ice Maker' simultaneously for 3 seconds. Or hold 'Freezer' and 'Refrigerator' temp buttons for 6 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your model's manual
fixes.description: If the standard combination doesn't work, consult the manual for your specific model number.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Follow the demo mode exit procedure for your model. The display will return to actual temperatures.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If demo mode cannot be exited with standard button combinations, the control board may have a software issue.

error_codes.meaning: Fresh food compartment temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor monitoring the fresh-food compartment is returning an open or out-of-range signal, so the control can't regulate cooling there accurately and the section may run warm or cycle oddly. A failed thermistor or a loose connector is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge and measure the fresh-food sensor — about 6k–8k ohms at room temperature; open confirms failure.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Firmly reconnect the thermistor plug inside the compartment and check for corrosion or a loose pin.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Fit a matching GE thermistor in the fresh-food compartment and verify resistance before closing up.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. If 2E returns after the reset, test and replace the fresh-food thermistor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but 2E keeps returning, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed and the main board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The 4E code fires when the control board reads an open or out-of-range resistance from the evaporator coil thermistor, which it relies on to time defrost cycles. Because the board can no longer trust the coil temperature, it flags the sensor rather than running defrost blindly. A correct sensor reads roughly 16k ohms at 32°F; readings that are infinite or wildly off trigger the fault. This distinguishes 4E from ambient or fresh-food sensor codes (1E, 2E) and from a full defrost failure.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug and let frost clear before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: ~16k ohms at 32°F.

fixes.title: Replace sensor
fixes.description: Clip-on replacement to evaporator coil.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. If 4E returns after the reset, test and replace the evaporator sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost builds up again quickly, defrost heater is failing.
error_codes.models_affected: GE refrigerators with electronic defrost control and clip-on evaporator sensors, including French-door and side-by-side models such as the GNE27JYMFS, GSS25GYPFS, GFE26JYMFS, and PVD28BYNFS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 4E appearing alongside gradual loss of cooling or frost buildup on the back panel, sometimes after a door was left ajar. A loose sensor connector or frost encasing the sensor can trigger it falsely, so many find that unplugging to fully defrost, then reseating the connector, clears the code before any part is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Humidity/ambient sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is getting no valid signal from the humidity or ambient temperature sensor, so it cannot fine-tune defrost cycles and anti-condensation heaters. Cooling usually continues normally. A failed sensor or loose connector is the typical cause.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: Low-priority repair. Check connector first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, then restore power. If 6E reappears within a day, reseat the sensor connector and replace the sensor — cooling is usually unaffected meanwhile.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: 6E rarely causes cooling failure.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer temperature rising — check frozen food for thawing
error_codes.detail: The control detected the freezer compartment warming above its safe range, so frozen food may begin to thaw. It is most often caused by the evaporator fan not circulating cold air, a blocked vent, or a door left ajar.

causes.description: Evaporator (freezer) fan motor failing or iced
Door left open or not sealing
Defrost problem allowing frost over the coil/fan

fixes.title: Confirm the door seals
fixes.description: Make sure the freezer door is fully closed and the gasket seals all around.

fixes.title: Clear the vents
fixes.description: Move food away from the air vents so cold air can circulate.

fixes.title: Listen for the evaporator fan
fixes.description: Open the door and trigger the door switch — the fan should run; a silent or grinding fan is failing.

fixes.title: Manually defrost if iced
fixes.description: If the fan is frosted over, unplug 24 hours with doors open, then restart.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Disconnect power for 1–2 minutes to clear the code; if FF returns, address airflow or the evaporator fan.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the freezer stays warm after defrosting and the fan won't spin freely, replace the evaporator fan motor.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — power to the refrigerator was interrupted
error_codes.detail: PF indicates the refrigerator lost power at some point, so food may have warmed during the outage. It is a notice, not a component fault.

fixes.title: Clear the code
fixes.description: Press any dispenser button (or pad) to clear PF.

fixes.title: Check food safety
fixes.description: Discard anything held above 40°F for more than 2 hours.

fixes.title: Verify cooling resumed
fixes.description: Confirm both compartments are returning to temperature.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press a control pad to clear PF. No repair is needed if cooling resumes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Only call a pro if cooling does not resume after power is restored.

error_codes.meaning: Icemaker fault — check the icemaker for proper operation
error_codes.detail: The control flagged the icemaker as not operating correctly, often a loss of communication with the icemaker module or a stalled fill/harvest. Ice production stops until resolved.

causes.description: Icemaker turned off or out of water
Water supply or fill valve issue

fixes.title: Confirm it's on and supplied
fixes.description: Make sure the icemaker is switched on and the water supply/fill tube is clear.

fixes.title: Clear ice jams
fixes.description: Remove any ice bridging the mold or jamming the harvest arm.

fixes.title: Reseat the harness
fixes.description: Check the icemaker wire harness connector is secure.

fixes.title: Reset the refrigerator
fixes.description: Unplug for 30 seconds, restore power, and watch for a fill cycle.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 30 seconds and confirm the icemaker is on and supplied with water.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If CI persists after reset with confirmed water, replace the icemaker module or check the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost system has not operated properly in the last 24 hours
error_codes.detail: The control has not seen a successful defrost cycle within the past 24 hours, so frost can build on the evaporator and degrade cooling. A failed defrost heater, defrost thermostat, or sensor is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug 24 hours with doors open to clear frost before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test the defrost heater
fixes.description: Check the heater element for continuity.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing.

fixes.title: Test the defrost thermostat
fixes.description: Verify the bimetal closes when cold.

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Replace whichever defrost part tests bad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 1–2 minutes to clear the code; if frost returns or dE reappears, diagnose the defrost circuit.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost rebuilds quickly after defrosting, replace the defrost heater or thermostat.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator temperature incorrect — check the temperature controls
error_codes.detail: The control reports the fresh-food compartment is not holding its set temperature. Causes range from a setting/ventilation problem to a sensor, fan, or sealed-system fault.

causes.description: Temperature set too high or recently changed
Condenser coils dirty / poor ventilation clearance

fixes.title: Check settings and clearance
fixes.description: Confirm the set temperature is correct and there's 2–3 in. of airflow clearance.

fixes.title: Clean the condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum the coils if dusty to restore cooling efficiency.

fixes.title: Reset the control
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours to stabilize.

fixes.title: Have the sealed system checked
fixes.description: If it still won't cool, the compressor/inverter or sealed system needs a pro.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent CC after reset usually requires professional diagnosis of the compressor/inverter or sealed system.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor open — temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control's temperature-monitoring circuit reads an open (infinite resistance) signal from the dryer thermistor, so it has no valid air-temperature feedback and cuts heat to stay safe. A failed thermistor or a broken wire to it is the usual cause.

causes.description: Wiring harness to thermistor broken or chafed

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure thermistor resistance — about 10k–12k ohms at room temp; an open/infinite reading confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before testing.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the thermistor plug, checking for corrosion or a backed-out pin that breaks the circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Located on the blower housing/exhaust duct; fit a matching GE thermistor and verify resistance before reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute to clear the fault, then restart a timed-dry cycle. If E1 returns as the cycle begins, the thermistor reading is still open — replace the sensor before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor measures correctly but E1 keeps returning, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed and the main board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor shorted — exhaust temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The outlet thermistor is returning an abnormally low resistance, which the control reads as a short circuit, so it can no longer trust exhaust-temperature feedback and disables heating. A shorted sensor or a pinched wire grounding the circuit is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure the outlet thermistor — 10k+ ohms at room temp is normal; a near-zero reading confirms a short.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Look for the thermistor lead pinched against the exhaust duct or cabinet sheet metal, shorting the circuit to ground.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Fit a matching GE outlet thermistor in the exhaust path and confirm a healthy resistance reading before running.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute to clear the code, then restart. If E3 returns immediately, the outlet thermistor is still shorted — replace it before running another load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor still shows E3, the short is in the harness or the control board's thermistor circuit — have a technician trace the wiring and test the board.

error_codes.meaning: High-limit thermostat tripped — overheating detected
error_codes.detail: The high-limit safety thermostat has tripped due to excessive heat. A blocked vent is the most common cause.

causes.description: High-limit thermostat failed permanently after trip

fixes.title: Clean vent system
fixes.description: Full duct cleaning — filter, duct, exterior cap.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: After cleaning, test for continuity. Tripped = open = replace.

fixes.title: Run after vent cleaning
fixes.description: If thermostat tests good after vent cleaning, restart normally.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean vent, replace thermostat if failed, unplug 5 minutes, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent is clear and thermostat replaced but E4 returns, heating element relay is stuck closed.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet thermistor failure — intake temperature sensor
error_codes.detail: The air-intake thermistor is returning an open or out-of-range signal, so the control can't monitor incoming air temperature and may cut heat or stop the cycle. A failed inlet thermistor or a broken connection in its harness is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure the inlet thermistor — about 10k ohms at room temperature; open or near-zero confirms failure.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the intake thermistor plug and inspect for corrosion or a loose pin interrupting the signal.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Swap in a matching GE thermistor at the air-intake duct and verify resistance before reassembling.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute, then restart a timed-dry cycle. If E8 returns, the inlet thermistor reading is still out of range — replace the sensor before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new inlet thermistor reads correctly but E8 keeps returning, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed and needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The exhaust air thermistor is returning an open or out-of-range signal, so the control cannot monitor exhaust temperature. The dryer may cut heat or stop mid-cycle to protect itself. A failed thermistor in the exhaust path is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust path.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute and restart. If E9 reappears when the cycle begins, replace the exhaust thermistor — don't keep running the dryer with a failed exhaust sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor relay failure on control board
error_codes.detail: The control board detected that the drum motor relay on the main board is stuck or no longer switching, so the motor cannot be reliably started or stopped. A burned relay contact on the board, often following a power surge, is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Motor relay burned on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: E10 almost always requires board replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If E10 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement for GE dryers is a direct swap. Photograph all wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Lint screen reminder
error_codes.detail: This is a maintenance reminder to check and clean the lint screen. Not an error.

causes.description: Lint screen cleaning reminder interval reached
Lint screen or housing genuinely clogged

fixes.title: Clean lint screen
fixes.description: Remove the screen, clear all lint, and wash with warm soapy water to remove fabric-softener film. Dry fully before reinstalling.

fixes.title: Vacuum the screen housing
fixes.description: Use a vacuum crevice tool in the lint screen slot to pull out lint that has fallen past the screen into the housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean lint screen. LS clears after screen is cleaned.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Stuck key on the control panel
error_codes.detail: The control detected one of the control-panel keys reading as pressed (stuck) for too long, so it cannot return to normal operation. Trapped debris or moisture under a key, or a failing membrane touchpad, is the usual cause.

causes.description: A control-panel key trapped by debris or moisture
Touchpad ribbon or control board fault

fixes.title: Wipe and free the keys
fixes.description: Wipe the control panel with a barely-damp cloth, pressing each key gently to free a stuck one, and dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer for 30 seconds, then plug it back in.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/control panel
fixes.description: If E14 returns, the membrane keypad or control panel has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wipe the panel and work each key to free a stuck button, then unplug for 30 seconds. If E14 persists, replace the touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If cleaning and a power cycle do not clear E14, the membrane touchpad or control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad stuck — a button is continuously pressed
error_codes.detail: A button on the oven's touch control pad has been continuously pressed for over 60 seconds. This is one of the most common GE oven errors.

fixes.title: Clean the keypad
fixes.description: Wipe around all control pad buttons with a barely-damp cloth. Dry completely before restoring power.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. F0 clears on many GE ovens after power cycling.

fixes.title: Replace the control panel/membrane
fixes.description: If F0 returns after cleaning and cycling, the membrane has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Wipe panel clean. If F0 returns, replace touchpad.
error_codes.models_affected: GE Profile, GE Café, and standard GE electric and gas ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F0 persists, the ERC (Electronic Range Control) board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — exceeded 590°F in bake or 990°F in self-clean
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor detected a temperature above the safe maximum. The oven has locked out for safety.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure (reporting falsely high temp)
Heating element relay stuck closed on ERC board

fixes.title: Turn off circuit breaker immediately
fixes.description: Allow oven to cool completely for 1 hour minimum.
fixes.warning: Do NOT open oven door. Stand clear. If you smell burning, evacuate and call the fire department.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor after cooling
fixes.description: GE oven sensors read ~1,080 ohms at room temp. Check for open circuit or short.

fixes.title: Replace sensor if failed
fixes.description: If sensor tests abnormal, replace it.

fixes.title: If sensor tests OK — replace ERC board
fixes.description: The heating relay is stuck closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Do not use oven until cause is identified.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F2 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required. A stuck relay requires ERC board replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings to the control board.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temp, GE oven sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Open circuit (OL/∞) = sensor failed.

fixes.title: Reseat connector
fixes.description: Check and firmly reseat the sensor connector at the sensor and at the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Located in the upper-back of the oven cavity.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely before reaching inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, restore power and test with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: GE, GE Profile, Hotpoint, and RCA electric ranges sharing the GE platform
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and F3 persists, the ERC board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control reads near-zero resistance from the oven temperature sensor (RTD), interpreting it as a short circuit. With no trustworthy temperature, the oven won't heat. A shorted sensor or a pinched harness grounding the circuit is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power to see if the short was a transient fault.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: With power off, measure the RTD — about 1,080 ohms at room temperature; a near-zero reading confirms a shorted sensor.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor wiring
fixes.description: Look for the sensor lead pinched or chafed against the cavity or rear panel, shorting it to ground.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Shorted sensors can't be repaired; fit a matching GE oven temperature sensor and verify resistance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the sensor, restore power at the breaker and run a short bake cycle to confirm F4 has cleared. If it returns immediately, the short is in the harness, not the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor reads correctly and the harness is sound but F4 persists, the ERC board's sensor input circuit is shorted and the board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Touchpad/membrane key button fault
error_codes.detail: A specific key on the control panel touchpad is stuck or has a fault. Similar to F0 but F7 may indicate the fault started from a key other than the first scanned one.

fixes.title: Clean the touchpad
fixes.description: Wipe carefully around all touch buttons.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace touchpad/membrane
fixes.description: If F7 returns after cleaning.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Clean and inspect touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F7 persists, ERC board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic Range Control internal fault
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control board failed an internal self-diagnostic — typically an EEPROM, memory, or logic fault — so it can no longer run cooking functions reliably. A failed board component, often after a power surge or years of heat cycling, is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes to fully reset the ERC board, then restore power and retest.

fixes.title: Check incoming voltage
fixes.description: Verify a stable 240V supply at the range; brownouts and surges can corrupt the board's memory.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F8 returns after a reset, the control board has failed internally and must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the oven's circuit breaker for 15 minutes so the ERC board fully discharges, then restore power. If F8 returns, the board has an internal fault and must be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F8 almost always means the ERC board must be replaced, a job involving high-voltage wiring at the range — professional installation is recommended to avoid shock and miswiring.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure — self-clean lock not completing
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor did not complete the lock or unlock operation within the required time during a self-clean cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check latch for food debris
fixes.description: Food can jam the latch mechanism. Clean around latch area.

fixes.title: Test and replace door latch assembly
fixes.description: Located at the top-rear of the oven.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F9 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch assembly and F9 persists, ERC board door lock relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic control (ERC) failure — loss of element relay backup protection
error_codes.detail: The most common GE oven board fault: the ERC (Electronic Range Control) lost the backup protection that monitors the element relay drivers, or a keypad/control module fault is being read as a board failure. The oven typically locks out and beeps. F1 is frequently triggered after a self-clean cycle.

causes.description: Failure after self-clean overheated the board

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes; F1 clears on some units after a reset.

fixes.title: Disconnect the keypad to isolate
fixes.description: If the keypad detaches from the board, disconnect it and power up — if F1 clears, the keypad is at fault.

fixes.title: Replace the control board (ERC)
fixes.description: If F1 returns with the keypad disconnected, the ERC has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug the oven before servicing the control.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. If F1 returns, the ERC or keypad needs replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: GE, GE Profile, and GE Café electric and gas ranges and wall ovens
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: GE publishes little on F1 — if a power cycle doesn't clear it, replace the control board (ERC); professional install is recommended for high-voltage wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock circuit failure — fault in the self-clean latch circuit
error_codes.detail: The control detected a problem somewhere in the oven door-lock circuit — the latch motor, lock switches, or wiring sending conflicting signals. The door may be stuck locked, often after a self-clean cycle.

causes.description: Door latch motor or lock switch failed
Latch jammed by debris or thermal binding

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker 5 minutes; let the oven cool fully if it just self-cleaned.

fixes.title: Clean around the latch
fixes.description: Clear food debris that can jam the latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test and replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: Test the lock motor/switches; replace the latch assembly if faulty.
fixes.warning: Unplug the oven before accessing the latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle for 5 minutes after the oven cools. If FC returns, service the door-lock circuit.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch and switches test good but FC persists, the ERC door-lock relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Meat probe (temperature probe) circuit fault — open or shorted
error_codes.detail: The control read the temperature/meat probe circuit as out of range (open or shorted). The oven may refuse probe-based cooking until the fault clears.

causes.description: Probe not fully seated in the jack
Probe jack / receptacle corroded or failed

fixes.title: Reseat or remove the probe
fixes.description: Unplug the probe and reinsert it fully, or remove it to run a normal (non-probe) cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the probe and jack
fixes.description: Check the probe and the oven-wall jack for damage or corrosion.

fixes.title: Replace the meat probe
fixes.description: Replace the probe if it tests open/shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Remove the probe and power cycle for 5 minutes; if FD clears, the probe or jack is at fault.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new probe and clean jack don't clear FD, the control board probe circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining within expected time
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the drain phase and expects the water level in the drum to drop within a set time window. If the pressure sensor still detects water after the drain pump has run, E18 (also shown as F18) fires and the cycle pauses with water remaining. Unlike E21, which flags a pump that is drawing abnormal current or is jammed, E18 simply means drainage was too slow — most often from a restricted flow path rather than an electrical pump fault.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: The filter is behind the lower-front service flap. Turn cap slowly to drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — significant water will drain.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Inspect hose for kinks from back of machine to standpipe.

fixes.title: Check standpipe
fixes.description: Pour water directly into standpipe. If it backs up, clear the household drain.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clear and hose is good, pump motor may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press the pause/cancel button to reset, then restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E18 persists, control board drain relay needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers that use the E18/F18 code convention, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E18 appearing at the end of a wash with standing water in the drum, and clearing the drain pump filter behind the lower-front flap resolves it in many cases. A partially kinked drain hose or a slow household standpipe can trigger it intermittently, so it may seem to come and go before a full blockage develops.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — cannot lock door for cycle
error_codes.detail: E02 fires when the control board fails to receive confirmation that the door interlock has engaged within its expected window at cycle start. The board energizes the lock solenoid, then checks the lock switch feedback; if the latch cannot close fully or the switch never confirms, the code triggers and the wash is blocked. This differs from door-open faults or drain-related codes like E04—E02 specifically points to the locking mechanism, latch alignment, or solenoid, not water flow.

fixes.title: Check door seal for caught items
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber gasket seal and inspect for trapped clothing.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch
fixes.description: Look for cracked or worn plastic on door latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test door lock solenoid
fixes.description: Disconnect and test solenoid coil for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and locked. Restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes and clicks but E02 persists, door lock solenoid replacement is needed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E02 appearing right at the start of a cycle, often after overloading, when a sleeve or towel edge is pinched in the gasket. A frequent quick win is reopening the door, clearing any trapped laundry, and firmly pressing it shut until it clicks; if it still won't lock after a clean latch, a worn latch or solenoid is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault — fill time exceeded
error_codes.detail: The control board tracks how long the inlet valve stays open before the pressure sensor confirms the target water level. If that level isn't reached within the allotted fill window, E17 (also shown as F17) fires. It points to restricted or absent incoming water rather than draining problems — that distinguishes it from E18, which flags water being pumped out too slowly or a drain-side fault. Low pressure, closed valves, or clogged inlet screens are the usual triggers.

causes.description: Inlet hose kinked or screen blocked

fixes.title: Open supply valves fully
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are completely open.

fixes.title: Clean hose screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses at machine connection and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity — 200–500 ohms expected.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists after inlet valve replacement, the pressure switch is likely faulty.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers with electronic fill monitoring, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC, plus most similar late-model IQ/Serie machines
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing after the water supply has been shut off for plumbing work or after moving the machine, when a valve was left partly closed or a hose got kinked. A frequent quick win is simply reopening the taps fully and rinsing the inlet mesh screens, which often clears it without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Anti-flood device activated — water detected in base
error_codes.detail: E04 fires when the AquaStop float switch in the base tray detects standing water and triggers a safety shutdown to prevent flooding. A polystyrene float rises with accumulated water and trips a microswitch, cutting the fill valve and stopping the cycle. Unlike drain or fill fault codes that reflect flow timing, E04 specifically indicates physical water pooling in the base, whether from a supply hose fitting, door seal, loose internal hose, or a cracked tub or pump housing.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt machine forward to drain water from base tray. Power off first.
fixes.warning: Turn off water and power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Check hose connections at wall and at machine for any weeping.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or detachment.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hoses
fixes.description: Access back panel and check internal hose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, allow to dry. Code clears when float sensor resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, internal tub or pump housing crack needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers equipped with the AquaStop anti-flood system, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing mid-cycle with the machine refusing to restart until the base is dried. A small amount of residual water or a slightly loose inlet hose can be a false trigger, and many find that snugging the supply fitting and letting the unit dry overnight clears the code.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake timeout — machine failed to fill within the expected time
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the flow meter and pressure sensor during the fill phase. F01 (also shown as E01) fires when the expected water volume is not reached before the fill timer expires — the board sees too little inflow. This points to a supply-side restriction rather than a drain fault. It differs from E17/F17, which flag an aquastop or leak-protection trip, and from drain-timeout codes, which occur when water cannot leave the tub.

causes.description: Water tap closed or partially open
Inlet hose kinked or fill-filter blocked

fixes.title: Open the water tap fully
fixes.description: Confirm the tap behind or beneath the machine is completely open.

fixes.title: Check and straighten the fill hose
fixes.description: Look for kinks along the hose path.

fixes.title: Clean the fill-hose inlet filter
fixes.description: Remove the hose at the machine connection and clean the small mesh filter.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is confirmed, test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the tap fully, clear any kinks, then restart the cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers across multiple series, including models such as the WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and F01 persists, replace the water inlet valve.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F01 appearing at the start of a cycle, often after the tap was left partly closed or the hose got pinched behind the machine during installation or a move. Many find the quick win is simply opening the tap fully or cleaning the small mesh inlet filter, which can be clogged with sediment.

error_codes.meaning: Heating timeout — water did not reach the target temperature
error_codes.detail: F02 fires when the control board monitors the NTC temperature sensor during a heated wash and finds the water is not climbing toward the target temperature within an expected time window. The board interprets this stalled heating as a fault in the heater circuit. Unlike F01 or F03, which point to other sensor or fill-related conditions, F02 specifically flags a heating timeout — most often an open or high-resistance element, damaged heater wiring, or an out-of-range NTC misreporting the actual water temperature.

causes.description: Heating element failed or has high resistance

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check for continuity across the element terminals.
fixes.warning: Unplug the machine before testing the element.

fixes.title: Check the wiring to the heater
fixes.description: Inspect the harness and connectors for damage.

fixes.title: Test the NTC temperature sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature the NTC should read several kΩ.

fixes.title: Replace the heating element
fixes.description: Replace if the element reads open or has very high resistance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 1 minute, restart the cycle. If F02 persists, the heating element needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers with an internal heating circuit, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element replacement is a moderate-to-difficult DIY; incorrect wiring is a fire hazard — professional repair is recommended.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F02 appearing partway through hot or sanitize cycles while cooler washes may finish normally. It can occasionally be a one-off triggered by a loose heater connector, so a power-cycle reset is worth trying first; if the code returns quickly, the heating element or its wiring usually needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain timeout — water did not drain within the expected time
error_codes.detail: F03 (also shown as E03) fires when the control board monitors the drain phase and detects that the water level has not dropped enough within roughly a 10-minute drain window. The board infers a flow restriction rather than a sensor fault, so it halts the cycle to avoid running the pump dry.
