Unlike fill or pressure-sensor codes (E04) or the leak/aquastop family (E18/F18), F03 points specifically at obstructed or failed drainage: a clogged filter, kinked hose, blocked standpipe, or a stalled pump impeller.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or standpipe blocked
Drain pump impeller blocked by debris

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Access the filter at the lower front of the machine; turn slowly to drain water, then remove and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — water will drain out.

fixes.title: Inspect the drain hose
fixes.description: Check for kinks from the back of the machine to the standpipe.

fixes.title: Clear the standpipe
fixes.description: Pour water directly into the standpipe to check for a blocked household drain.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hose are clear, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the drain filter, confirm the hose path, then press Pause/Cancel and restart.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers across the WFB range, including compact models such as the WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If filter and hose are clear and F03 persists, replace the drain pump.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F03 appearing mid-cycle with standing water left in the drum, often after coins, lint, or a stray sock reach the pump filter. Many say a simple filter clean-out at the lower front panel clears it, so that is worth trying before assuming a failed pump; a partially kinked drain hose behind the machine is another frequent, easily missed trigger.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drive motor not running correctly
error_codes.detail: F04 fires when the control board monitors the drive motor and detects it isn't behaving as commanded — failing to start, stalling under load, or turning at the wrong speed based on tachometer feedback. Worn carbon brushes are the most frequent trigger, followed by winding failure, a faulty motor control board, or a loose or corroded motor connector. Unlike F43 or F03, which point to speed-sensing or related drive issues, F04 indicates the motor itself is not running correctly.

fixes.title: Power cycle the machine
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart; intermittent faults can clear.

fixes.title: Check the motor connector
fixes.description: Reseat the motor harness connector.
fixes.warning: Unplug the machine before servicing the motor.

fixes.title: Inspect/replace the carbon brushes
fixes.description: Brushes under 10–15 mm typically need replacement on brush-motor models.

fixes.title: Test the motor windings
fixes.description: Check winding resistance with a multimeter.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes, restart. If F04 persists, inspect the carbon brushes.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers with brush-type (universal) drive motors, primarily older WFB-range machines
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If brushes and winding test good, replace the motor control board or the drive motor.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F04 appearing mid-cycle, often during spin when the motor is under the most load, and the drum may fail to turn. It can sometimes be triggered by a loose motor connector rather than a dead motor, so a quick power cycle and reseating the harness is worth trying first; on higher-mileage machines, replacing worn carbon brushes frequently resolves it.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed — door not shut or door lock not engaging
error_codes.detail: F16 (also shown as E16) fires when the control board fails to confirm the door interlock has fully engaged, so it blocks the cycle for safety before water can enter. The board monitors the door-lock switch circuit; if it never reads a closed-and-locked state, the code sets. Unlike F01 or E02, which point to lock timing or unlocking faults, F16 specifically means the door was never registered as latched — from an ajar door, trapped laundry, or a worn latch tongue.

causes.description: Laundry or object caught in the door gasket
Door lock mechanism worn or failed
Wiring to the door lock faulted

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber gasket and remove any trapped items.

fixes.title: Push the door firmly closed
fixes.description: Push until a definite click is heard.

fixes.title: Inspect the door lock / latch
fixes.description: Look for cracks or wear on the plastic latch tongue.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the door gasket, close the door firmly, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but F16 persists, the door lock solenoid has failed — replace the door lock assembly.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers across most series, including models such as the WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F16 appearing right at cycle start, often after overfilling when a garment edge slips into the gasket and prevents a solid click. The frequent quick win is reopening, tucking laundry fully inside, and pushing until a firm click; if it clicks yet still errors, the latch or lock assembly is usually suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drum not spinning or motor running abnormally
error_codes.detail: The control monitors motor speed feedback (typically via a tachometer or Hall sensor) and fires F21 when the drum fails to reach or hold the commanded speed. This can mean the motor draws current but the drum won't turn, or the controller sees no rotation signal at all. Unlike drain-related codes such as F04 or E18, F21 points squarely at the drive path — a mechanical jam, worn carbon brushes, failed windings, or the motor control electronics.

causes.description: Drum jammed by a foreign object

fixes.title: Check for a foreign object
fixes.description: Unplug the machine and try to turn the drum by hand; resistance indicates a jam.
fixes.warning: Unplug before reaching into the drum.

fixes.title: Inspect carbon brushes
fixes.description: Worn brushes (under 10–15 mm) must be replaced on brush-motor models.

fixes.title: Test motor windings
fixes.description: Measure winding resistance with a multimeter.

fixes.title: Replace the motor or control board
fixes.description: If brushes are fine and windings are bad, replace the motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart; if drum is jammed, clear the obstruction first.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If carbon brushes and motor windings test good, replace the motor control board.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers, including brush-motor models such as the WAT28400UC; note that some newer units like the WGA12400UC use brushless motors where worn brushes do not apply
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F21 appearing at the start of a spin cycle, sometimes after a bulky item or stray object shifts into the drum. A quick win many describe is unplugging, turning the drum by hand to check for jams, and clearing anything caught between drum and tub before assuming a motor failure.

error_codes.meaning: Leak sensor activated — AquaStop or flood-protection sensor triggered
error_codes.detail: F23 fires when the polystyrene float in the base tray rises far enough to trip the flood-protection microswitch, telling the control board water has collected under the tub. The machine halts and typically runs the drain pump continuously to prevent overflow. Unlike drain faults (F03) or fill/valve faults, F23 specifically signals standing water in the base — not a slow drain — so it persists until the tray is emptied and the leak source is corrected.

causes.description: Base-tray float sensor triggered by condensation or spill

fixes.title: Tilt the machine to drain the base
fixes.description: Power off and carefully tilt the machine forward to drain water from the base tray.
fixes.warning: Turn off water and power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect the door seal
fixes.description: Check the rubber boot gasket for tears or detachment.

fixes.title: Check supply hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect both ends of the fill hose for any weeping.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hoses
fixes.description: After the base is dry, access the back and check internal hose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain the base, find and fix the leak source, allow to dry, then reset by restoring power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible after draining and drying, a pump seal or internal fitting is leaking — professional inspection needed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers equipped with AquaStop or base-tray flood protection, including models such as WAT28400UC and WGA12400UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F23 appearing mid-cycle or when starting after the machine has sat unused, and it can be falsely triggered by condensation or a minor spill that pools in the tray. A frequent quick win is tilting the unit to drain the base and letting it dry overnight before restoring power, though a persistent code usually points to an actual weeping hose or seal.

error_codes.meaning: Drum not turning — motor jammed, worn brushes, or motor fault
error_codes.detail: F43 fires when the control board commands drum rotation but the tachogenerator on the motor reports little or no movement, signaling a mismatch between expected and actual drum speed. The board detects the drum failing to spin up within its timing window. Unlike F21 (drum turns erratically or too slowly) or F04/E04 leak-related faults, F43 specifically flags a stalled or non-starting motor — from a jam, worn brushes, a seized winding, or lost tacho feedback preventing speed confirmation.

causes.description: Drum jammed by a foreign object (coin, wire, etc.)
Drive motor seized or winding failure

fixes.title: Check for a drum obstruction
fixes.description: Unplug and rotate drum by hand to feel for binding.
fixes.warning: Unplug before reaching into the machine.

fixes.title: Inspect/replace carbon brushes
fixes.description: Remove and measure brushes; replace if shorter than ~10–15 mm.

fixes.title: Test the motor
fixes.description: Measure winding resistance and tachogenerator output.

fixes.title: Replace motor if failed
fixes.description: Replace the drive motor if the winding is open or shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart; if F43 returns, inspect the carbon brushes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drum, brushes, and motor all check out, the motor control board has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch front-load washers with brushed (carbon-brush) drive motors, including models such as WAT28400UC; note that machines with newer brushless EcoSilence Drive motors will not have brush-related causes
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F43 appearing mid-cycle with a full, unspun load, often after hearing a hum but no drum movement. A frequent false trigger is a small item like a coin or bra wire wedged between drum and tub, so rotating the drum by hand after unplugging is the quick win many report before assuming brush or motor failure.

error_codes.meaning: Leak protection activated — water detected in base tray
error_codes.detail: E15 fires when a polystyrene float in the base tray rises against a microswitch, telling the control board that water has pooled beneath the tub. The board triggers AquaStop, running the drain pump continuously and locking the cycle until the tray is dry and the float drops. Unlike E24/E25, which point to blocked or restricted drainage through the pump and hose, E15 specifically indicates water has escaped the sealed circuit into the base — from a leak, overfill, or even heavy condensation.

causes.description: Overfilled with water — door seal worn or torn
Sump or pump housing crack allowing water into base
Condensation or steam accumulation triggering float sensor
Internal hose connection loose or cracked
Using too much detergent causing foam overflow

fixes.title: Tilt the dishwasher to drain the base
fixes.description: With power off at the breaker, carefully tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees. Water will drain from the base pan. Place towels down. Let it sit tilted for 1–2 minutes.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before tilting. The unit is heavy — get assistance.

fixes.title: Allow the base to dry
fixes.description: After draining, leave the dishwasher upright with the door open for several hours to allow any remaining moisture to evaporate from the base.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Run your fingers along the full perimeter of the rubber door gasket. Look for cracks, tears, or sections that have pulled away from the channel. Replace if damaged.

fixes.title: Check for oversudsing
fixes.description: If you used too much detergent, suds overflow into the base. Use only recommended dishwasher detergent — 1 tablespoon maximum.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hose connections
fixes.description: If the above don't apply, access the inside of the door panel and check the water inlet, circulation, and drain hoses for cracks or loose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Tilt the unit to drain the base, allow it to dry completely, then restart. If the leak has been resolved, the float sensor will reset and the E15 code will clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If E15 recurs after drying and the door seal is intact, there is an internal water leak (cracked sump, hose, or pump) requiring professional inspection.
error_codes.models_affected: Virtually all Bosch dishwashers equipped with the AquaStop anti-flood system, including models such as SHEM63W55N, SHPM65Z55N, SHXM4AY55N, SHEM3AY55N, SHP878ZD5N, and SHX878ZD5N.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E15 appearing suddenly mid-cycle, sometimes with a running pump that won't stop. A frequent false trigger is steam or condensation after a hot cycle rather than an actual leak, so many find that simply tilting to drain and letting the base dry overnight clears it; if it returns quickly, suspect a real leak.

error_codes.meaning: Drain blocked — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: E24 fires when the control board detects that standing water is not clearing within the expected drain cycle time — the pump runs but the water level sensor still reads full. It points specifically to a physical restriction in the drain path (filter, hose, disposal port, or impeller) rather than an electrical pump fault. This distinguishes it from E25, which typically flags a pump or drain-cover issue, and E15, which signals water in the base pan.

causes.description: Clogged drain filter and mesh screen
Garbage disposal connection blocked or knockout not removed
Drain pump impeller blocked by glass or debris

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter system
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew the coarse filter (twist counterclockwise), then lift out the flat fine mesh filter. Rinse both thoroughly under running water using a brush.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Pull the dishwasher out slightly and trace the drain hose. It should loop up to the underside of the counter and not be kinked.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal before starting the dishwasher. If newly installed, check that the knockout plug was removed.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller
fixes.description: If filter is clean and hose is clear, reach into the sump and feel around the pump opening for broken glass or hard debris blocking the impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, press the On/Off button for 3 seconds to reset, then run a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump still doesn't drain (you can hear it trying), the pump motor has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Most Bosch dishwashers that report numeric E-codes, including the SHEM63W55N, SHPM65Z55N, SHXM4AY55N, SHEM3AY55N, SHP878ZD5N, and SHX878ZD5N.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E24 appearing mid-cycle with a pool of water left in the tub, and clearing the coarse and mesh filters often resolves it right away. A frequent false trigger is a newly installed garbage disposal whose knockout plug was never removed, or a high-loop drain hose that got kinked when the unit was pushed back into place.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump or filter blocked — drain impeller obstructed
error_codes.detail: E25 fires when the control board commands the drain pump but detects that water is not leaving the tub, and the fault pattern points to a physical obstruction at the pump or filter rather than the hose. The board typically senses insufficient impeller movement or continued high water level after the pump runs. This distinguishes it from E24, which implicates a kinked or clogged drain hose, and from E15, an anti-flood tray/leak trigger.

causes.description: Hard debris (bone, toothpick, glass) blocking pump impeller
Accumulated food debris in filter basket

fixes.title: Clean filter and remove impeller debris
fixes.description: Follow the same steps as E24. Pay particular attention to clearing the pump impeller area.
fixes.warning: Use gloves when reaching into the pump area.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller rotation
fixes.description: After clearing, reach in and try to spin the pump impeller. It should rotate freely.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump if impeller is damaged
fixes.description: If impeller is broken or pump motor is seized, replace the pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing debris, press On/Off for 3 seconds to reset then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E25 persists, there is a control board drain relay fault.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch dishwashers across most series, including models with a removable filter and self-priming drain pump such as the SHEM63W55N, SHPM65Z55N, SHXM4AY55N, SHEM3AY55N, SHP878ZD5N, and SHX878ZD5N
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E25 mid-cycle with standing water at the tub bottom, often after glass breakage or a large food load. Many find the quick win is simply twisting out and rinsing the filter and fishing debris from the impeller well; false alarms sometimes clear after removing a small item lodged near the pump.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element or NTC sensor failure — water not heating
error_codes.detail: E01 fires when the control board cannot bring water to the programmed temperature within the expected time. The board monitors the NTC thermistor while energizing the flow-through heater; an open heating element, an out-of-range or shorted NTC reading, or a failed heater relay all prevent the sensed temperature from rising. Unlike drainage or fill faults, E01 points specifically at the heating circuit, so cycles may run but dishes stay cold and drying is poor.

causes.description: Heater relay on control board failed

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect element terminals. Should read 15–25 ohms. Open circuit = element failure.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or sensor as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor both test OK, the control board heater relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch dishwashers using a flow-through heating element with an integrated NTC temperature sensor, common across most 300/500/800-series tall-tub models
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E01 appearing mid-cycle alongside dishes that come out wet or cold, and dishwashers that never feel warm inside. Occasionally a loose element terminal or a one-off glitch triggers it, so testing continuity before buying parts is a frequently mentioned quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Heating circuit failure — heater not functioning
error_codes.detail: E09 fires when the control board commands the heating circuit but detects no expected electrical response through the heater path — typically a stuck or failed heater relay on the main PCB, or burned wiring feeding the element. Unlike E01, which points more directly at the heating element or its sensing, E09 flags the switching and control side of the circuit. The board sees the heater failing to energize or draw current, so water and drying stay cold.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on main PCB

fixes.title: Test heating element first
fixes.description: Rule out element failure before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect element wiring for burn marks or melted insulation.

fixes.title: Replace control board or relay
fixes.description: If element is good, the PCB heater control circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart on a short test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is complex. Professional diagnosis recommended to confirm it's the board and not a wiring issue.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch dishwashers with an integrated flow-through heater and PCB-based heater control, including models such as SHEM63W55N, SHPM65Z55N, SHXM4AY55N, SHEM3AY55N, SHP878ZD5N, and SHX878ZD5N
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report cold or spotty dishes, poor drying, and cycles that seem to run but never heat, with E09 appearing mid- or end-cycle. Because a good element can be blamed unfairly, many find it worth ohm-testing the element first; a burned wire or relay is often the real culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Water shortage — insufficient water entering dishwasher
error_codes.detail: During the fill phase the control monitors flow and expected water-level rise via the flow meter and level sensing. When water arrives too slowly or not at all within the timed window, E05 fires and the cycle pauses or aborts to protect the circulation pump from running dry. Unlike leak-related codes such as E15, E05 points upstream to supply restriction — a throttled valve, kinked hose, clogged inlet mesh, or a failed inlet valve solenoid rather than water already inside the tub.

causes.description: Water supply valve under sink closed or partially closed
Inlet mesh filter blocked with scale

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixes.description: Check for kinks from the wall to the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Clean inlet mesh filter
fixes.description: Unscrew the hose from the dishwasher and clean the small mesh filter inside the inlet port.

fixes.title: Test and replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid for continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore water supply and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water supply is confirmed good and error persists, inlet valve replacement is needed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch dishwashers across the 300/500/800 and Benchmark series, including models such as SHEM63W55N, SHPM65Z55N, SHXM4AY55N, SHEM3AY55N, SHP878ZD5N, and SHX878ZD5N
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E05 appearing after plumbing work under the sink or after the dishwasher has been moved, when the supply valve was left partly closed or the hose got pinched. A frequent quick win is simply reopening the shut-off valve fully, and where hard water is an issue, cleaning the small inlet mesh filter often clears it.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — oven not heating
error_codes.detail: E002 fires when the control board commands the bake or broil element on but the temperature sensor detects no rise, or the board reads an open heating circuit. It indicates loss of continuity somewhere between the relay and the element itself — most often a burned-through element, but also a scorched harness connector or a failed control relay. It differs from sensor-fault codes like E111, which flag an out-of-range temperature reading rather than an absence of heat.

causes.description: Heating element burned through (visible break or blistering)
Element wiring harness connection burned or loose

fixes.title: Inspect heating element visually
fixes.description: Open the oven and look at the bake element (bottom) and broil element (top). A burned-through element will have a visible break, hole, or blistered area.

fixes.title: Test element continuity
fixes.description: After allowing oven to cool, disconnect element terminals and test with multimeter. Good element reads 15–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace the element
fixes.description: Unscrew the mounting screws, pull the element forward, disconnect the wires, and connect the new element.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely and disconnect power at the breaker.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, turn power back on and test with a short bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element looks intact and tests with correct resistance but the oven still won't heat, the control board relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch wall ovens and ranges with electric bake/broil elements, including models such as the HBL8453UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this appearing mid-cycle or on the next preheat after a bake element visibly arcs or 'pops,' sometimes with a brief flash or burning smell. A loose or corroded terminal connection can occasionally trigger a false alarm on an otherwise good element, so reseating the wiring before ordering parts is a frequently mentioned quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor failure — NTC sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: E111 fires when the control board reads an open circuit on the NTC oven temperature sensor — effectively an infinite or out-of-range resistance where it expects roughly 1,000–1,100 ohms at room temperature. Without a valid reading, the board cannot close its heat-regulation loop, so it locks out cooking. This distinguishes E111 from short-circuit or drift faults: it points to a broken probe element, an unseated 2-wire connector, or severed wiring rather than a miscalibrated but live sensor.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe burned out or open circuit
Probe connector loose at control board
Wiring from probe to board damaged

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe is usually a small pointed sensor inside the oven cavity. Test resistance at room temperature — should read approximately 1,000–1,100 ohms.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: The probe connects to the control board via a 2-wire connector. Ensure it is firmly seated.

fixes.title: Replace the probe
fixes.description: Unscrew from the oven back wall and replace with a new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the probe, run the oven on a short bake cycle. The error should not return.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the probe is new and tests correctly but E111 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch wall ovens and ranges with an NTC cavity temperature sensor, including the HBL8453UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E111 appearing at power-up or partway through a bake cycle, sometimes after a self-clean run stresses the probe. A loose or partially unseated sensor connector is a frequent false trigger, and many find reseating it clears the code before any part is ordered.

error_codes.meaning: Safety cutout tripped — oven overheated
error_codes.detail: E132 fires when the oven's thermal safety cutout detects an internal temperature above the maximum safe threshold, independent of the setpoint. The control board typically triggers this when the temperature probe misreads (or fails), a heating element stays energized, or the cooling fan can't shed heat—so the cavity keeps climbing. Unlike a probe open-circuit fault (E111), E132 confirms real overheating occurred and the hardware safety interlock intervened, which is why it is treated as a critical, non-resettable safety event.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing control board to keep heating
Cooling fan failure causing heat buildup

fixes.title: Do not use the oven
fixes.description: Stop all oven use until this is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: SAFETY CRITICAL — do not operate the oven until diagnosed.

fixes.title: Allow oven to cool completely
fixes.description: Wait at least 2 hours before any inspection.

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: A failed probe can cause the control board to keep heating without limit. Test resistance (1,000–1,100 ohms at room temp).

fixes.title: Check the cooling fan
fixes.description: Ovens have a cooling fan. Listen for it running when the oven is on. No sound = failed fan.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. This code requires diagnosing the underlying overheating cause before operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E132 is a safety event requiring professional service before further oven use. The root cause must be identified to prevent recurrence.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch wall ovens and range ovens with electronic temperature control and a thermal safety cutout, including models such as the HBL8453UC
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E132 appearing during or shortly after high-heat cooking, self-clean cycles, or preheat, often with the oven feeling unusually hot outside. Occasionally it can be triggered by a stalled or noisy cooling fan rather than an element fault, so listening for the fan is a useful first check—though the code should still be professionally diagnosed before further use.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply — insufficient fill detected
error_codes.detail: KitchenAid washers share the Whirlpool platform. F8E1 indicates the washer is not detecting adequate water fill within the timeout period. Supply valve, inlet screens, and water pressure are the first things to check.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves behind washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply issue, press and hold Start for 3 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and valve is new, flow meter needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: F9E1 means the KitchenAid washer ran its drain step but couldn't empty the tub within the allowed time, so standing water remains and the cycle pauses. A clogged drain pump filter is almost always the culprit and clears without any parts.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Open the lower-front access panel, set out towels and a pan, then slowly unscrew the filter cap to drain water and clear debris.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose isn't kinked, keeps a proper high loop, and isn't pushed too far into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain the hose empties into isn't clogged or backing up.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hoses are clear, test the pump motor for continuity and listen for it running during drain.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the blockage, reseat the filter cap fully, then select Drain & Spin and press Start — F9E1 clears once the washer empties the tub within the expected time.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter, hoses, and household drain are all clear but F9E1 returns, the drain pump or the control board's pump relay has likely failed and a technician should test both.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure
error_codes.detail: F5E2 means the washer's door lock could not engage or disengage within the allowed time, so the cycle won't start or stops mid-wash — the machine never runs unlocked. A worn latch, laundry caught in the door seal, or a failed lock solenoid is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Look for laundry, coins, or debris caught in the door gasket that stops the door from closing fully against the latch.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Examine the latch hook and strike for cracked plastic or wear, and confirm the door isn't sagging out of alignment.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 1 minute, then restore power to clear a transient lock-timeout fault.

fixes.title: Test the lock solenoid
fixes.description: Remove the door lock assembly and test the solenoid for continuity; replace the assembly if it's open or won't engage.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear anything caught in the gasket, close the door firmly so the latch seats, then unplug the washer for 1 minute and restore power. Press Start — F5E2 clears once the door locks.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes cleanly and a new lock assembly is installed but F5E2 persists, the lock output circuit on the main control board has failed and the board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected excessive foam in the drum and paused the cycle to let the suds break down — it may add rinse water to help. The cycle runs much longer than normal but resumes on its own. Using too much detergent, or non-HE detergent in an HE machine, is the cause in nearly every case.

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: Washer will resume automatically within 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only; max 1 tablespoon per load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clears automatically. No reset needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a mechanical fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: Fault code 7-1 means the dishwasher couldn't drain within the allowed time, leaving standing water in the tub. A clogged filter or drain hose, a jammed pump impeller, or a failed drain pump motor are the causes — most are clearable without parts.

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the filter assembly under the bottom rack and rinse out food debris and grease that block drainage.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose routing
fixes.description: Confirm the drain hose has a high loop or air gap and isn't kinked or clogged where it meets the disposal or standpipe.

fixes.title: Clear the pump impeller
fixes.description: With the filter out, check the drain pump opening for glass, seeds, or debris jamming the impeller and remove it.
fixes.warning: Wear gloves — broken glass is common here.

fixes.title: Replace the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter, hose, and impeller are clear but the pump won't run, replace the drain pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the filter, hose, and pump impeller, then cancel the cycle by holding the cycle button until the lights reset and start a fresh cycle — 7-1 clears once the tub drains fully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter, hose, and impeller are all clear but the dishwasher still won't drain, the drain pump motor or the control board's pump relay has failed and a technician should test both.

error_codes.meaning: Stuck relay on control board
error_codes.detail: Fault code 6-2 means a relay on the main control board is stuck closed or open, so the board can no longer switch a load — heater, pump, or valve — on and off correctly. This is an internal board fault, usually from a voltage surge or moisture, and normally requires replacing the control board.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Switch off the breaker for 15 minutes (most units are hardwired), then restore power — a stuck relay occasionally resets.

fixes.title: Inspect for moisture or surge damage
fixes.description: With power off, check the control board for water spotting, corrosion, or scorched relay contacts.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If 6-2 returns after a power cycle, the stuck relay can't be repaired and the control board needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off the dishwasher's breaker for 15 minutes to fully de-energize the board, then restore power. If 6-2 returns, the stuck relay cannot be reset and the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Because 6-2 is a hardware fault on the control board and most KitchenAid dishwashers are hardwired, replacing the board safely involves mains wiring — call a technician unless you're comfortable with that.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — water temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: Fault code 1-1 means the NTC thermistor that measures wash-water temperature is reading open or out of range, so the control disables the heating function and cycles run cold or stall. A failed thermistor or a loose connector at the sensor is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Switch off the dishwasher's breaker for 60 seconds (most are hardwired), then restore power to clear a transient sensor fault.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: With power off, measure the thermistor resistance — about 6k ohms at room temperature; an open or far-off reading confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Swap the thermistor, mounted in the sump, if it reads open or out of range — an inexpensive bolt-in sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power at the breaker for 60 seconds (most KitchenAid dishwashers are hardwired), then restore power. After replacing the thermistor, run a normal cycle and verify the water heats without 1-1 returning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but 1-1 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit or the wiring harness to the thermistor has failed and should be traced by a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: SY EF means the control lost speed feedback from the freezer evaporator fan, so cold air stops circulating to the refrigerator and freezer compartments. A failed fan motor or ice jamming the blade is the usual cause; temperatures can climb quickly if the fan stays off.

fixes.title: Defrost the freezer
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator and fully defrost the freezer to melt ice that may be stopping the evaporator fan blade.

fixes.title: Inspect the fan area
fixes.description: Remove the freezer rear panel and check the fan blade for frost, broken plastic, or debris before replacing parts.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to the evaporator fan motor; if it doesn't spin smoothly, the motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: Access the motor behind the freezer back panel and swap it if it won't run or the bearings are noisy.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing ice or replacing the fan motor, plug the refrigerator back in and allow up to 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. SY EF clears once the control detects normal evaporator fan feedback.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If SY EF returns after the fan motor is replaced, or ice builds back around the fan within a few days, the defrost heater, defrost sensor, or control board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: SY CF means the control lost feedback from the condenser fan at the back or bottom of the refrigerator, so the compressor cannot shed heat and may overheat. Dust-packed coils, debris blocking the blade, or a failed condenser fan motor are the normal causes.

fixes.title: Clean the condenser area
fixes.description: Pull the toe grille or rear cover and vacuum dust from the fan, coils, and air passages so the blade can move air freely.

fixes.title: Check the fan blade
fixes.description: Spin the condenser fan by hand; it should turn freely without scraping, wobbling, or hitting debris.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to the condenser fan motor; if it won't spin or starts only intermittently, the motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the motor
fixes.description: Remove the rear or bottom access panel and swap the condenser fan motor assembly if it fails testing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning the fan area or replacing the motor, plug the refrigerator back in and allow several hours for temperature recovery. SY CF clears once the control sees normal condenser fan operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan area is clean and the blade spins freely but SY CF persists, the condenser fan motor, its wiring, or the main control board output needs technician testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Frigidaire washer didn't pump the water out within the programmed drain time, so it halts to avoid a flood and leaves the tub full. A clogged pump filter, a kinked or blocked drain hose, or a worn drain pump are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Open the lower front access panel, drain into a tray, then unscrew and rinse the pump filter of lint and debris.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Make sure the hose runs in a high loop, isn't kinked, and the standpipe or sink drain isn't blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: With the filter clear, check the pump motor for continuity and listen for it humming without pumping.

fixes.title: Replace the drain pump
fixes.description: If the pump fails its continuity test or is seized, fit a new drain pump and clear any debris from the volute.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage or replacing the pump, run a Drain/Spin cycle to confirm the water pumps out and E21 clears. If it returns, recheck the filter and hose for trapped debris.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear and a new pump still won't drain, the control board's pump relay or the wiring to the pump has failed — have a technician test the board.

error_codes.meaning: Fill time exceeded — water not filling
error_codes.detail: The pressure sensor did not register the target water level before the fill timer expired, so the control paused the cycle to avoid running dry. A closed or partially open supply valve, or sediment-clogged inlet screens, are the most common causes.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Check both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh filters.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Door open — lid or door not properly closed
error_codes.detail: The control can't confirm the door is closed and locked, so the cycle won't start or pauses mid-wash. A door not pushed fully shut, a worn or broken latch hook, or a failed lock solenoid that never reports 'locked' are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Check for obstructions
fixes.description: Look for clothing or the door gasket caught between the door and frame stopping it from latching.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door until you hear a definite click so the latch fully engages the strike.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch
fixes.description: Check the latch hook for wear or cracks that stop it seating; replace the latch assembly if damaged.

fixes.title: Test the lock solenoid
fixes.description: With power off, check the door lock solenoid for continuity; an open coil won't engage and report locked.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly until it clicks and restart the cycle. If E41 returns, unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then inspect the latch and test the door lock solenoid before running a load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes properly and the latch is sound but E41 persists, the door lock solenoid or the control board's lock circuit has failed and needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock control board failure
error_codes.detail: E45 fires when the main control board's communication with the door lock circuit board fails or returns invalid data, meaning the board cannot verify or command the lock state. Unlike E41 (door lock not detected) or E42 (door remains unlocked), E45 points to the lock control electronics or its wiring rather than a stuck latch or open door. The board detects the fault before the wash cycle can safely proceed and halts operation.

causes.description: Main control board door lock relay

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test door lock assembly
fixes.description: If lock tests failed, replace assembly.

fixes.title: Replace control board if needed
fixes.description: If door lock tests OK, main board door lock relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If error returns, door lock or board replacement needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both door lock and board are replaced and error continues, wiring harness has a fault.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire front-load washers with an electronic door lock circuit, common on models with E4x fault-code series
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E45 appearing at the start of a cycle when the door won't lock or the machine refuses to run. A power cycle sometimes clears it temporarily if the trigger was a transient glitch, but a returning code usually points to the lock assembly or a chafed wiring harness connector.

error_codes.meaning: Water leak in tub or air leak in the pressure-sensor air bell
error_codes.detail: The control sensed a water leak in the outer tub, or an air leak in the air bell/hose that feeds the water-level pressure sensor, so it can no longer trust the level reading. A cracked tub, leaking hose, or a split air dome are typical causes.

causes.description: Air leak in the pressure-sensor hose or air bell
Door boot/gasket or hose connection leaking

fixes.title: Inspect for water under the tub
fixes.description: Look for drips from the tub, boot, and hose connections.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before reaching inside the cabinet.

fixes.title: Check the pressure-sensor air hose
fixes.description: The hose from the sump air bell to the sensor must be intact and tight with no air leaks.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor if needed
fixes.description: If the hose is good, the sensor may be faulty.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the leak, dry the base and run a test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no leak is visible and the hose is tight, replace the pressure sensor; a tub crack needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump relay failure on the control board
error_codes.detail: The drain pump relay on the main control board has failed, or the wire to the drain pump has come off, so the control cannot drive the pump. Water stays in the tub.

causes.description: Drain pump relay on control board failed
Wire to the drain pump disconnected or broken

fixes.title: Check the pump wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the connector and wires at the drain pump for a loose or broken connection.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: Verify the pump motor for continuity if wiring is intact.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the pump and wiring are good, the board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reconnect any loose pump wire, then restart. If E23 persists, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump and its wiring test good, the drain pump relay on the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door will not unlock at the end of the cycle
error_codes.detail: The control could not release the door lock when the cycle ended. It is most often caused by water still in the tub (the safety logic keeps the door locked until drained), or by a sticking lock mechanism.

causes.description: Water not fully drained, keeping the door locked
Door lock mechanism sticking / not releasing
Clogged drain filter or weak drain pump
Control board not reading the lock switch correctly

fixes.title: Confirm the tub is empty
fixes.description: Run Drain/Spin to force a drain; the door stays locked while water remains.

fixes.title: Power reset
fixes.description: Press Cancel, unplug for 2 minutes, restore power, and wait 30–60 seconds for the door to release.

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Clear the coin trap/drain filter if drainage is weak.
fixes.warning: Place towels down; water will spill out.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixes.description: If the tub is empty and reset fails, the lock is sticking.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain the tub, press Cancel, then unplug 2 minutes; the door should release on power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the tub is empty and a reset doesn't release the door, replace the door lock assembly.

error_codes.meaning: Control board communications fault
error_codes.detail: The main control board reported an internal communications problem and could not coordinate the cycle. This points to the board itself rather than an external component.

causes.description: Main control board internal communications failure
Power surge or moisture damage to the board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: Check that the harness connectors on the control board are fully seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If E43 returns, the control board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If E43 returns, the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E43 is a board-internal fault; if a reset and reseated connectors don't clear it, replace the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Control board communications fault
error_codes.detail: The control board logged a communications fault very similar to E43 — an internal board problem preventing it from managing the cycle. The board is the usual replacement.

causes.description: Main control board internal communications failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat connectors
fixes.description: Confirm the control board harness connectors are seated firmly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If E44 persists, replace the control board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If E44 returns, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Like E43, E44 is a board-internal fault — replace the control board if a reset doesn't clear it.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock PTC circuit reads open during spin
error_codes.detail: During the spin phase the control read the door-lock PTC heater circuit as open, so it could not confirm the door is locked and halted to protect against opening at speed. A failing lock assembly or its wiring is the usual cause.

causes.description: Wiring/connector to the door lock open

fixes.title: Power reset
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes and restart a spin to see if E47 clears.

fixes.title: Check the lock wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness between the door lock and the control board for an open connection.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixes.description: If wiring is good, the PTC lock has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 2 minutes and restart. If E47 returns during spin, service the door lock circuit.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door lock and wiring test good, the control board door-lock circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Bad signal from the motor tachogenerator
error_codes.detail: The control is receiving an invalid or missing speed signal from the motor's tachogenerator (tach), so it cannot regulate drum speed. The motor may stall, run erratically, or not spin.

causes.description: Motor control board tach input fault

fixes.title: Check the motor connector
fixes.description: Reseat the motor/tach harness connector and inspect for corrosion.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before servicing the motor.

fixes.title: Test the tach coil
fixes.description: Measure the tachogenerator coil resistance at the motor.

fixes.title: Replace the motor if faulty
fixes.description: Replace the drive motor if the tach coil is open/shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug, reseat the motor connector, and restart. If E52 persists, test the tach and motor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the tach signal stays bad after reseating the connector, replace the drive motor or motor control board.

error_codes.meaning: High temperature on the motor speed-control (inverter) heat sink
error_codes.detail: The motor speed-control board's heat sink overheated, usually from overloading or repeated high-current spin attempts, and the control shut down to protect itself.

causes.description: Inadequate ventilation around the control board
High motor current from a binding drum

fixes.title: Let it cool and reduce the load
fixes.description: Pause, remove some laundry, and let the washer cool before restarting.

fixes.title: Check for a binding drum
fixes.description: Spin the drum by hand for drag or a foreign object behind it.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Replace the speed-control board
fixes.description: If E5C recurs without overloading, the inverter/speed-control board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Let the washer cool ~30 minutes, lighten the load, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If E5C returns with normal loads, replace the motor speed-control (inverter) board.

error_codes.meaning: Communication problem between the main and motor control boards
error_codes.detail: The main control board and the motor speed-control board lost reliable communication, so cycle commands aren't reaching the motor. A harness fault or a failed board is the usual cause.

causes.description: Harness between main board and motor control board faulted

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Check the connectors between the main board and motor control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If E5E persists, replace the motor control board, then the main board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and reseat the board connectors. If E5E returns, a board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating the harness doesn't clear E5E, replace the motor control board (then the main control board).

error_codes.meaning: Communication / configuration fault between control boards
error_codes.detail: The main control board could not establish proper communication or configuration with another board (door-lock or motor control), so the cycle won't proceed. A loose harness or a failed board is the usual cause.

causes.description: Door lock or motor control board not recognized

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: Check all harness connectors between the boards and the door lock.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If E90 persists, the main control board is the likely failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and reseat connectors. If E90 returns, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating connectors doesn't clear E90, replace the main control board.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The dryer's electronic control board (main PCB) failed an internal self-check, so it can no longer reliably sequence the motor, heater, and sensors and the dryer may go dead or stop mid-cycle. A failed board component, often from a power surge or moisture, is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer for 15 minutes to fully discharge the board, then restore power and start a timed-dry cycle.

fixes.title: Check the power supply
fixes.description: Verify a stable 240V supply and tight connections at the outlet and terminal block; surges corrupt the board.

fixes.title: Inspect for moisture
fixes.description: Look for condensation or water tracks on the board in damp laundry rooms that can short its circuits.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If E11 returns after a reset, the main board has failed and must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer and photograph all wiring first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 15 minutes so the control board fully discharges, then restore power and run a timed-dry cycle. If E11 returns, the board has an internal fault and needs replacing.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a power cycle doesn't clear E11 and the supply voltage is good, the control board has failed internally — replacement is straightforward but requires photographing every wire position to avoid miswiring.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure
error_codes.detail: The control detected an open circuit in the heating element, so the drum turns but no heat is produced and clothes stay wet. An element coil burned through from age or from restricted exhaust airflow is by far the most common root cause.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect wires, test continuity. Should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access element from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

fixes.title: Check board relay
fixes.description: If element OK, board heater relay failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, control board relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain problem — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher couldn't pump out the wash water within the allowed time, so it stops with standing water in the tub. A clogged filter, a kinked or air-locked drain hose, a blocked disposal knockout, or a worn drain pump are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the cup-shaped filter in the tub floor and rinse out food debris and grease that block flow.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose has a high loop and no kinks, and isn't clogged where it meets the disposal or standpipe.

fixes.title: Clear the disposal knockout
fixes.description: If newly connected to a disposal, make sure the plastic knockout plug was removed and the disposal is clear.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: With the filter and hose clear, check the drain pump for continuity and listen for it running without moving water.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the filter, hose, or disposal, cancel the cycle (hold Cancel/Start to drain), then start a short cycle to confirm the water drains and i20 clears.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter, hose, and disposal are all clear but i20 persists, the drain pump has failed or the control board's pump circuit is faulty — have a technician test and replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected in base
error_codes.detail: Water has reached the base pan and tripped the anti-flood float switch, so the dishwasher stops and runs its drain pump continuously to protect against a leak. A torn door seal, oversudsing, a loose internal hose, or a cracked tub are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Drain the base pan
fixes.description: With power off, tip the dishwasher forward over a tray to drain the trapped water and reset the float.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect the door seal
fixes.description: Check the door gasket for tears or gaps that let water escape down the front into the base.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal hose fittings and the pump/sump connections for drips or loose clamps.

fixes.title: Cut detergent and rinse aid
fixes.description: Oversudsing overflows the tub — use only dishwasher detergent, about 1 tbsp, and check rinse-aid dosing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain the base pan, fix the leak source, and dry the float area, then restore power and start a cycle. i30 clears once the float drops and no new water reaches the base.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the base is dry and no seal, hose, or sudsing problem is found but i30 returns, there is a hidden leak or a cracked tub that needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply problem — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: The control did not detect the expected water level within the fill window, so the cycle pauses rather than run the wash pump dry. Dishes come out dirty or the cycle stalls early. A closed supply valve or kinked inlet hose is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is open.

fixes.title: Check hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Frigidaire dishwashers are often hardwired — switch off the breaker for 5 minutes to reset the control, restore power, then start a new cycle after confirming the supply valve is fully open.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted
error_codes.detail: PF signals that the control board lost power partway through a cycle and then regained it. On restoring power, the board sets PF as a memory flag so you know the last cycle may not have completed rather than reporting a hardware fault. Unlike component error codes tied to a specific sensor, valve, or motor, PF points only to the incoming supply — a momentary outage, a tripped breaker, or a loose junction-box connection — and clears once acknowledged.

fixes.title: Clear the code and restart
fixes.description: Press Start/Cancel to clear PF, then start a new cycle to finish the interrupted load.

fixes.title: Check household power if recurring
fixes.description: If PF appears often without known outages, check the breaker and the dishwasher's junction-box connections for looseness.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire dishwashers with a digital control display, including models such as FFCD2418US, FGID2466QF, and FFBD2412SS
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PF appearing after a storm, a brief blackout, or a breaker trip, and it usually clears with a simple Start/Cancel press and a fresh cycle. If it keeps showing up with no known outages, a loose wire nut at the junction box or a marginal breaker is a frequent culprit worth checking.

error_codes.meaning: Clogged filter or sump — restricted water circulation
error_codes.detail: Food soils have built up in the filter/sump area to the point where water cannot circulate properly. The dishwasher stops the cycle and displays i40. Cleaning the glass trap and filter assembly is the first step.

causes.description: Food-soil buildup in the glass trap / sump filter
Wash pump impeller clogged with debris

fixes.title: Clean the glass trap and filter
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack and clean the filter/glass trap assembly per the owner's guide.

fixes.title: Clear the sump area
fixes.description: Inspect and remove any food, labels, or debris from the sump.

fixes.title: Re-run the unit
fixes.description: Reinstall the filter and run a normal cycle to confirm the code clears.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filter and sump, then press Start/Cancel and restart a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If i40 returns after cleaning, the wash pump impeller may be clogged and needs professional cleaning or replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor / circulation pump and heater assembly fault
error_codes.detail: A fault was detected in the circulation pump, which is the assembly that both drives wash spray and heats water. The dishwasher cannot wash or dry effectively. Could also indicate a drain-motor fault on some models.

fixes.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixes.description: Switch off the breaker for 30 seconds and restart. An intermittent fault may clear.

fixes.title: Check electrical connections
fixes.description: Inspect the harness connector at the circulation pump for looseness or corrosion.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before accessing the pump.

fixes.title: Test the motor windings
fixes.description: Measure resistance across the pump motor terminals.

fixes.title: Replace the circulation pump assembly
fixes.description: If the motor tests bad or is seized, replace the pump/heater assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off the breaker for 30 seconds; if i50 returns, service the circulation pump.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump tests good but i50 persists, the control board pump relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water heating failure — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher could not heat the water to the required wash temperature within the cycle time. This may cause poor wash performance and prevent the heated-dry phase from working.

causes.description: Incoming water too cold for the heater to compensate

fixes.title: Run hot water at sink first
fixes.description: Pre-flush the supply line so the dishwasher starts with hot water.

fixes.title: Check water heater temp
fixes.description: Set the home water heater to at least 120°F (49°C).

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check for continuity; a failed element reads open.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing.

fixes.title: Replace the element if failed
fixes.description: Replace the heating element if it tests open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off the breaker for 30 seconds, run hot water at the sink, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element tests good but i60 persists, test the NTC temperature sensor and, if that's good, the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — touchpad/control not talking to main board
error_codes.detail: The electronic control system has lost reliable communication between the user-interface (touchpad) board and the main control board. The dishwasher can't coordinate the cycle without both boards exchanging data, so it stops. A loose or corroded ribbon/harness between the two boards is the most common — and most fixable — cause.

causes.description: Loose or unseated ribbon/harness between UI and main board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off the dishwasher's breaker (or unplug) for 5 minutes to clear a transient glitch.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Open the door panel and firmly reseat the ribbon/harness between the touchpad and the main control board; check for corrosion.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the door panel.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, the UI (touchpad) or main control board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power for 5 minutes and reseat the harness between the touchpad and control board before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating the harness doesn't resolve iC0, the touchpad or main control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — refrigerator or freezer above safe temperature
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected that compartment temperature is above the safe threshold. Food safety may be compromised if this has persisted for more than 2 hours.

causes.description: Door left open or not sealing properly

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Press paper between door and frame. If it pulls out easily, the seal is worn.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum the condenser coils behind or under the unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Listen for both the condenser fan (back/bottom) and evaporator fan (inside freezer) running.

fixes.title: Allow time to recover
fixes.description: If door was accidentally left open, close it and wait 4 hours.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly. H1 clears when temperature returns to normal range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans are running, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, the compressor or refrigerant needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The control isn't getting a valid running signal from the evaporator fan motor in the freezer, so cold air can't circulate to the fresh-food and freezer sections and temperatures climb. A seized fan bearing, ice jamming the blade, or a broken wire are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge and fully defrost the freezer — frost from a defrost-system fault commonly jams the evaporator fan.

fixes.title: Inspect the fan and wiring
fixes.description: Behind the freezer rear panel, spin the fan by hand and check its connector for a broken or loose wire.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to the motor leads; if it won't spin freely or draws no current, the motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: Fit a new evaporator fan motor behind the freezer back panel and confirm it spins on power-up.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing ice or replacing the fan, unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize before judging whether SY EF has cleared.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new evaporator fan runs but ice quickly returns and jams it again, the defrost heater or defrost thermostat is failing and a technician should diagnose the sealed defrost system.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error between control boards
error_codes.detail: SY CE means the refrigerator's main control board has lost communication with the user-interface (touchpad/display) board. The control system can't coordinate cooling and display functions without the two boards exchanging data. A loose or corroded wiring harness between the boards is the most common cause, followed by a failed UI or main board.

causes.description: Loose or corroded harness between UI and main board
Wiring damage in the door or cabinet routing

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, then restore power — a transient communication glitch often clears.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Access the UI board (usually in the dispenser/door or top control housing) and reseat the harness to the main board; check for corrosion or pinched wires.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before opening control housings.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, replace the user-interface board or the main control board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and reseat the harness between the UI and main control boards before restoring power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If power cycling and reseating the harness don't clear SY CE, the user-interface or main control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Runaway temperature — oven overheating
error_codes.detail: F10 fires when the control board reads oven cavity temperature climbing beyond the safe setpoint, indicating heat it can no longer regulate. The board compares the RTD probe's resistance against expected values; an open or drifting probe reads as low temperature, so the board keeps calling for heat, or a welded relay keeps the element energized regardless of the probe. Unlike F30/F11 probe-circuit faults that flag a sensor reading fault, F10 signals actual runaway heating and demands the oven be shut down.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing uncontrolled heating

fixes.title: Stop oven use immediately
fixes.description: Do not use the oven until the fault is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — stop oven use immediately.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe should read ~1,100 ohms at room temperature. Out of spec = replace.

fixes.title: Replace control board if probe is OK
fixes.description: If probe tests fine, the board's temperature control relay has stuck.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. Diagnose the underlying cause first.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F10 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required before returning the oven to service.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire electric and gas ranges/wall ovens using the standard F-series fault codes, including models such as FCRE3052AS and FGGH3047VF
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F10 appearing mid-bake or during preheat, sometimes with the oven noticeably hotter than the set temperature. A probe that has drifted out of spec is a frequent culprit, and some report the code clearing after a probe replacement; however, because a stuck relay can keep heating even when powered off at the panel, cutting power at the breaker until diagnosed is the safest quick step.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature probe failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Oven Control reads an open circuit on the oven temperature probe (RTD), so it has no valid cavity temperature and won't run bake or broil safely. A burned-out probe or a loose/broken connection at the board is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the oven
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power to rule out a transient fault.

fixes.title: Test the probe resistance
fixes.description: With power off, measure the probe — about 1,100 ohms at room temperature; an open/infinite reading confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing.

fixes.title: Check the probe connector
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat the probe plug at the rear of the cavity and at the control board for a loose or burned pin.

fixes.title: Replace the probe
fixes.description: Unscrew the probe from the oven back wall and fit a matching Frigidaire probe, verifying resistance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the probe, restore power at the breaker and run a short bake cycle to confirm F30 has cleared and the oven holds temperature. If it returns, recheck the connector.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new probe tests correctly and the connector is sound but F30 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed and the board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted key — button stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: F11 fires when the electronic oven control (EOC) reads a keypad input as continuously closed, as if a button is held down. The board scans the membrane keypad's row/column matrix; a shorted or stuck line makes it register a permanent press, locking out normal commands. This differs from F10, which signals a runaway or over-temperature condition sensed at the temperature probe rather than a keypad fault. The trouble lies in the keypad membrane or its ribbon connection to the control board.

causes.description: Stuck button from grease or moisture

fixes.title: Clean control panel
fixes.description: Clean around all buttons with dry cloth. Check for sticky residue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace keypad
fixes.description: If a button remains stuck, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F11 returns on the same key, that key is shorted.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If keypad replacement doesn't resolve F11, the control board has a shorted keypad input.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire electric and gas ranges/wall ovens with membrane keypad control panels; the code can appear across the line, including models such as FCRE3052AS and FGGH3047VF
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F11 appearing after spills, steam, or grease build-up near the touchpad, and a simple wipe-down plus a 10-minute power cycle sometimes clears it. If the same key keeps triggering the code after cleaning, most find the membrane keypad itself has failed and needs replacing.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature probe short circuit
error_codes.detail: F31 fires when the control board reads near-zero resistance on the oven temperature probe (RTD) circuit, the opposite of the open-circuit condition that triggers F30. A healthy probe reads roughly 1,100 ohms at room temperature; a short makes the board interpret an impossibly high temperature, so it locks out heating for safety. The short can originate inside the probe itself, in wiring pinched against the metal cavity, or at the board's sensor input.

causes.description: Wiring pinched causing short to chassis

fixes.title: Test the probe
fixes.description: Disconnect the probe from the control board. At room temperature, it should read approximately 1,100 ohms. Near 0 ohms = shorted probe.

fixes.title: Inspect probe wiring
fixes.description: Look for pinch points where the wiring passes through the oven cavity. Even slight pressure against the chassis can cause a short.

fixes.title: Replace the temperature probe
fixes.description: A shorted probe cannot be repaired. The probe threads into the upper-rear oven wall — typically 1–2 screws. Pull 2–4 inches of wire slack through and connect the new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing probe, run a short 350°F bake cycle to confirm F31 is resolved.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire electric and gas ovens/ranges with a resistance-based temperature probe, including models such as FCRE3052AS and FGGH3047VF
