fixes.description: Some Broan models have a small glass fuse (1–2A) on the control board for the lighting circuit. Inspect and replace if blown.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker. Replace bulb with correct type for your model.
error_codes.models_affected: All Broan and Nutone range hood models
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new bulb, socket has power, and lights still do not work, the lighting control board or switch has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Slow drain — pump is draining but too slowly
error_codes.detail: F21 fires when the control board detects that the washer has not fully drained within the expected time window during a drain cycle. Unlike F20 (a no-water-detected or fill fault), F21 specifically flags sluggish drainage — the pump runs but water level drops too slowly, so the board times out. Common on Whirlpool-built Kenmore models, it points to restricted flow through the pump filter, drain hose, or a weakening impeller rather than a total pump failure.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access panel at lower front. Drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear and hose free, pump may be weak or failing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run Drain & Spin cycle after clearing blockage.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump filter cleaning doesn't resolve F21, the pump may need replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool-platform Kenmore front-load washers, primarily the 110 Series
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F21 mid-cycle with standing water left in the drum, often after washing bulky or linty items. A clogged pump filter — sometimes trapping coins, lint, or small garments — is the frequent quick win, and clearing it followed by a Drain & Spin often resolves it. A kinked or improperly looped drain hose can also trigger a false alarm.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-built 110-series Kenmore washers, F20 means the control opened the inlet valve but detected no water entering the drum within the allowed fill time, so the cycle stops. A closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens cause most cases — no parts needed.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and straighten any kinked fill hoses, then press Pause/Cancel twice to clear the code. Unplug for 60 seconds if it persists, then restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-manufactured Kenmore models (typically 400+ series), OE means the washer ran the drain step but couldn't empty the tub within the allowed time. A clogged pump filter or kinked drain hose is usually the cause and often clears without replacement parts.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Open the front lower panel, drain water through the small hose or cap, then remove and clean the pump filter thoroughly.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose is not kinked or crushed and the outlet height is in the proper 28–47 inch range.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain is not backing up and the hose end is not sealed airtight in the pipe.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hoses are clear, listen for pump operation during drain and test the motor for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the pump filter and hose, fully reseat the filter cap, then run a Spin Only or Drain cycle. OE clears once the washer drains the tub within the expected time.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump filter, drain hose, and standpipe are all clear but OE persists, the drain pump motor or the control board output has failed and should be tested by a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-built Kenmore washers, LE means the motor stalled or the control lost the rotor-position signal from the hall sensor, so the drum cannot reach the commanded speed. An overloaded drum, failed hall sensor, or stator winding fault are the normal causes.

fixes.title: Reduce and rebalance the load
fixes.description: Remove about half the items, untangle bulky pieces, and retry a spin so the motor is not fighting an overloaded drum.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the motor control, then restart with a smaller load.

fixes.title: Check the motor harness
fixes.description: Unplug the washer and inspect the rotor/stator wiring connectors for loose pins, corrosion, or rubbed insulation.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: Replace the inexpensive hall sensor near the rotor if LE returns with normal loads and the drum spins freely by hand.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes, reduce the load, and restart. LE clears when the control sees normal motor rotation; if it returns on an empty spin, the hall sensor or motor circuit needs testing.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hall sensor replacement does not resolve LE, or the drum will not turn freely by hand, the stator, rotor, bearings, or main control board need professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: UE is triggered when the washer's balance/vibration sensing detects that the drum is loading unevenly during the spin ramp-up. The control repeatedly slows, redistributes with water and slow rotation, then retries rather than spinning an off-center load that would strain the tub and bearings. It differs from drainage or door codes because the machine is mechanically fine — it simply can't achieve safe high-speed spin. Persistent UE across balanced loads points toward worn suspension springs, shocks, or damper wear rather than a load problem.

fixes.title: Redistribute laundry
fixes.description: Open door, move items evenly around drum.

fixes.title: Adjust load size
fixes.description: Remove or add items to balance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute and press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs every load, suspension springs are worn.
error_codes.models_affected: LG-platform Kenmore front-load and top-load washers, including 796 Series and Elite HE3/HE4 models; the older 110 Series (Whirlpool-built) typically does not use this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report UE with single bulky items like a comforter, towel, or pair of jeans, and it often clears simply by opening the door and spreading the load out. A frequent false-alarm situation is an unlevel machine or worn suspension, so if UE appears even with well-distributed laundry, checking the feet and leveling is often the quick win before suspecting parts.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-built 796-series Kenmore washers, IE means the control opened the inlet valve but the water level didn't rise within the expected fill window, so the cycle halts. A partly closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens are the most common root causes.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Fully open both hot and cold valves.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully, check the fill hoses for kinks, and clean the inlet screens. Press Power off, wait 60 seconds, then power on and restart the cycle — IE clears once a normal fill is detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The washer did not empty within the programmed drain window, so the control stops with water left in the drum to prevent overfill or spin damage. A clogged pump filter, kinked hose, blocked standpipe, or weak drain pump is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Open the lower access panel, drain into a tray, then remove and rinse the filter and pump cavity.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose has no kinks, is not pushed too far into the standpipe, and keeps a proper high loop.

fixes.title: Clear the standpipe
fixes.description: Run water through the standpipe or sink connection to verify the household drain isn't blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the path is clear, check the pump for continuity and replace it if seized, open, or only humming.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Spin Only or Drain/Spin cycle and confirm the tub empties completely. If E20 returns, recheck the filter and hose before replacing the pump.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter, hose, and household drain are clear but the pump won't run or E20 persists, the pump motor or control-board pump relay needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting water entering during fill. Supply valve, inlet screens, and inlet valve are the common causes.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully, then press Start/Pause to resume or cancel and restart the cycle. If E10 returns in the first minutes of fill, clean the inlet screens and retest.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock switch never signaled the control that the door is fully closed and locked, so the cycle will not start. Most often the door just needs a firmer push or laundry is caught in the gasket; a failed lock solenoid is the next most likely cause.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Inspect gasket for caught clothing.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E40 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor failure
error_codes.detail: The drive motor has failed or is not operating correctly. The drum will not rotate.

fixes.title: Check for belt (belt-drive models)
fixes.description: Inspect belt for breakage.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor, test winding continuity.

fixes.title: Replace motor or control board
fixes.description: Replace whichever component failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the motor control, then restart on a spin-only cycle. If E50 returns as soon as the drum tries to turn, motor or board replacement is required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Wiring harness fault — communication error
error_codes.detail: The control detected lost or corrupted communication through the washer's main wiring harness, so modules may not exchange status reliably and the cycle stops. A loose connector at the control, damaged harness, or failed communication port is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to reset the control boards, then restore power and retry a short cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixes.description: Check every connector plug for a loose fit, especially at the main control, UI board, motor, and door lock.

fixes.title: Trace the harness
fixes.description: Look for rubbed insulation, broken conductors, or corrosion where the harness flexes or passes metal edges.

fixes.title: Replace the harness
fixes.description: If connectors are secure but E90 returns, replace the damaged harness or the failed communication board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the controls, then restart a short cycle. If E90 returns, the wiring harness and board connectors need a full inspection before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness is intact and all connectors are tight but E90 persists, a control board communication port has failed and a technician should identify which board to replace.

error_codes.meaning: Long fill time — washer not filling during the wash phase
error_codes.detail: E11 fires when the control board does not sense the target water level being reached within the allotted fill window during the main wash phase. The pressure sensor monitors the level and, if it climbs too slowly or not at all, the timeout trips. Unlike E13 (a leak or drain-related fault) or the closely related E10 family, E11 points specifically to slow or absent fill during wash — most often a restricted supply, blocked inlet screen, or a weak inlet valve solenoid.

causes.description: Supply tap partly closed or low pressure

fixes.title: Check the tap and hose
fixes.description: Open the supply tap fully and straighten the fill hose. Confirm reasonable household water pressure.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Close the tap, remove the fill hose, and clean the mesh screen at the washer inlet.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Test the solenoid for continuity and replace if open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the tap fully and press Start/Pause to resume. If E11 returns early in fill, clean the inlet screen and retest.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and valve are confirmed good and E11 persists, the pressure sensor or control board needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Electrolux front-load washers, including models such as EFLS627UTT and ELFW7637AT
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E11 appearing early in a cycle, often after a hose was kinked behind the machine or a supply tap was left only partly open. A frequent quick win is simply opening the tap fully and cleaning the mesh inlet screen, which can clear the code without any parts.

error_codes.meaning: Water leak detected — level dropping when it should not
error_codes.detail: The control sees the water level falling during a phase when it should be holding, indicating a leak in the tub, sump, or air-bell hose, or a faulty pressure sensor reading.

causes.description: Leak at tub, sump, or hose connection

fixes.title: Inspect for water under the machine
fixes.description: Look for water at the base, sump hose clamps, and door boot.
fixes.warning: Unplug before reaching into the cabinet.

fixes.title: Check the pressure (air-bell) hose
fixes.description: Confirm the clear pressure hose to the sensor is intact and tightly clamped with no splits.

fixes.title: Check drain hose height
fixes.description: Ensure the drain hose has a proper high loop so the tub cannot siphon.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: If no leak is found, the pressure sensor may be misreading.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Stop the cycle and inspect for leaks before reuse.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A persistent leak that you cannot locate, or one near electrical components, should be handled by a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not pumping out fast enough
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the water-level sensor during the drain phase and expects the tub to reach the empty threshold within a set time window. When flow is too slow or absent, E21 fires. It differs from E20, which flags an outright drain-circuit fault or open pump connection, and from E23, which points to a pump control/wiring issue. E21 specifically indicates the pump is trying but water isn't clearing fast enough, most often from a clogged filter or restricted drain path.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Behind the lower access panel. Drain residual water, remove and clear the filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready for residual water.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose and standpipe
fixes.description: Confirm no kinks and that the standpipe is not blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the path is clear, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run a Spin/Drain cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump tests failed, replace the pump.
error_codes.models_affected: Electrolux front-load washers, including models such as EFLS627UTT and ELFW7637AT
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E21 appearing mid- or end-of-cycle with standing water left in the drum, often after washing items that shed lint, coins, or debris. Cleaning the pump filter behind the lower panel is frequently the quick win; a kinked hose pushed against the wall after the machine was moved is another common false trigger.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump triac/relay failure on the control board
error_codes.detail: The control board's drain-pump triac (the switching component that powers the pump) has failed, or the wiring/connection to the pump is broken. The pump may not run even though the drain path is clear.

causes.description: Drain pump triac on control board failed
Wire to pump loose or broken

fixes.title: Check the pump wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the connector and wires between the control board and the drain pump.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Test the pump motor
fixes.description: Measure pump winding resistance; an open winding means a failed pump.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If wiring and pump are good, the board triac has failed.
fixes.warning: Photograph all connectors before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E23 returns with a clear drain path, the board needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-level triac diagnosis and replacement is best left to a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor not communicating with the control board
error_codes.detail: The electronic pressure (water-level) sensor is sending a signal outside its valid range or no signal at all, so the washer can no longer trust how much water is in the tub. It will typically stop and may keep the door locked.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for one minute to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixes.description: Check the clear hose between the sump air-bell and the sensor for blockage or kinks.

fixes.title: Check sensor wiring
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the pressure sensor and inspect for corrosion.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: If the hose and wiring are good, replace the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug one minute. If E31 returns, inspect the pressure hose and sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor and hose check out, the control board input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor indicates water overfill
error_codes.detail: The pressure sensor is reporting a water level above the safe maximum. This can be a stuck inlet valve continuing to fill, or a pressure-sensing fault reporting a false high level.

fixes.title: Shut off the water
fixes.description: If the tub is overfilling, close the supply tap immediately.
fixes.warning: Standing water plus electricity is a hazard — unplug the machine.

fixes.title: Inspect the inlet valve
fixes.description: A valve that won't fully close will keep filling; test and replace if stuck.

fixes.title: Check the pressure sensor and hose
fixes.description: If the tub is not actually overfull, the sensor is misreading.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the tap and unplug. Do not run the washer until the overfill cause is found.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A washer that keeps overfilling should be serviced promptly to avoid water damage.

error_codes.meaning: Door open — control cannot confirm the door is shut
error_codes.detail: The control thinks the door is open: either the door is genuinely not latched, or the door switch is not signalling closed. The cycle will not start until the door is sensed closed.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Press the door until it clicks. Make sure no laundry is caught in the seal.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch and striker
fixes.description: Check that the striker engages the lock cleanly; adjust the door if it sags.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Test the door lock/switch for continuity in the closed state.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly and restart. If E41 persists with the door shut, test the door switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door is closed and the switch tests good, the control board door input may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock device failure — door will not lock
error_codes.detail: The door lock interlock did not confirm a locked state, so the wash cannot begin. The lock assembly (PTC/solenoid) is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Check door alignment
fixes.description: Confirm the door closes flush and the striker enters the lock cleanly.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for one minute and retry.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixes.description: If it will not lock, replace the interlock.
fixes.warning: Unplug before replacing the lock.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug one minute. If E42 returns, replace the door lock assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new lock does not clear E42, the board's lock circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock triac failure on the control board
error_codes.detail: The triac on the control board that drives the door lock has failed, so the board cannot energize the lock even though the lock itself may be good.

causes.description: Door lock triac on control board failed

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes to rule out a transient.

fixes.title: Verify the door lock is good
fixes.description: Test the lock assembly so you don't replace the board unnecessarily.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: E43 almost always means board replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph connectors before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E43 returns, the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board triac replacement should be handled by a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Door-closed sensing fault — closure sensor not detected
error_codes.detail: The control did not get a valid signal from the door-closed sensing circuit. The sensor or its wiring is the typical cause; the door itself may actually be shut.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug one minute and retry.

fixes.title: Inspect the door switch wiring
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the door lock/switch and check for damage.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock/switch assembly
fixes.description: If the sensing circuit is faulty, replace the assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug one minute. If E44 persists, test the door sensing circuit.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door assembly tests good, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock control circuit fault on the control board
error_codes.detail: The control board's door-lock sensing/drive circuit is faulty. The board cannot reliably read or control the lock state, so the cycle is blocked.

causes.description: Door lock circuit on control board failed
Wiring between board and lock faulty

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Check lock wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness between the control board and the door lock.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If wiring and lock are good, the board has failed.
fixes.warning: Photograph connectors first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E45 returns, the control board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-level door circuit repair is best left to a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor triac short-circuit on the control board
error_codes.detail: E51 fires when the control board detects that the triac switching power to the drive motor has failed shorted (closed), leaving the board unable to stop or modulate current to the motor. The board typically halts operation to protect itself, so the drum may not spin. Unlike E52 or E53, which point to motor tachometer or feedback signal faults, E51 indicates a hardware short in the board's motor-drive circuit, often triggered by a shorted motor winding, wiring fault, or surge.

causes.description: Drive motor triac shorted on control board
Motor winding shorted, damaging the triac

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes before further testing.

fixes.title: Test the drive motor
fixes.description: Measure motor winding resistance to rule out a shorted motor that could re-damage a new board.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: A shorted triac requires board replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph connectors before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E51 returns, the control board (and possibly the motor) needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Always confirm the motor is not shorted before replacing the board, or the new board may fail too.
error_codes.models_affected: Electrolux front-load washers using the main control board with a triac-driven drive motor, including models such as EFLS627UTT and ELFW7637AT
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing at the start of or during the spin phase, sometimes with the drum failing to turn. Because a shorted motor can destroy a replacement board, many find it worth confirming motor winding resistance before ordering parts; a simple 10-minute unplug reset occasionally clears a one-off surge-related trip but rarely fixes a genuinely shorted triac.

error_codes.meaning: No signal from the motor tacho generator
error_codes.detail: The control is not receiving a valid speed signal from the motor's tachometer, so it cannot regulate drum speed. The tacho coil, its wiring, or the motor connection is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Check the motor connector
fixes.description: Reseat the wiring connector at the motor and the control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixes.description: On brushed motors, worn carbon brushes cause erratic or no tacho signal.

fixes.title: Test/replace the tacho or motor
fixes.description: If the tacho coil is open, the motor usually needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E52 returns, inspect the motor connector and brushes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If wiring, brushes, and tacho are good, the control board speed input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor control circuit fault — low voltage to the speed control
error_codes.detail: The control board's motor-control/speed circuit is reading a fault, often low voltage or a bad signal between the main board and the motor speed control. The drum may not run.

causes.description: Speed control circuit on board faulty
Wiring between main board and speed control loose

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes to clear a transient.

fixes.title: Check internal wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness between the main control board and the motor speed control.
fixes.warning: Unplug before inspecting.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If wiring and motor are good, replace the board.
fixes.warning: Photograph connectors first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E53 returns, the motor control board needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor-control board diagnosis is best handled by a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Insufficient heating during wash — water not reaching temperature
error_codes.detail: The water did not heat to the target temperature within the expected time during the wash. A failed heating element or NTC sensor is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Measure element resistance — typically ~20–30 ohms; an open reading means a failed element.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Check the wash NTC resistance against spec.

fixes.title: Replace the failed part
fixes.description: Install a new element or NTC as needed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the heater at the tub base.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and run a hot wash test. If E61 returns, test the element and NTC.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element and NTC are good, the board's heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Overheating during wash — water temperature too high
error_codes.detail: The control detected the wash water heating above the safe limit (typically over ~88°C). This is usually a heater relay stuck on or an NTC misreading low so the board over-heats.

causes.description: Heater relay stuck closed on board

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and allow it to cool.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: An NTC reading low fools the board into over-heating.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: A relay stuck closed requires board replacement.
fixes.warning: A washer that overheats should not be used until repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. Do not run hot cycles until the overheating cause is found.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A stuck heater relay is a board fault and should be serviced promptly.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element relay failure on the main board
error_codes.detail: The relay on the control board that switches the heating element has failed, so the board cannot energize the heater (or cannot switch it off). Cycles run cold or the heater behaves erratically.

causes.description: Heater relay on control board failed

fixes.title: Verify the heating element
fixes.description: Confirm the element is good so you don't replace the board unnecessarily.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes to clear a transient.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: A failed heater relay requires board replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph connectors before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E66 returns with a good element, replace the board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board relay diagnosis and replacement is best left to a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error between the user interface and the main board
error_codes.detail: E91 fires when the main control board fails to establish or maintain proper data communication with the user-interface (display) board. The control monitors the signal handshake across the connecting harness; if messages are missing, corrupted, or timed out, it logs E91. It sits in the E9x configuration/communication family—distinct from E90 (configuration/mismatch) and E94—and most often reflects a loose or damaged ribbon connector rather than an outright board failure, though either board can be at fault.

causes.description: Loose/damaged harness between UI and main board

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat the interface harness
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat the ribbon/connector between the UI and main board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the faulty board
fixes.description: If reseating fails, replace the UI or main board as diagnosed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E91 returns, reseat the UI-to-main harness.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the harness is sound, board-level diagnosis is best handled by a technician.
error_codes.models_affected: Electrolux front-load washers using the E9x control platform, including models such as EFLS627UTT and ELFW7637AT
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E91 appearing suddenly, sometimes after a power blip, occasionally with the display frozen or unresponsive. A full unplug-and-reset often clears a one-time glitch, and when it recurs, reseating the console harness is the fix people most often mention before replacing a board.

error_codes.meaning: Washer configuration error — incorrect control data
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an invalid configuration/EEPROM setting, so it cannot match the program data to the machine. This often follows a board swap that wasn't configured to the model, or corrupted memory.

causes.description: Control board not configured to the model

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes to clear a transient memory glitch.

fixes.title: Reconfigure the board
fixes.description: If a replacement board was fitted, it must be configured to the model (technician/service tool).

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the configuration cannot be restored, replace the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E94 returns, the board needs (re)configuration or replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Configuration requires a service tool — have a technician set the board to your model.

error_codes.meaning: Insufficient heating — dryer not reaching temperature
error_codes.detail: The control detected the drum reaching temperature too slowly or not at all, so cycles run long and clothes come out damp. A burned-out heating element is the most common root cause, followed by a high-limit thermostat tripped by a blocked vent.

fixes.title: Check for vent blockage
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit to trip, cutting heat.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test for continuity — should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity. Tripped thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or thermostat as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, then run a timed-dry test on high heat. If E61 returns or the dryer still runs cool, test the heating element and high-limit thermostat before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and thermostat are good, board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors current draw through the heating element circuit. E64 fires when it senses an open circuit — no current flowing — meaning the element has burned through or a terminal/connection has failed, so the drum tumbles but produces no heat. This distinguishes it from related code E61, which typically flags a shorted or over-current heater condition rather than an open one. Restricted exhaust airflow raises element temperature over time and accelerates burnout.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test for continuity. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer before accessing element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, board heater relay has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Electrolux electric dryers with an electronic control board and current-monitored heating circuit, including models such as the EFME627UTT and ELFE7637AT
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report clothes coming out damp with the drum still spinning, and the code often surfaces on older units or after long-neglected vent cleaning. Before assuming the worst, some find a burned terminal or loose wire rather than a fully failed element, which is a cheaper fix.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or out-of-range resistance from the NTC temperature sensor, so it cannot regulate drum heat. The dryer may run without heat or stop mid-cycle. A failed thermistor in the exhaust duct is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure the NTC sensor — about 10k ohms at room temperature; open or out-of-range means failed.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the thermistor plug in the exhaust duct area and inspect for corrosion or a loose pin.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Trace the sensor wires back to the control and repair any rubbed or broken insulation.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install a matching Electrolux thermistor in the exhaust duct and verify resistance before reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or repairing its connector, unplug the dryer for 1 minute, restore power, and run a timed-dry cycle to confirm E62 clears.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but E62 persists, the control board's temperature-sensor input circuit has failed and needs professional board diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock / safety circuit fault — dryer will not start
error_codes.detail: The control cannot confirm the door-lock or start safety circuit, so the dryer will not begin a cycle. A worn door latch or a loose connection between the latch and the control board is the common cause.

causes.description: Door latch/lock worn or not engaging
Wiring between latch and board loose

fixes.title: Reset the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5–10 minutes, then retry.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Clear any lint or debris and confirm the door clicks shut and the latch engages.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Test the door switch/latch for continuity in the closed state.

fixes.title: Check latch wiring
fixes.description: Reseat the connector between the latch and the control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5–10 minutes and retry. If E01 persists, test the door switch and its wiring.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch and wiring test good, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water drainage / condensate pump fault (condenser & heat-pump dryers)
error_codes.detail: On condenser and heat-pump dryers the condensed water is pumped to the reservoir or drain. E20 means that water is not being moved — usually a clogged condensate pump, blocked filter, or full reservoir that isn't draining.

fixes.title: Empty the water reservoir
fixes.description: Empty the condensate tank if your model has one.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter/sump
fixes.description: Remove and clear lint from the pump filter and sump area.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready for residual water.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Straighten and clear the condensate drain hose.

fixes.title: Test the condensate pump
fixes.description: If the path is clear, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Empty the tank and clean the pump filter, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but water won't pump, replace the condensate pump.

error_codes.meaning: Door circuit fault — control cannot confirm the door state
error_codes.detail: The E4x family covers the door circuit. E40 means the control board cannot read a consistent door-open/closed state, either because of the door switch or its wiring.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Confirm the door latches with a click and nothing blocks the seal.

fixes.title: Reset the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and retry.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Test for continuity as the door is closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly and restart. If E40 persists, test the door switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the switch and wiring test good, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Configuration / communication error in the electronics
error_codes.detail: E93 belongs to the E90–E94 family that indicates configuration or communication problems between the dryer's electronic parts. It often follows a board swap that wasn't configured to the model, or a loose harness between boards.

causes.description: Control board not configured to the model

fixes.title: Reset the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat the board harness
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat connectors between the user interface and main control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug before opening the console.

fixes.title: Reconfigure or replace the board
fixes.description: A replacement board must be configured to the model; if config can't be restored, replace the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E93 returns, the electronics need (re)configuration or service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board configuration needs a service tool — have a technician set it to your model.

error_codes.meaning: Heating / power-supply circuit fault
error_codes.detail: The EH family indicates a power-supply or heating-circuit problem. EHd typically means the control sees a fault in the heater power circuit — incoming voltage, the heater, a blown thermal fuse, or the control board itself.

causes.description: Heating element or thermal fuse open
Door switch/fuse in the heat circuit open

fixes.title: Check the power supply
fixes.description: Confirm the dryer is on a correct, dedicated circuit at the proper voltage.

fixes.title: Test the thermal fuse and element
fixes.description: A blocked vent can blow the thermal fuse; test the fuse and heating element for continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing the heat circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If voltage, fuse, and element are good, the board heater circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If EHd returns, test voltage, the thermal fuse, and the element before suspecting the board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the supply, fuse, and element are good, board-level repair is best left to a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Pump fault — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The control detected that water was not pumped out within the expected drain time — the drum still holds water when the pump should have emptied it, so the program stops with F11. The most common root cause is a pump filter clogged with lint or debris; a kinked drain hose or an object jamming the pump impeller follow close behind.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower service flap. Turn slowly and drain water before removing filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; correct standpipe height.

fixes.title: Remove impeller obstruction
fixes.description: Check pump housing for foreign objects.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: Miele pumps are high-quality but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run a Drain/Short Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele service intervals are long, but pump filter should be cleaned every 6 months.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — slow or failed drain
error_codes.detail: The washer is taking too long to empty, so the control stops before spin to avoid running with water in the drum. A clogged pump filter, partial drain-hose blockage, foreign object in the pump, or a weakening drain pump is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Use the lower service flap, drain slowly through the emergency hose, then remove and rinse the filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Trace the hose for kinks, crushed sections, or lint buildup at the standpipe connection.

fixes.title: Remove pump obstructions
fixes.description: With the filter out, check the impeller area for coins, buttons, or hair pins stopping rotation.

fixes.title: Replace the pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump is noisy, weak, or seized, fit a matching Miele pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the filter or hose, run a Drain/Short Spin cycle and confirm the drum empties quickly. If F19 returns, don't force a spin cycle with water still in the tub.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drain path is clear but F19 persists or the pump only hums, the pump or its wiring needs testing; Miele authorized service is recommended for replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water protection system activated — flood protection triggered
error_codes.detail: The Miele waterproof system (Waterproof System/WPS) has detected water in the base of the machine. This is a safety lockout.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt forward (power off) to drain base tray.
fixes.warning: Power off water and electricity first.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or pulled sections.

fixes.title: Check supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Inspect both ends of the supply hose for weeping.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak source, dry thoroughly. Sensor resets when base is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no leak source found, internal hose or sump connection needs Miele authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The Miele washing machine motor has experienced a fault. This is a serious error typically requiring professional service. Miele motors are brushless and exceptionally durable, but can fail after many years.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. A transient motor fault may clear.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — F53 can sometimes be triggered by extreme overloading.

fixes.title: Contact Miele service
fixes.description: F53 motor faults on Miele machines almost always require authorized technician diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F53 persists, contact Miele authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele motor replacement is complex and typically requires factory-trained technicians. DIY motor replacement risks voiding the warranty.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control is receiving an open or out-of-range signal from the wash-water NTC thermistor, so it can't verify heating temperature and may stop the program. A failed thermistor, loose connector, or damaged sensor harness is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: With power off, measure the NTC at room temperature — it should read roughly 6k–10k ohms, not open.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the sensor and inspect for corrosion, moisture, or a backed-out pin.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Trace the thermistor harness for rubbed insulation or breaks where it passes the tub and heater area.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install a genuine Miele NTC thermistor if resistance is out of range or intermittent when moved.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement or connector repair, restart the washer and run a heated program; F31 clears only after the control sees a valid temperature rise.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but F31 returns, the harness or control-board temperature input is failing and should be diagnosed by Miele service.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor short-circuited
error_codes.detail: The control read the water/heating NTC temperature sensor (or its wiring) as short-circuited, so heating is disabled and the program stops with F1. At about 68°F the sensor should read roughly 14.5k ohms — a reading far below that points to a shorted thermistor, a pinched harness, or moisture in a connector.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch back on — a transient moisture short can clear.

fixes.title: Inspect sensor connector
fixes.description: Check for water ingress or corrosion at the NTC plug and dry it out.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: At room temp it should read ~14.5k ohms; near 0 ohms confirms a short.

fixes.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Fit a genuine Miele thermistor if the part is shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, restart. If F1 returns, the sensor or wiring needs attention.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the new sensor faults immediately, a wiring or board issue is likely — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor open-circuited
error_codes.detail: The control read the water/heating NTC temperature sensor (or its leads) as open-circuited — the opposite of F1 — so it cannot confirm water temperature and stops with F2. An open circuit usually means a broken thermistor element, a disconnected plug, or a severed lead.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch back on.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Confirm the NTC plug is fully seated on both the sensor and the board.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: An infinite/open reading at room temp confirms the sensor is open.

fixes.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Fit a genuine Miele thermistor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, restart. If F2 persists, replace the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F2 after a new sensor points to harness or board damage — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Hot water intake fault
error_codes.detail: F15 fires on models with a separate hot-water feed when the control board fails to register adequate flow or the expected temperature on that hot inlet within its timed fill window. The board monitors the hot inlet valve, flow, and feed temperature; a closed stopcock, kinked hose, clogged mesh screen, or a failed hot inlet valve all prevent proper intake. Unlike a general cold-fill fault, F15 specifically flags the hot circuit, so cold-only fills may still work.

causes.description: Hot supply valve closed or restricted

fixes.title: Open the hot tap fully
fixes.description: Confirm the hot-water stopcock under the sink is fully open.

fixes.title: Straighten/clean the hose
fixes.description: Trace the hot hose for kinks and clean the mesh screen at the inlet.

fixes.title: Replace the hot inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is good but the fault stays, the valve has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the hot supply, straighten the hose, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hot supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Miele W1 Series and other Miele washers configured for dual hot-and-cold water intake
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F15 shortly after installation, plumbing work, or moving the machine, when the hot stopcock is left partly closed or the hose gets pinched behind the unit. Fully opening the hot tap and straightening the hose often clears it quickly; if the supply is confirmed good, a failing inlet valve is the usual remaining culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Heating fault — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The control expected the wash water to reach its target temperature within a set time but the NTC never confirmed it, so the program halts with F20 and washes run cold. A failed heating element is the leading cause, followed by the NTC sensor or the heater relay on the board.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and check continuity — a typical element reads ~20–30 ohms; open means failed.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Confirm the thermistor reads in range (see F1/F2).

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Fit a genuine element or sensor as diagnosed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a heated cycle to confirm the fault clears.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heater replacement on Miele washers requires care with the seal and harness — authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock fault — door will not lock
error_codes.detail: The machine could not confirm the door lock engaged, so for safety it will not start and shows F34. A door not fully latched is the most common trigger, followed by a failed interlock or a wiring fault to the lock.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Press the door until it clicks; clear any laundry trapped in the seal.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch and striker
fixes.description: Check the striker hook and lock cavity for damage or obstruction.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock
fixes.description: If the door is shut but F34 stays, the interlock has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly, switch off ~60 seconds, restart. If F34 returns, the lock needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele door interlock replacement is best handled by authorized service to keep the safety circuit intact.

error_codes.meaning: Door unlock fault — door will not release
error_codes.detail: At the end of the cycle the control could not confirm the door lock released, so it holds F35 and keeps the door shut. A failed lock mechanism or its wiring is the usual cause; the door stays locked until the fault is cleared or the lock is replaced.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch on — the lock may release on reset.

fixes.title: Use the emergency release
fixes.description: If fitted, use the manual lock release behind the lower flap to free the door.
fixes.warning: Only after the drum has stopped and water is drained.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock
fixes.description: A lock that will not release needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off 5 minutes; if the door frees but F35 returns, the lock needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door stays locked with water inside, contact Miele authorized service rather than forcing it.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic unit fault — main control failure
error_codes.detail: The control electronics reported an internal fault and the program stops with F39. This points at the main electronic unit (control board) rather than a single sensor or motor, and usually needs board-level diagnosis.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for 5–10 minutes to clear a transient board fault.

fixes.title: Check supply quality
fixes.description: Confirm the machine is on a sound circuit, not a weak extension or unstable supply.

fixes.title: Board diagnosis/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F39 indicates the electronic unit needs Miele diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off 5–10 minutes and restart. If F39 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control-board faults on Miele washers require authorized service — board coding and calibration are model-specific.

error_codes.meaning: Faulty electronic memory — incorrect data
error_codes.detail: The control read invalid data from its electronic memory (EEPROM), so it cannot run reliably and stops with F41. This is a board-level fault — corrupted or failing memory on the main electronic unit.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes; an occasional glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Board re-coding/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F41 means the electronic unit needs reprogramming or replacement by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F41 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: EEPROM faults require Miele service — the board must be coded to the specific model.

error_codes.meaning: Appliance model not programmed
error_codes.detail: The control electronics are not coded to a specific model, so the machine does not know its own configuration and stops with F43. This typically appears after a board replacement that was not programmed, or after a memory fault wiped the model code.

causes.description: Replacement board not coded to model

fixes.title: Confirm recent board work
fixes.description: F43 almost always follows a control-board swap that skipped the model coding step.

fixes.title: Program the control
fixes.description: The electronic unit must be coded to the exact model by Miele service tooling.

error_codes.reset_instructions: F43 is not user-resettable — the control must be programmed to the model by Miele service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Model coding requires Miele service tooling — always authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Defective Flash RAM — incorrect data supplied
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault in its Flash RAM and is being supplied incorrect data, so it halts with F45. Like F41/F43 this is a fault inside the main electronic unit rather than a mechanical part.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes to attempt a clear.

fixes.title: Board diagnosis/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F45 means the electronic unit needs reflashing or replacement by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F45 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Flash RAM faults require Miele service — reflashing/coding is model-specific.

error_codes.meaning: Drive fault — motor or drive electronics
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault in the drive system and stops with F50. Miele motors are brushless and durable, so the fault often sits in the motor control electronics or wiring rather than the motor windings themselves; severe overloading can also trigger it.

fixes.title: Power cycle and reduce load
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes and remove some laundry — a transient drive fault may clear.

fixes.title: Check the drum turns freely
fixes.description: With power off, confirm nothing is jamming the drum or belt.

fixes.title: Drive diagnosis
fixes.description: Persistent F50 needs board/motor diagnosis by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart with a lighter load. If F50 persists, the drive needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive electronics and motor work on Miele washers require authorized technicians.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure/level sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control received an implausible reading from the water-level pressure sensor, so it cannot judge fill or drain accurately and stops with F51. A blocked or split pressure hose, debris in the chamber, or a failed sensor are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixes.description: Check the thin hose between the tub chamber and the sensor for kinks, splits, or trapped water.

fixes.title: Clear the pressure chamber
fixes.description: Clean any soap/debris build-up that can block the air column.

fixes.title: Replace the level sensor
fixes.description: If the hose and chamber are clear, the sensor has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the pressure hose/chamber, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fault persists after cleaning and a new sensor, Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Spin fault — final spin speed below 400 rpm
error_codes.detail: The drum never reached the required final spin speed (under ~400 rpm), so the load comes out wetter than expected and the machine flags F56. Severe imbalance, an overload, or a drive/sensor issue keeps the spin from ramping up.

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the door, rebalance bulky items, and avoid overloading.

fixes.title: Run a spin-only cycle
fixes.description: Test whether a balanced load spins up correctly.

fixes.title: Drive/sensor diagnosis
fixes.description: If a balanced load still fails, the drive or speed sensor needs checking.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Rebalance the load and run a spin-only cycle. If F56 persists with a balanced load, service is needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent low-spin faults with a balanced load point to drive or bearing issues — Miele service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Water path control unit fault
error_codes.detail: The control could not correctly operate or confirm the water-path control unit (the valve/diverter assembly that routes water through the detergent and rinse paths), so it stops with F63. A jammed diverter, a wiring fault, or a failed control unit are the usual causes.

causes.description: Scale or debris jamming the diverter

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Check for limescale
fixes.description: In hard-water areas, scale can jam the diverter — descaling may help.

fixes.title: Replace the water path unit
fixes.description: If the diverter is jammed or the unit failed, it needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F63 persists, the water-path unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: The water-path control unit is a sealed assembly best replaced by Miele authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Drum lighting cap missing
error_codes.detail: The control detected that the drum interior lighting cap/cover is missing or not correctly fitted and flags F65. This is a minor, mostly cosmetic fault around the drum light — it does not affect the wash, but the machine reports it for completeness.

fixes.title: Refit the lighting cap
fixes.description: Check the drum interior light cover is present and clicked into place.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: If the cap is present, reseat the light module connector.

fixes.title: Replace the light module
fixes.description: If the lamp/module has failed, fit a genuine replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Refit the lighting cap, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F65 is minor — only call a pro if the light module itself has failed and you are not comfortable replacing it.

error_codes.meaning: Drainage fault — water not pumped out
error_codes.detail: F11 fires when the control commands the drain pump but the sump's water level fails to fall within the expected time window, so the cycle stops with standing water in the tub. The board monitors level via the drain circuit rather than a specific pump fault, which is why a clogged filter combination triggers it as readily as a failing pump. This distinguishes F11 from inlet or overfill faults, which report an unexpected rise in water instead of a stalled drop.

fixes.title: Clean the filters
fixes.description: Miele dishwashers have a fine filter and coarse filter. Remove and clean both.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure proper high-loop installation and no kinks.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, pump may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filters and check the drain hose, then switch the dishwasher off at the unit, wait about 60 seconds, switch it back on, and restart on a short program — the fault clears once the sump drains normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele drain pump replacement is best handled by authorized service.
error_codes.models_affected: Miele dishwashers across recent generations, including the G 7000 Series, as well as earlier fully integrated and freestanding models that share the sump-and-filter drainage design.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F11 appearing mid-cycle with a puddle left in the base, and cleaning the coarse and fine filters plus clearing food debris from the sump often resolves it. A kinked or improperly looped drain hose after the unit was moved is a frequent false trigger, so it's worth checking before suspecting the pump.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault — dishwasher not filling
error_codes.detail: The Miele dishwasher did not take in the required amount of water within the fill time limit, so the program stops with F13. The control monitors fill volume and pressure at the circulation system — a closed or partially open supply tap is the most common cause, followed by a blocked inlet screen or kinked hose.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially open

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Unscrew supply hose and clean the screen at the inlet port.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Miele inlet valves are reliable but do fail after many years.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the under-sink supply valve fully and straighten the inlet hose. Switch the dishwasher off at the unit, wait about 60 seconds, switch it back on, and start a fresh program — the fault clears once water flows normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heating issue — water not heating to target temperature
error_codes.detail: F15 fires when the control monitors wash-water temperature during heating and the NTC sensor fails to confirm the target is reached within the allotted time. The board interprets the stalled temperature rise as a heating fault and halts the cycle to avoid running an ineffective wash. Unlike a drainage or fill code, F15 points specifically at the heat path: a failed flow-through heater, an out-of-range NTC sensor, or a defective heater relay on the control board.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temp: ~6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating component replacement on Miele requires care to maintain waterproof sealing.
error_codes.models_affected: Miele dishwashers with a flow-through heating circuit, including the G 7000 Series and comparable earlier generations
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code appearing mid-cycle with lukewarm or cold interior water and dishes that come out greasy or poorly dried. It can occasionally be a false trigger from a marginal sensor reading, so testing the NTC before replacing the heater is often the quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Flow meter fault — water quantity sensor failure
error_codes.detail: F70 fires when the control board stops receiving valid pulse signals from the flow meter that tracks incoming water volume during fill. Because the board relies on those pulses to close the inlet valve at the right quantity, missing or erratic counts risk under- or overfilling. A scaled or debris-jammed impeller, a loose or corroded connector, or a failed meter each cut the pulse train. Unlike F11 (drainage/water level faults), F70 points specifically at the fill-metering sensor, not blocked draining.

fixes.title: Clean the flow meter
fixes.description: Remove the flow meter and flush out scale or grit so the impeller can spin freely during fill.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the flow-meter plug and inspect for moisture, corrosion, or a loose pin breaking the pulse signal.

fixes.title: Check inlet flow
fixes.description: Verify the inlet screen and supply valve are clear so low flow is not being mistaken for meter failure.

fixes.title: Replace the flow meter
fixes.description: If cleaning and connector checks don't restore pulses, install a matching Miele flow meter.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning or replacing the flow meter, restart on a short program and confirm the dishwasher fills normally without F70 returning during the fill step.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F70 returns with clean inlet flow and a known-good meter, the wiring or control board input is failing and Miele authorized service should diagnose it.
error_codes.models_affected: Miele dishwashers with an electronic flow meter on the water inlet, including the G 7000 Series and comparable earlier generations
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F70 appearing at the start of a cycle during the fill step, sometimes after a period of hard-water use. In hard-water areas a scale-bound impeller is a frequent false trigger, and many find that flushing the meter or reseating its connector clears the code without a new part.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault at the start of a step
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher failed to take in the required water at the start of a program step, so it stops with F12. It is closely related to F13 (which faults later in the step) and almost always traces to the supply side — a closed or restricted tap, a kinked hose, or a blocked inlet screen.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially open

fixes.title: Open the supply tap
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink valve is fully open and water pressure is adequate.

fixes.title: Straighten the inlet hose
fixes.description: Trace the hose from wall to unit for kinks.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Unscrew the supply hose and clean the mesh screen at the inlet port.

fixes.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is good but F12 stays, the valve has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the tap fully and straighten the hose, then switch off ~60 seconds, switch back on, and restart on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater pressure switch fault
error_codes.detail: The control could not get a valid signal from the heater pressure switch — the safety switch that confirms water is circulating before the heater is energized — so it stops with F14 to protect the heating circuit. A blocked or split pressure hose, debris in the circulation path, or a failed switch are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Clean the filters and sump
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the filter combination so the circulation path is clear.

fixes.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixes.description: Check the hose to the pressure switch for kinks, splits, or trapped water.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixes.description: If hose and path are clear, the switch has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filters, check the pressure hose, switch off ~60 seconds, then restart on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pressure-switch and heating-circuit work is best handled by Miele authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Heating circuit fault — heater relay or temperature sensor
error_codes.detail: The control expected the heater to bring the water to temperature but the heating circuit did not respond as expected, so it stops with F24. The fault sits in the heating circuit — most often the heater relay on the board, the NTC temperature sensor, or the heating element itself.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Confirm the temperature sensor reads in range (~14.5k ohms cold).

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check element continuity — open means failed.

fixes.title: Board/relay diagnosis
fixes.description: A failed heater relay on the control needs board-level diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off ~60 seconds and restart on a full program to confirm heating.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heater relay and element work require care with the waterproof sealing — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Circulation pump / frequency converter fault
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault driving the circulation pump (the pump that sprays water through the arms during the wash), often reported by its frequency converter, so the program stops with F78 and dishes are left unwashed. A blocked or jammed pump impeller, a wiring fault, or a failed converter/pump are the usual causes.

causes.description: Circulation pump impeller jammed by debris

fixes.title: Clear debris from the sump
fixes.description: Remove the filters and check the sump and spray-arm feed for glass, seeds, or labels.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the unit for several minutes to clear a transient drive fault.

fixes.title: Replace the circulation pump
fixes.description: If the impeller is jammed/failed or the converter is faulty, the pump needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the sump and switch off several minutes. If F78 persists, the circulation pump needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement on Miele dishwashers is a sealed-system job for authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Slide-shutter positioning fault during water intake
error_codes.detail: The control could not confirm the correct position of the slide shutter / diverter that directs water during intake, so it stops with F84. It is closely related to the F63 water-path fault — the diverter valve, its position sensing, or the control electronics are the usual causes; excess detergent foam can also confuse the positioning.

fixes.title: Use the correct detergent dose
fixes.description: Excess or wrong detergent can foam and disrupt the shutter — run a rinse and dose correctly.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the unit for several minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Diverter/board diagnosis
fixes.description: Persistent F84 needs the diverter valve and control electronics checked by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a rinse with the correct detergent dose and power off several minutes. If F84 persists, the diverter/control needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Diverter and control-board faults on Miele dishwashers require authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet valve fault — water supply issue
error_codes.detail: Speed Queen washers are extremely reliable commercial-grade machines for home use. E3 indicates the water inlet valve is not functioning correctly or water pressure is insufficient.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially closed

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Test water pressure
fixes.description: Minimum 20 PSI required.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity (200–500 ohms).

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve if failed
fixes.description: Speed Queen parts are available from authorized dealers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are built for longevity. If valve fails, genuine SQ parts are recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The control ran the drain pump but the water level didn't drop within the allowed time, so the cycle stops with E8 and the tub stays full. On these commercial-grade machines the most common root cause is lint, coins, or small items lodged in the pump filter or impeller rather than an actual pump motor failure.

fixes.title: Access pump filter
fixes.description: Speed Queen front-loaders have a pump filter. Drain water and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper installation.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump
fixes.description: Speed Queen pumps are commercial-grade but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Authorized Speed Queen service is available through their dealer network.

error_codes.meaning: Motor feedback error — motor control fault
error_codes.detail: The motor control system has detected incorrect feedback from the drive motor. This is a serious error typically requiring technical diagnosis.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check motor belt (if applicable)
fixes.description: Some SQ models use a belt drive.

fixes.title: Contact Speed Queen service
fixes.description: Speed Queen machines are built to last 25 years — motor faults typically warrant authorized service.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E2 returns, Speed Queen authorized service is recommended.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen's commercial heritage means complex motor faults are best handled by their dealer service network.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not spin with an open or faulty lid switch.

causes.description: Lid switch actuator bent or broken

fixes.title: Inspect lid switch actuator
fixes.description: The plastic tab that depresses the switch when the lid closes. Check for cracks.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Depress switch manually and listen for click. Test with multimeter for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Unscrew switch from lid frame and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new lid switch doesn't resolve E5, the wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main controller cannot communicate with a sub-module or the user interface. This typically indicates a control board issue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
