fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check wiring harness connectors
fixes.description: Reseat all connectors on the control board.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If power cycle and connections don't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E1 returns, contact Speed Queen authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are repairable for life — authorized dealers stock parts for decades.

error_codes.meaning: Heating error — dryer not heating correctly
error_codes.detail: The Speed Queen dryer's heating system is not functioning correctly. The drum may turn but produces no or insufficient heat.

causes.description: Tripped high-limit thermostat from blocked vent

fixes.title: Check and clean vent
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit thermostat to trip permanently.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — 10–20 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity — failed thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed part
fixes.description: Install genuine Speed Queen replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing heating components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer (or switch off its breaker) for 60 seconds, clear the lint screen and vent, then restore power and run a timed-dry test cycle to confirm the dryer heats normally before loading laundry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent, element, and thermostat all check out, the control board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or out-of-range resistance from the exhaust thermistor, so it can no longer regulate drum temperature and shuts the cycle down to prevent overheating. An open-circuit thermistor is the cause in most cases; a loose connector or chafed wiring accounts for the rest.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or reseating its connector, unplug the dryer (or flip the breaker) for 60 seconds, restore power, and run a short heated cycle to confirm the code stays clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle cannot complete
error_codes.detail: Amana washers are built on the Whirlpool platform, so F9E1 means the washer ran the drain step but couldn't empty the tub within the allowed time, leaving standing water. A clogged drain pump filter is the most common cause and clears without any parts.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Open the lower-front access panel, set out towels and a pan, then slowly unscrew the filter cap to drain water and clear debris.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose isn't kinked, keeps a proper high loop, and isn't pushed too far into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain the hose empties into isn't clogged or backing up.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the filter and hoses are clear, test the pump motor for continuity and listen for it running during drain.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the blockage, reseat the filter cap fully, then select Drain & Spin and press Start — F9E1 clears once the washer empties the tub within the expected time.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter, hoses, and household drain are all clear but F9E1 returns, the drain pump or the control board's pump relay has likely failed and a technician should test both.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply
error_codes.detail: The control opened the fill valve but the pressure sensor never saw enough water arrive within the allowed fill time, so the cycle pauses or aborts. A partly closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens cause most cases and can be fixed without any parts.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Clean mesh screens at washer inlet ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and check the hoses for kinks, then unplug the washer for 60 seconds. Plug back in and press Start — F8E1 clears once the washer detects a normal fill.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds
error_codes.detail: The washer detected excess foam in the tub and paused the cycle to run a suds-reduction routine before continuing. Using too much detergent, or any non-HE detergent in this high-efficiency machine, is the cause in nearly every case — it is not a mechanical fault.

fixes.title: Wait
fixes.description: Washer resumes automatically.

fixes.title: Use less detergent
fixes.description: HE detergent only, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wait — the washer runs a suds-reduction rinse and resumes by itself, usually within 30 minutes. To clear it faster, press Cancel, run a Rinse & Spin with no detergent, then restart the load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD rarely needs service. Call a pro only if it appears on every load despite correct HE detergent dosing — repeated false SUD codes point to a failing pressure sensor or a partial drain restriction trapping foam.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main electronic control board failed its internal self-check, so the dryer halts and won't respond normally. A failed onboard component (relay or capacitor) is the usual root cause, often triggered by a power surge — sometimes a long power cycle clears a transient fault.

fixes.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Confirm the dryer has full 240V power — a half-tripped double-pole breaker can mimic a control board fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer (or switch off its breaker) for 15 minutes, then restore power to clear a transient control fault.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and reseat the wiring connectors on the control board; loose or corroded pins can trigger F01.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F01 returns after a power cycle, the main control board has failed and needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for a full 15 minutes (or switch off the breaker) so the control board fully discharges, then restore power. If F01 returns right away, the board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 returns after a power cycle and connector check, or appears on a new control board, have a technician test the incoming power supply and wiring harness before assuming the board is at fault.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open or out-of-range circuit from the exhaust thermistor, so it can't measure outgoing air temperature or regulate heat safely — the dryer stops or won't heat. A failed thermistor is the most common cause; it's an inexpensive part in the exhaust duct.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute, then restore power — this clears a transient thermistor reading after any repair.

fixes.title: Test the exhaust thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure the thermistor's resistance at room temperature; it should read roughly 10k–12k ohms, not open.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Swap the thermistor on the blower housing in the exhaust duct if it reads open or far out of range — an inexpensive part.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After reconnecting or replacing the thermistor, unplug the dryer for 1 minute and restore power, then start a heated cycle and confirm F22 stays clear and the dryer heats normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correct but F22 keeps returning, the control board's sensor input circuit or the wiring harness to the thermistor has failed and needs a technician to trace.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — temperatures above safe range
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected temperatures above the safe threshold for food storage. Check the door seals and fans.

causes.description: Door left open or seal worn

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Check all door gaskets for proper sealing.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum condenser coils at back/bottom of unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Confirm both evaporator and condenser fans are running.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close doors and wait 4 hours. H1 clears when temperature normalizes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans run, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, compressor or refrigerant needs inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet fault — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: Fisher & Paykel washers are known for their innovative top-load designs. E4 indicates a water supply issue preventing proper filling.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Remove hose and clean mesh filter at washer port.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and clean the inlet screens. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the fault, plug back in, and restart the cycle — the code clears once the machine fills at a normal rate.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel authorized service available for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The washer control commanded drain but water is not leaving the drum at the expected rate, so it stops before spin. A clogged pump/filter area, kinked hose, or drain pump motor that is seized or electrically open is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the model-specific filter or pump access, drain water into a tray, and clear lint or coins from the pump cavity.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the hose is routed correctly, not kinked, and not blocked where it enters the standpipe or tub.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: With power off, check pump continuity and confirm the impeller turns freely instead of binding or humming.

fixes.title: Replace the pump
fixes.description: If the pump is seized, open, or won't move water after cleaning, install a matching Fisher & Paykel drain pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the drain path, run a spin or drain cycle and confirm the tub empties before high-speed spin. If E5 returns, stop and recheck the pump and hose.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hose and filter are clear but the pump does not run, the pump wiring or motor controller output may be faulty; Fisher & Paykel authorized service can test it safely.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault — lid not detected as closed (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not agitate or spin for safety.

fixes.title: Inspect switch actuator
fixes.description: Check the plastic tab that triggers the lid switch.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Multimeter test for continuity when depressed.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Standard repair on top-load models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If switch replacement doesn't resolve E6, wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor controller fault
error_codes.detail: The motor controller detected abnormal motor feedback or drive current from the brushless motor system, so it stops the cycle to protect the stator and board. A failed controller board, stator fault, or rotor-position sensor problem is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to reset the motor controller, then restore power and try a spin-only cycle.

fixes.title: Check for overloading
fixes.description: Remove half the load and redistribute bulky items; extreme overload can trigger motor-current protection.

fixes.title: Inspect for binding
fixes.description: Spin the basket by hand with power off; dragging, seized bearings, or jammed clothing can overload the controller.

fixes.title: Contact F&P service
fixes.description: Motor controller, stator, and rotor-position faults require model-specific diagnostics and parts.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes, reduce the load, and restart on spin-only. If E2 returns as soon as the basket tries to turn, stop using the washer and book authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel brushless motor systems are proprietary; if load and mechanical binding are ruled out, authorized service should test the controller, stator, and rotor sensor.

error_codes.meaning: Not draining — drain fault
error_codes.detail: The DishDrawer cannot pump water out of the drawer within the allowed drain time, so dirty water remains in the tub. A clogged drawer filter/sump, kinked or poorly routed drain hose, or failed drain pump is the usual cause.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean the drawer filter
fixes.description: Remove the filter plate and clean the sump area in the bottom of the drawer, clearing seeds, glass, or labels.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm the hose has the required high loop, is not kinked behind the drawer, and the sink connection is open.

fixes.title: Clear the drain connection
fixes.description: If tied to a disposal or air gap, remove sludge or a knockout plug blocking dishwasher discharge.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the sump and hose are clear, test or replace the DishDrawer drain pump that is not moving water.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning the filter and drain path, restart a rinse cycle and confirm the drawer empties before the cycle ends. U4 clears when the control sees normal drain flow.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but U4 persists, the drain pump or drawer wiring has failed; Fisher & Paykel DishDrawer repair uses model-specific parts.

error_codes.meaning: Flood protection activated — water in base
error_codes.detail: The flood system detected water in the DishDrawer base tray and tripped the float/sensor, so the unit stops to prevent a cabinet leak. A damaged drawer seal, loose internal hose, oversudsing, or cracked tub is the usual source.

fixes.title: Drain the base tray
fixes.description: Turn power off and carefully tilt the unit forward over towels to drain water from the base tray.
fixes.warning: Turn off power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect the drawer seal
fixes.description: Check the drawer gasket for tears, flattening, or debris that lets wash water escape into the base.

fixes.title: Check internal hoses
fixes.description: Inspect pump, fill, and drain hose connections inside the drawer for drips or loose clamps.

fixes.title: Reduce suds
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher detergent and correct dose; oversudsing can overflow the drawer into the base.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain the base tray, fix the leak source, dry the sensor area, then restore power. U1 clears when the float/sensor resets and no new water enters the base.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the base refills and no seal, hose, or sudsing issue is visible, an internal crack or drawer component leak needs Fisher & Paykel authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Heating failure — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The DishDrawer did not heat wash water to the programmed temperature, so wash performance and sanitizing are compromised. A failed heater plate/element, inaccurate NTC temperature sensor, or stuck heater relay is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: With power off, disconnect and test the heater for continuity; an open circuit means the heater failed.

fixes.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixes.description: Measure the NTC sensor at room temperature — roughly 6k–7k ohms is expected on many models.

fixes.title: Inspect heater wiring
fixes.description: Check the heater and sensor connectors for heat damage, corrosion, or loose pins in the drawer harness.

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Install the failed heater, sensor, or control component and reseal all drawer penetrations correctly.
fixes.warning: Waterproof sealing is critical.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repairing the heater or sensor, run a full hot program and verify the drawer heats normally without U7 returning. Do not rely on cold-rinse cycles to prove the repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the heater and sensor test correctly but U7 persists, the control board heater relay or wiring is faulty; service is recommended because waterproof resealing is critical.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The Haier washer is not detecting water entering the drum during the fill cycle.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves must be fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to machine.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace valve if failed
fixes.description: Install new valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and straighten the inlet hose. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the code, plug back in, and restart the cycle from the beginning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, inlet valve replacement resolves this.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The control board started the drain pump but the pressure sensor still detected water in the tub after the allowed drain time, so the cycle pauses with E2. In most cases lint, coins, or debris clogging the pump filter is the root cause, followed by a kinked drain hose.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access via lower front panel. Drain and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high-loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the drain filter and hose, unplug the washer for 60 seconds, then run a Spin-only cycle to confirm the tub empties before starting a full wash.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: E4 fires when the control board never receives confirmation from the door interlock switch that the door is both closed and locked. Before allowing water to enter, the board polls the lock circuit; if the closed-and-locked signal is absent or intermittent, it aborts the cycle for safety. Most often the latch simply hasn't engaged or fabric blocks the strike, but a burned-out lock solenoid or a broken latch hook produces the same missing signal even when the door appears shut.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing caught in the gasket.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push firmly until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is worn.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, clear any laundry from the gasket, then push the door closed firmly until it clicks. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds, restore power, and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E4 persists, lock solenoid has failed.
error_codes.models_affected: Haier front-load washers with an electronic door interlock, including the HLC1700AXW
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E4 right at the start of a cycle, often after overloading when a sleeve or towel edge sneaks into the gasket and blocks the latch. Many find that firmly re-closing the door until it clicks clears it; if it returns with the door visibly shut, the latch assembly is the usual suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Motor error — drive motor fault
error_codes.detail: The control board lost proper feedback from the drive motor — it commanded the drum to turn but tachometer or current readings came back wrong, so the cycle stops to protect the motor. Most often the motor control board has failed; worn motor windings or a loose wiring harness are the other usual culprits.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — overloading can trigger motor errors.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor and test winding continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor control board or motor replacement requires professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Water level sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The water level pressure sensor is sending readings outside the expected range, so the control can't tell how much water is in the tub and stops the cycle to avoid overfilling or running the heater dry. A kinked or disconnected pressure hose is common, but a failed sensor is the leading cause.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Locate the small hose connecting the tub to the pressure sensor. Ensure it's not kinked, cracked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect hose from sensor and gently blow — should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor doesn't respond to air pressure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After reseating the pressure hose or replacing the sensor, unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the fault from memory, plug back in, and start a new cycle to verify the level reading.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pressure hose and sensor are fine, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor failure — freezer compartment
error_codes.detail: The freezer NTC thermistor is sending an open or out-of-range signal, so the control can't accurately regulate freezer temperature and may overcool or run warm. A failed sensor, loose connector, or damaged harness near the evaporator is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure the freezer sensor: about 16k ohms at 32°F/0°C or 6k–8k ohms at room temperature is expected.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the thermistor connector and inspect for corrosion, frost intrusion, or a loose pin.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Trace the sensor wiring around the evaporator for breaks or rub damage that can open the circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Clip the old sensor from the evaporator area and install a matching Haier thermistor in the same position.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or reseating its connector, unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly but F1 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed and the board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor on the evaporator is not reporting a valid temperature, so the control can't end defrost correctly and frost can build until airflow is blocked. A failed sensor, loose plug, or heavy frost encasing the sensor is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator and let all evaporator frost melt before testing, so ice is not masking the fault.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Measure the defrost sensor resistance; roughly 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature is expected.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the sensor connector and inspect for moisture or corrosion from repeated frost cycles.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip the failed sensor from the evaporator and install a matching Haier defrost sensor in the same spot.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After defrosting or replacing the sensor, unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for normal frost pattern and temperature recovery.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the new defrost sensor tests correctly but frost returns quickly, the defrost heater, thermal fuse, or control board defrost circuit needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main control board has lost communication with a sub-module or display/UI board, so cooling commands and status feedback may not coordinate correctly. A loose harness connector is common, followed by a failed board communication circuit.

fixes.title: Power cycle the refrigerator
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to reset both boards, then restore power and watch whether F6 returns.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: Inspect and firmly reconnect every harness plug at the main control board and display/sub-module board.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Look for pinched, corroded, or broken wires between boards, especially where the harness passes hinges or panels.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If the harness is good and F6 returns, replace the main control board or failed sub-module board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 10 minutes, restore power, and confirm whether the display and cooling restart normally. If F6 returns, the board connectors and harness must be inspected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating connectors and checking the harness doesn't clear F6, a communication circuit on the main board or display board has failed and needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Door cannot close — door detected open or unlatched during operation
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors the door latch switch and expects a closed signal before and during a cycle. dC fires when the board never sees a confirmed latch engagement or loses it mid-run, indicating the door isn't mechanically seated. This differs from dE, which points to the lock's electronic engagement or the solenoid, and from FL, a lock-failure state. dC typically reflects a physical closing obstruction, misalignment, or a worn latch that won't register.

causes.description: Laundry pinched in the door seal
Boot gasket protruding into the door path

fixes.title: Clear the door seal
fixes.description: Open the door and pull any fabric out of the gasket fold, then close firmly.

fixes.title: Check hinge and striker alignment
fixes.description: Look for door sag; tighten hinge screws and confirm the striker enters the latch slot squarely.

fixes.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: If the door closes but won't register, replace the latch/lock unit.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door fully, reclose it firmly, and press Start/Pause to resume the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door visibly closes flush but dC persists, the latch microswitch or board input needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load Samsung washers with a door latch/lock circuit, including models such as the WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, and WF50BG8300AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report dC appearing right at cycle start, often after overloading when a sleeve or towel corner gets caught in the gasket fold. The quick win is usually opening the door, tucking laundry fully inside, and reclosing firmly; if the door looks flush but the code returns, a sagging hinge or tired latch is the more likely culprit.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — drum cannot reach spin speed safely
error_codes.detail: Ub is the Samsung unbalance code, distinct from the vibration-sensor 'dc'/'UE' family. During the spin ramp, the control board monitors load distribution via the drum's motion and tub sensors. If the mass stays lopsided after several redistribution tumbles, it aborts high-speed spin to shield the suspension rods, dampers, and bearing. Occasional Ub is normal; persistent Ub on evenly loaded washes points to worn suspension components or an off-level cabinet rather than the laundry itself.

causes.description: Items bunched to one side of the drum
Single heavy item like a comforter or rug
Washer not level on the floor

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Pause the cycle, open the door, and spread items evenly around the drum.

fixes.title: Level the washer
fixes.description: Use a bubble level on the top panel and adjust the leveling feet until solid on all four corners.

fixes.title: Inspect suspension dampers
fixes.description: If Ub appears on every load, the shock absorbers/dampers are likely worn and need replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute the laundry, close the door, and press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If even small balanced loads trigger Ub, have a technician check the suspension and drum bearing.
error_codes.models_affected: Front-load and top-load Samsung washers across most series, including front-loaders like WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW and WF50BG8300AV, and top-loaders such as WA50R5400AV, WA45T3200AW and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report Ub with bulky single items like comforters, rugs, or towels that clump wet, and the quick win is usually pausing to spread the load or adding a towel or two to balance it. It can also appear falsely on a washer that has shifted out of level, so rechecking the feet often helps.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalance error variant — repeated failed redistribution attempts
error_codes.detail: U6 fires when the control board's balance sensing detects an off-center load and the automatic redistribution routine — where the drum tumbles and re-attempts to settle the load — fails repeatedly. Rather than risk spinning a lopsided drum at high RPM, the machine drains and halts. It differs from Ub/UE mainly by display generation and by signaling that multiple correction attempts were already exhausted, not a single detection, so it often points to load geometry or aging suspension.

fixes.title: Untangle and redistribute
fixes.description: Separate tangled sheets or straps and arrange items evenly around the drum.

fixes.title: Split bulky loads
fixes.description: Wash comforters alone with two or three towels to balance, or split very large loads in half.

fixes.title: Check suspension and leveling
fixes.description: Rock the cabinet — any wobble means feet need adjustment; constant U6 means dampers are tired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, rebalance the load, and restart the spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent U6 with balanced loads points to suspension or bearing wear that needs professional replacement.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and top-load washers with electronic balance sensing, including models such as the WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF50BG8300AV, WA50R5400AV, and WA54R7600AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report U6 with single bulky items like comforters, bath mats, or a tangle of sheets, and simply redistributing the load usually clears it on the next spin. If it keeps appearing even with evenly balanced, moderate loads, worn dampers or an unlevel floor are the usual culprits.

error_codes.meaning: Water temperature sensor (thermistor) error — heater thermistor out of range
error_codes.detail: tE1 fires when the control board reads the heater-circuit thermistor as open, shorted, or outside its expected range (roughly 10k–40k ohms varying with water temperature). Because the board can no longer confirm water temperature, it aborts heated cycles as a safety measure. This differs from tE2 and tE3, which point to other temperature sensors, and from HE, which flags a heater or heating-performance fault rather than a sensor-reading failure.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient sensor glitch, then retry a warm cycle.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect power, access the heater at the rear lower tub, and measure thermistor resistance — an open or near-zero reading confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before probing the heater circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor/heater assembly
fixes.description: On many Samsung models the thermistor is part of the heating element assembly; replace as a unit if integrated.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heater circuit — confirm power is disconnected before touching terminals.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. If tE1 returns on heated cycles, the sensor needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if you're not comfortable testing resistance on the mains-powered heater circuit.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung washers with a heating circuit and integrated thermistor — primarily front-load models such as the WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, and WF50BG8300AV, though heated top-loaders can also report it
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tE1 appearing only on warm or hot cycles while cold washes run fine. A brief power cycle sometimes clears a transient glitch, but a corroded or loose harness plug at the heater is a frequent culprit worth reseating before replacing parts.

error_codes.meaning: Duct/outlet temperature sensor error (washer-dryer combo models)
error_codes.detail: tE2 reports a fault in the secondary temperature sensor rather than the main wash-heater thermistor tracked by tE1. On combo and steam models this thermistor monitors the duct or condensing path, so the control board fires tE2 when it reads an open circuit, a short, or a temperature value outside the plausible range. It distinguishes itself from tE1/tE3 by pointing to that secondary sensing location, which is more exposed to lint and condensation.

causes.description: Lint or moisture on the sensor (combo models)

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes; a one-off reading glitch will clear.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor harness
fixes.description: Trace the wiring to the duct/secondary sensor and reseat the connector; look for green corrosion or pinched wires.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace the secondary thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance at room temperature; replace if open or shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the unit for 5 minutes and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor tests good and tE2 persists, the main board needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: Washer-dryer combo and steam-capable models with a secondary duct/condensing-path thermistor, such as the WV60M9900AV; standard front-load and top-load washers without a drying circuit rarely show this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code surfacing partway through drying or steam cycles rather than at startup, and a simple power cycle sometimes clears a one-off glitch. Lint buildup or moisture on the sensor can occasionally cause a false trigger, so cleaning and reseating the connector is often the quick win before replacing the thermistor.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser/steam temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: tE3 is the third thermistor fault in Samsung's tE family, monitoring the sensor on the condensing duct or steam-generator circuit. The control board reads the thermistor's resistance and flags tE3 when the value falls outside the expected range—open, shorted, or drifting—typically from a failed sensor, loose harness, or scale buildup. Steam and drying functions are halted to prevent overheating. Unlike tE1 and tE2, which watch the main wash-water thermistors, tE3 is specific to the steam/condenser sensor.

causes.description: Scale buildup on the steam generator sensor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and rerun the cycle to rule out a transient fault.

fixes.title: Reseat the sensor connector
fixes.description: Access the steam generator/condenser area and reseat the thermistor plug; clean any scale or lint off the sensor body.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Test resistance and replace if open/shorted; sensors are inexpensive and usually two screws plus a plug.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. Avoid steam cycles until the sensor is fixed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If steam cycles keep faulting after sensor replacement, the steam generator itself needs professional service.
error_codes.models_affected: Steam-equipped and washer/dryer combo models with a condensing duct or steam generator, such as the WF50BG8300AV and WV60M9900AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tE3 appearing when starting a steam or dry cycle rather than a plain wash, and a simple power cycle sometimes clears a transient trigger. Reseating or cleaning scale off the sensor often resolves it, though a failed thermistor is the most frequent underlying cause.

error_codes.meaning: Heating phase — estimated time over 2 hours (display status, often mistaken for an error)
error_codes.detail: '2H' is a time-remaining status, not a fault code: the control board estimates cycle duration and, when the projected time exceeds 99 minutes, it substitutes an hour count such as 2H, 3H or 4H. It appears most on sanitize, steam or heavily soiled heated cycles where the heater must raise water temperature. Unlike tE1 or HE, which flag an actual thermistor or heating-circuit failure, 2H only indicates a long heat-up estimate that should count down as the water warms.

causes.description: Normal display for a long heated cycle
Scaled or weak heating element extending heat-up time
Thermistor reading low so the heater runs long

fixes.title: Let the cycle run
fixes.description: If the time eventually counts down below 99 minutes, the display is behaving normally — no repair needed.

fixes.title: Compare cycle duration
fixes.description: Time a sanitize cycle against the manual's stated length; more than ~30 minutes over suggests a weak heater.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Measure element resistance (roughly 10–30 ohms); replace if open or badly scaled.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heater — unplug the washer before testing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: No reset needed for normal operation; if stuck, unplug for 5 minutes and rerun a shorter cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If heated cycles never finish or water stays cold, have the heater and board tested professionally.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung front-load and select top-load washers with a heated sanitize or steam cycle, including models such as the WF45T6000AW, WF45R6100AP, WF42H5000AW, WF50BG8300AV and WV60M9900AV
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report seeing 2H at the start of sanitize or steam cycles and worrying it is an error, though it usually converts to normal minutes once heating progresses. A frequent false alarm is cold incoming water, which lengthens the estimate; the quick win most report is simply letting the cycle run and confirming it eventually counts down.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error (older Samsung models) — water not draining in time
error_codes.detail: E2 fires when the control board runs the drain phase but the pressure sensor still reports water in the tub after the drain timer expires. The board expects the level to fall below an empty threshold within a set window; if it doesn't, it flags a drain fault. This distinguishes it from a fill or leak code — the machine drained too slowly or not at all, most often from a restricted filter, hose, or pump rather than a sensor miscalibration.

fixes.title: Clean the debris filter
fixes.description: Open the lower front access panel, drain residual water via the small hose, and clean the filter thoroughly.
fixes.warning: Several liters of water may come out — have towels and a shallow pan ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Straighten any kinks and confirm the hose isn't pushed more than 6 inches into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: Run a Spin/Drain cycle and listen for the pump hum; a silent or grinding pump needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before replacing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Rinse+Spin cycle to confirm draining.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but water still stands in the drum, the pump or pressure switch needs professional testing.
error_codes.models_affected: Older Samsung washers with two-character LED displays (both top-load and front-load) that predate the 5C/5E code convention; none of the current known-model list uses the E2 designation.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E2 appearing at the end of a wash with water left standing in the drum, and clearing the debris filter often resolves it right away. A partially kinked drain hose or lint-clogged filter is a frequent culprit, so it's worth checking those before suspecting the pump.

error_codes.meaning: Overflow / water level error (older Samsung models)
error_codes.detail: E3 on legacy Samsung washers indicates the water level rose above the safe limit — the overflow equivalent of OC/OE on newer displays. The washer drains automatically as a safety measure. The usual culprits are a leaking inlet valve or a faulted pressure sensor.

causes.description: Water inlet valve not closing fully
Pressure sensor or hose fault giving a false low reading
Blocked pressure chamber causing late level detection

fixes.title: Watch for fill with power off
fixes.description: Turn the washer off at the wall with supply valves open; if the drum still fills, the inlet valve is mechanically stuck and must be replaced.

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: Inspect the thin air hose from the tub to the pressure sensor for kinks, splits, or detergent sludge.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the top panel.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: If the hose is clear and the valve closes, the level sensor is misreporting and should be swapped.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the water supply, unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. Keep supply valves closed until the cause is found.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Overfilling risks flooding — if you can't identify the cause quickly, shut off the supply valves and call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Air circulation error — insufficient airflow detected through the drum
error_codes.detail: The control board interprets 1 AC when the drum's temperature climbs faster than a properly vented load should allow. Because Samsung infers airflow from the rate of temperature rise rather than a direct flow sensor, any restriction—packed lint screen, clogged duct, or a stalled blower—causes heat to accumulate near the thermistor and trips the code. It fires preemptively to protect the machine, distinguishing it from cooling-related codes like Clg or heater faults such as HE, which point to different subsystems.

causes.description: Clogged exhaust vent or duct run
Lint screen or screen housing packed with lint
Crushed or kinked flexible duct behind the dryer

fixes.title: Clean the lint screen and housing
fixes.description: Remove the screen, wash it with warm soapy water, and vacuum the slot it sits in.

fixes.title: Clean the full vent run
fixes.description: Disconnect the duct from the dryer and brush/vacuum the entire run to the outside hood; confirm the exterior flap opens freely.

fixes.title: Inspect the blower wheel
fixes.description: With power disconnected, remove the front panel and check the blower for lint wads, socks, or a wheel slipping on its shaft.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer (or shut off gas and unplug) before opening the cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the airflow restriction, then unplug for 5 minutes and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the vent run is long, routed through walls, or 1 AC returns after cleaning, hire a vent cleaning service or technician.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers that display alphanumeric fault codes, including many DVE/DVG electric and gas models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 1 AC appearing partway through a cycle, especially with longer or wall-routed vent runs and after several months without a deep vent cleaning. A packed lint screen or a kinked duct behind the dryer is a frequent false-alarm trigger, and many find a full vent cleaning clears the code quickly.

error_codes.meaning: Clog warning — exhaust duct restriction detected (early D80/D90 indicator)
error_codes.detail: Clg fires when the dryer's vent sensor detects that exhaust airflow has dropped below an acceptable threshold during operation. The control board compares measured back-pressure and airflow against expected values; sustained restriction triggers the warning. Unlike the D80/D90/D95 codes that display a specific restriction percentage, Clg is a simpler early-warning flag on models that show a text alert rather than a numeric readout. It differs from heater faults like HE, which relate to the heating circuit rather than airflow.

causes.description: Lint buildup in the exhaust duct
Exterior vent hood blocked or flap stuck
Duct run too long or with too many elbows
Crushed transition duct behind the dryer

fixes.title: Clean the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect the duct and clean the full run with a rotary brush kit; vacuum from both ends.

fixes.title: Check the exterior hood
fixes.description: Go outside while the dryer runs — strong, warm airflow should exit. Clear the flap of lint, nests, or screening.

fixes.title: Rerun the Vent Blockage test
fixes.description: Use the dryer's vent check mode (empty drum, Time Dry per the manual) to confirm the restriction is cleared.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run the vent blockage test cycle; the warning clears when airflow is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If Clg persists after a thorough cleaning, the in-wall duct run may need professional vent service.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dryers with a vent sensor and airflow self-check, typically models that also report D80/D90/D95 percentages (both electric and gas), roughly 2015 onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report Clg appearing after clothes take noticeably longer to dry or the drum runs hot. A quick win many describe is cleaning the exterior vent hood and the full duct run, which often clears it; false triggers can occur with a partly stuck flap or a crushed transition hose behind the unit.

error_codes.meaning: No gas — burner failed to ignite (gas models)
error_codes.detail: The dryer's igniter glowed but the flame sensor never detected ignition, or gas flow is absent. Samsung gas dryers retry ignition a few times, then lock out with nG. The most common causes are a closed gas valve, a failed igniter, or a worn flame sensor.

causes.description: Gas supply valve closed or supply interrupted

fixes.title: Confirm gas supply
fixes.description: Verify the shutoff valve behind the dryer is open and other gas appliances in the home work.

fixes.title: Observe the igniter
fixes.description: With the front panel removed, start a heat cycle and watch the burner: no glow means a failed igniter; glow without flame points to gas valve coils.
fixes.warning: Gas appliance — shut off the gas valve and unplug before any disassembly; if you smell gas, stop and ventilate.

fixes.title: Replace flame sensor or valve coils
fixes.description: If the igniter glows and shuts off without flame, replace the valve coils; if the flame lights then drops, replace the radiant flame sensor.
fixes.warning: Do not bypass safety sensors. Leak-test fittings with soapy water after reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore gas supply, unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, and restart a heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any persistent gas smell, or uncertainty handling gas fittings, means stop and call a qualified gas appliance technician.

error_codes.meaning: Duct sensor blockage — vent/moisture sensor reading abnormal due to restricted exhaust
error_codes.detail: PS fires when the duct-mounted sensor reports readings outside its expected range during a cycle, which the control board interprets as restricted exhaust or a sensor-circuit fault. Lint film on the sensor face skews its readings first; a clogged vent run keeps moist air lingering past it; and a chafed or loose harness can mimic the same abnormal signal. Unlike cooling codes such as Clg, PS points specifically at airflow sensing rather than a completed or aborted cool-down phase.

fixes.title: Clean the sensor and duct
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, locate the sensor in the internal exhaust duct, and gently clean it along with the surrounding duct.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Clean the full vent run
fixes.description: Brush out the duct from the dryer to the exterior hood to eliminate the restriction triggering the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace the duct sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't clear PS, test continuity and replace the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, unplug for 5 minutes and restart the dryer.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PS returns after sensor cleaning and a full vent cleanout, have the sensor circuit and board tested.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung electric and gas dryers with an internal duct-mounted airflow/moisture sensor, including models such as DVE45R6100C, DVE45T6000W, DV42H5000EW, DVE50BG8300V, and DVG45T3200W
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PS appearing mid-cycle after months without vent cleaning, especially when loads dry slowly or the cabinet feels hot. A quick wipe of the sensor plus a full vent brush-out often clears it, and a fair number of cases turn out to be nothing more than a lint-caked sensor rather than a failed part.

error_codes.meaning: Noise sensor error — abnormal sound/vibration signal from the wash system
error_codes.detail: The nE code fires when the control board reads an abnormal sound or vibration signature from the noise (knock) sensor mounted near the pump and motor. Rather than a flow or level fault, it flags acoustic anomalies — cavitation from insufficient water, hard debris striking the impeller, worn pump bearings, or a faulty sensor circuit. It differs from flow codes like 3C or leak-related 9E in that it monitors sound patterns, not pump feedback or water presence directly.

causes.description: Debris (glass, bone, pit) in the circulation pump
Low water level causing pump cavitation

fixes.title: Inspect the sump and filters
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack, filters, and check the sump for hard debris; clear anything around the impeller area.
fixes.warning: Glass shards may be present — wear gloves.

fixes.title: Verify the fill level
fixes.description: Start a cycle and check water reaches the base of the heating area; weak fill means inlet valve or supply problems causing cavitation noise.

fixes.title: Check or replace the noise sensor
fixes.description: With power off, reseat the sensor connector; replace the sensor if the error persists with a clean, properly filling machine.
fixes.warning: Unplug or switch off the breaker before removing panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 60 seconds, then rerun a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Grinding or screeching from the pump along with nE means pump replacement — a job best left to a technician.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers equipped with a noise/knock sensor on the wash system; the code is not specific to any single model but can appear across full-size models with this sensor.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report nE after a glass breaks or a fruit pit slips past the filter, and clearing the sump often resolves it. Weak household water pressure can be a false trigger via cavitation, so it's worth confirming the fill before suspecting the sensor.

error_codes.meaning: Pump/zone wash fault — distribution motor or pump circuit error
error_codes.detail: PC fires when the control board cannot confirm the zone-wash distribution motor has reached its commanded position, or when it reads current draw suggesting a binding, corroded, or open pump/motor circuit. Unlike drain-related faults such as nE, PC points to the wash-side distribution valve and pump wiring rather than water level or drainage. A common root cause is long-term moisture corroding the connector at the pump assembly beneath the tub, mimicking a failed motor.

causes.description: Corroded connector or harness at the pump assembly

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the breaker for 5 minutes — a position-sense glitch can clear.

fixes.title: Inspect connectors under the tub
fixes.description: Tip the unit (water supply off, drained) and inspect the pump and distribution motor connectors for green corrosion; clean or repair the harness.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before working under the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Replace the distribution motor
fixes.description: If the valve doesn't rotate during a cycle, replace the distribution (zone wash) motor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power at the breaker for 5 minutes, then restart a normal cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Corroded wiring under the tub or a suspected board fault should be handled by a professional.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung dishwashers with zone/vario wash distribution systems, including models such as DW80R5060US, DW80K5050US, DW80B6060US, and DW80CG4051SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report PC appearing mid-cycle, sometimes after years of use, and a breaker power-cycle occasionally clears a one-off sense glitch. Where the code returns, many trace it to green corrosion on the under-tub pump connector rather than the motor itself, so inspecting that harness first is often the quick win.

error_codes.meaning: Drain-related sensor fault — drainage not confirmed by the water level sensor
error_codes.detail: C5 on Samsung dishwashers (notably Waterwall and some DW80 series) flags a drainage/level-sensing inconsistency: the level sensor still sees water after the drain phase. It overlaps heavily with 5C in cause — check drainage first, sensor second.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or air-gap/disposal port blocked

fixes.title: Clean filters and sump
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the filter stack and clear the sump of food debris.

fixes.title: Check the drain path
fixes.description: Confirm the hose has a high loop, isn't kinked, and the garbage disposal knockout plug was removed if newly installed.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: Listen for the pump at the end of cycle; a silent pump with standing water needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before replacing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 60 seconds after clearing the drain path, then rerun a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water still stands after the filter, hose, and pump check out, the level sensor and board need professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Fan speed error — a cooling fan is not running at the commanded RPM
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors RPM feedback pulses from each cooling fan and expects rotation matching its commanded speed. Code 26E fires when a fan — most often the freezer evaporator or condenser fan — reports no rotation or a speed well off target. Unlike temperature codes (21E/22E) that flag a warming compartment, 26E specifically identifies mechanical or electrical fan failure. Ice from a failing defrost cycle seizing the evaporator fan blade is the classic trigger, distinct from a motor or harness fault.

causes.description: Fan blade obstructed by debris or shifted shroud

fixes.title: Force defrost or full thaw
fixes.description: Unplug the unit (food relocated) and let it thaw 8–24 hours, or run forced defrost from the diagnostic menu to melt ice off the fan.

fixes.title: Check the fan for obstruction
fixes.description: Remove the relevant panel and confirm the blade spins freely by hand; clear debris and reseat the shroud.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but doesn't run when powered, swap the fan motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the fault, unplug for 2 minutes to reset the board and let the unit restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans repeatedly ice over, the defrost system needs professional diagnosis — fan replacement alone won't fix it.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung French-door and side-by-side refrigerators with feedback-monitored fan motors, including models such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report 26E appearing alongside weak cooling or a compartment slowly warming, and many find the evaporator fan encased in frost after opening the rear interior panel. A thaw or forced defrost often clears it temporarily, but if the code returns within days that usually points to an underlying defrost problem rather than the fan itself.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fill tube heater error
error_codes.detail: 33E fires when the control board reads an open or out-of-range circuit on the fill tube heater, the small heater that prevents residual water in the fill tube from freezing between ice cycles. The board senses no valid resistance or driver feedback and flags the fault. Unlike 34E (ice maker/defrost related) or 41E/42E fill and sensor faults, 33E points specifically at the fill tube heater element or its wiring, not the water valve, auger, or temperature sensors.

causes.description: Harness or connector fault to the heater
Ice blockage stressing the heater circuit

fixes.title: Thaw the fill tube
fixes.description: Power off the ice maker, and gently warm the fill tube area (hair dryer on low) to clear any ice plug.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before using any heat source near plastic parts; keep the dryer moving.

fixes.title: Test the heater circuit
fixes.description: With the unit unplugged, measure continuity across the fill tube heater; an open reading confirms failure.

fixes.title: Replace the heater/fill tube assembly
fixes.description: On most models the heater comes with the fill tube assembly.
parts: Samsung ice maker fill tube heater ($20–$50)

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press and hold Power Freeze + Power Cool (or per your model's manual) for 8–10 seconds to reset, or unplug for 2 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the heater tests fine but 33E persists, the control board output should be checked professionally.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung refrigerators with automatic ice makers, particularly French-door and side-by-side models such as the RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code alongside the ice maker quietly stopping while cooling stays normal, often after a fill tube ices over. Gently thawing the tube sometimes clears it temporarily, but a heater that has failed open usually brings the code back until the fill tube heater assembly is replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: The control board monitors resistance from the ice maker's thermistor to time harvest and monitor compartment temperature. Code 34E fires when that reading goes open, shorted, or outside its expected range, so the board halts ice production to avoid mistimed harvests. Frost intrusion is the frequent culprit, corroding the connector or encasing the sensor. It differs from defrost-sensor faults like 41E/42E, which involve the evaporator circuit, and from 33E, which points more broadly at ice maker function.

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker compartment
fixes.description: Use forced defrost mode (Fridge + a model-specific button) or thaw manually to clear frost around the sensor.

fixes.title: Inspect the connector
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the ice maker, and check the harness plug for corrosion; clean and reseat.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the ice maker.

fixes.title: Replace the ice maker assembly
fixes.description: Samsung sells the sensor only as part of the ice maker assembly on most models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes after the repair; the code clears once the sensor reads in range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring icing that keeps killing the sensor indicates a sealing/defrost defect best handled by a technician familiar with Samsung ice maker service bulletins.
error_codes.models_affected: French-door and side-by-side models with an in-fridge or in-door ice maker, including known units such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code alongside Samsung's well-known ice maker frosting problems, often after ice stops dispensing. A forced defrost or manual thaw frequently clears it temporarily, but many find the code returns until the connector is cleaned or the assembly is replaced, which can hint at an underlying sealing issue.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker (ice room) fan error — ice compartment fan not rotating
error_codes.detail: 41E fires when the control board sees no rotation feedback from the ice room fan, the fan that pushes cold air through the door-mounted ice compartment. The board monitors the fan's Hall-sensor signal; when RPM drops to zero or reads no pulse, it logs the fault. Frost jamming the blade is the usual trigger, distinguishing it from cooling-fan codes like 42E or defrost-sensor faults, which involve different sensors and airflow paths rather than the ice room itself.

causes.description: Ice room fan frozen in frost
Air duct gasket leak letting moist air in and frosting the fan

fixes.title: Force defrost
fixes.description: Enter forced defrost (Fd) from the diagnostic panel to melt frost in the ice room, or thaw the compartment manually.

fixes.title: Check the fan
fixes.description: With power off, remove the ice maker and confirm the fan blade turns freely; clear ice and debris.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the ice maker assembly.

fixes.title: Replace the ice room fan
fixes.description: If free but not running, replace the fan motor; also reseat the duct gaskets to slow re-frosting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After defrosting, unplug for 2 minutes; the code clears when fan feedback returns.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost rebuilds within weeks, the ice room sealing needs professional repair — repeated fan swaps won't hold.
error_codes.models_affected: French-door and door-ice models with a door-mounted ice room, such as the RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, and RF23M8070SR
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after ice production slows or the dispenser stops, often following a period of humid weather or frequent door openings. A forced defrost frequently clears it, but if frost returns within weeks it usually points to a duct gasket leak rather than a bad fan.

error_codes.meaning: Ice room damper/sensor error — ice compartment not maintaining temperature
error_codes.detail: 42E fires when the control board loses valid feedback from the ice room circuit — the damper that regulates cold airflow into the door ice compartment fails to move as commanded, or its sensor reads outside the expected range. The board flags that the ice compartment can't hold its target temperature, so ice clumps and production falls. Unlike 41E or 34E, which point at other cooling zones or defrost circuits, 42E is specific to the door-mounted ice room's damper and sensor.

causes.description: Ice room damper frozen or failed
Ice room sensor out of range
Frost accumulation in the air duct

fixes.title: Defrost the ice room
fixes.description: Run forced defrost or thaw manually to free the damper and clear duct frost.

fixes.title: Test damper operation
fixes.description: In diagnostic mode, command the damper and listen for movement; a silent damper after thawing has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing door/ice room panels.

fixes.title: Replace the ice room sensor
fixes.description: If the damper works, test the sensor's resistance and replace if open or shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes after repair to reset the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door-mounted ice room disassembly is fiddly and parts are model-specific — call a pro if you're unsure of your exact model's layout.
error_codes.models_affected: Samsung French-door and side-by-side refrigerators with an in-door ice room (models such as RF28R7351SG, RF263BEAESR, RF23M8070SR, RS27T5200SR, and RF27T5201SR), plus similar models using a door-mounted ice compartment fed by a metered air damper.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report this code after noticing clumped or slushy ice, and it often follows heavy frost buildup in the ice compartment duct. A forced or manual defrost frequently clears it as a quick win, though a code that returns soon after usually points to a stuck damper or a drifting sensor.

error_codes.meaning: Compressor lock error — compressor failed to start or stalled
error_codes.detail: 84E means the inverter board detected the compressor drawing locked-rotor current or failing to reach speed. This is one of the more serious Samsung refrigerator codes: causes range from a failing inverter board (cheap) to a seized compressor (expensive).

causes.description: Loose or burnt connector between inverter and compressor
Low line voltage or power quality issue

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to reset the inverter; if the compressor restarts and runs, monitor for recurrence.

fixes.title: Inspect inverter connections
fixes.description: With the unit unplugged, check the inverter board (near the compressor) for burnt connectors or bulged components; reseat all plugs.
fixes.warning: Capacitors on the inverter board can hold charge — wait several minutes after unplugging.

fixes.title: Replace the inverter board
fixes.description: An inverter swap is the cost-effective first repair; if the new board still throws 84E, the compressor itself has failed.
fixes.warning: Mains voltage present at the inverter — unplug before any work.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. If 84E returns, do not keep power-cycling — diagnose the inverter.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new inverter board doesn't fix it, compressor replacement requires an EPA-certified sealed-system technician — get a quote before committing, as it can approach replacement cost.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor error — water temperature sensor reading out of range
error_codes.detail: The tE code fires when the control board reads the heating thermistor's resistance as open, shorted, or outside the expected range, leaving it unable to verify or regulate water temperature. Heated and steam cycles abort as a safety measure. Unlike LE (motor/rotor) or PE (pressure/water-level sensing), tE is specific to the temperature-sensing circuit — typically the thermistor at the heating element, its connector, or the board's sensing input.

causes.description: Loose or corroded connector at the heater

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to clear a transient sensor fault, then rerun a warm cycle.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, access the heater through the rear panel, and measure thermistor resistance — roughly 30k–50k ohms at room temperature on most LG models; open or zero confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before probing the mains-powered heater circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect the sensor from the heater assembly and swap it; if integrated, replace the heating element assembly.
fixes.warning: Confirm power is disconnected before touching heater terminals.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart. If tE returns on heated cycles, the sensor needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor still throws tE, the main control board's sensing circuit needs professional diagnosis.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load and steam-capable washers with a heating element and integrated thermistor, such as the WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, and WM8100HVA; top-load models without a heater are far less likely to show tE
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report tE appearing partway through warm, hot, or steam cycles while cold washes still run fine. A loose or corroded connector at the heater can trigger it falsely, so reseating that plug is a frequent quick win before replacing parts.

error_codes.meaning: Overcurrent error — excessive current detected in the motor drive circuit
error_codes.detail: F6 fires when the main board's inverter power module measures motor-drive current above its safe threshold, tripping a hardware protection to keep the IPM from failing. Unlike LE (locked/overloaded motor) or CE (a sustained overcurrent LG flags separately), F6 is an instantaneous overcurrent spike in the drive stage. It typically points to a shorted stator winding, a Hall-sensor timing fault feeding the drive bad commutation data, a harness short, or degradation within the board's own power section.

causes.description: Hall sensor fault confusing the drive timing
Wiring harness short between board and motor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes; a single overcurrent trip from a momentary jam can clear.

fixes.title: Test the stator windings
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, remove the rear cover and rotor, and measure resistance between stator terminals — readings should be equal across all three pairs (typically 5–15 ohms); a shorted pair condemns the stator.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before removing the rotor; the rotor bolt is torqued tight.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If the motor tests balanced and the harness is intact, the board's drive section has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug and wait several minutes before handling the board — capacitors hold charge.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. Repeated F6 trips mean stop and diagnose — continued retries can destroy the board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Distinguishing a bad stator from a bad board requires meter work on live components — most owners should call a technician.
error_codes.models_affected: LG direct-drive washers, both front-load and top-load models (for example the WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM4500HBA, WM8100HVA front-loaders and the WT7300CW, WT7900HBA top-loaders)
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F6 appearing mid-cycle, often during spin ramp-up when motor current peaks, and a single trip sometimes clears after a ten-minute power cycle. A one-time jam or heavy unbalanced load can trigger it falsely, but repeated F6 codes usually indicate a genuine stator or board fault and shouldn't be retried repeatedly.

error_codes.meaning: Child lock active — controls are locked, not an error
error_codes.detail: CL is a status indicator, not a fault code. When Child Lock is active the control board deliberately ignores all panel input except the unlock combination, preventing children from altering a running cycle. The board displays CL to confirm the lock state. It differs from true error codes because no sensor or component failure is involved — the panel is simply electronically disabled until the correct button combo is held to clear it.

causes.description: Stuck control panel button keeping the combo pressed
Control board glitch latching the lock

fixes.title: Deactivate Child Lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Child Lock button (or the two buttons marked with the lock symbol, often Temp + Options) for 3–5 seconds until CL disappears.

fixes.title: Power cycle if unresponsive
fixes.description: Unplug for 60 seconds and try the unlock combination again after the panel reboots.

fixes.title: Check for a stuck button
fixes.description: Press each panel button — any that feel sticky or don't click may be holding the lock combo; clean around the button or replace the panel if jammed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Child Lock button combination for 3–5 seconds; refer to the lock-symbol label on your control panel.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the panel stays locked after a power cycle and the correct unlock combo, the control panel or board needs service.
error_codes.models_affected: Virtually all LG washers with a digital control panel, both front-load and top-load models (e.g. WM3900HWA, WM4000HBA, WM3400CW, WT7300CW, WM8100HVA, WM4500HBA, WT7900HBA), since the Child Lock feature is standard across the lineup.
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report seeing CL after unresponsive buttons and assume a breakdown, when the lock was usually toggled by accident during cleaning or by a curious child. Holding the lock-symbol combination for a few seconds typically clears it right away; if it returns immediately, a sticky button may be holding the combo down.

error_codes.meaning: Dry heater error — drying heater fault (washer-dryer combo models)
error_codes.detail: The dHE code fires when the control board monitoring the dry phase sees the condensing/drying heater fail to raise air temperature within the expected time, or when the duct thermistor reads outside its valid resistance range. Unlike tE, which flags a general wash-water temperature sensor fault, dHE is specific to the drying circuit — an open heater element, tripped thermal fuse, or airflow starved by lint. Wash functions typically continue; only the dry portion aborts and reports the fault.

fixes.title: Clean the drying duct
fixes.description: Unplug the unit and clean lint from the air intake, condensing duct, and any accessible filter — restricted airflow overheats and trips the circuit.

fixes.title: Test the dry heater and thermal cutoff
fixes.description: With power disconnected, measure continuity across the drying heater and its thermal fuse; an open reading on either confirms the failure.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heating circuit — unplug the washer before testing.

fixes.title: Replace the duct thermistor
fixes.description: If the heater and fuse test good, measure and replace the duct temperature sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart a dry cycle. If a thermal fuse blew, find the airflow cause before replacing it.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fuse keeps blowing after a thorough lint cleanout, a technician should check the blower and heater control relay.
error_codes.models_affected: LG washer-dryer combo units with an integrated drying heater, such as the WM3900HWA and WM4000HBA ventless combo series; standard washers without a dry cycle do not throw this code
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report dHE appearing part-way through or at the start of the dry phase while wash cycles still finish normally. A thorough lint cleanout of the intake and condensing duct is the most frequent quick win, since restricted airflow can trip the thermal cutoff and mimic a failed heater.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected (Aqua error) — water sensed in the base pan
error_codes.detail: LG washers with leak detection have a sensor in the base pan; AE means water reached it. The washer stops and may drain as a safeguard. The leak can be anywhere — hose clamps, the boot gasket, the dispenser, or a cracked tub — and gravity carries it to the pan.

causes.description: Loose internal hose clamp or split hose
Door boot gasket torn or folded
Drain pump housing or tub seal leaking

fixes.title: Dry the base pan
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, tip it back carefully or remove the lower panel, and dry the base pan so the sensor resets — then find the source before running it again.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before reaching into the base.

fixes.title: Run a test cycle and watch
fixes.description: With panels off and power restored, run a short cycle and watch with a flashlight for the drip source during fill, wash, and drain phases.
fixes.warning: Observe only — keep hands clear of moving and live parts.

fixes.title: Repair the source
fixes.description: Reseat hose clamps, replace a torn boot gasket, or replace the leaking pump housing as found.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Dry the base pan completely, fix the leak source, then unplug for 5 minutes before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you can't locate the leak source after observing a full cycle, call a technician — running a leaking washer risks floor damage and electrical faults.

error_codes.meaning: No heat / airflow fault — dryer runs but cannot maintain heating
error_codes.detail: nF flags that the dryer detected inadequate heat rise, which on LG dryers is almost always an airflow problem rather than the heater itself. Restricted exhaust causes thermal cutoffs to cycle the heat off, and the board reports the no-heat condition.

causes.description: Lint filter or internal duct packed with lint
Thermal cutoff or high-limit thermostat tripped/failed
Heating element or gas ignition fault

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter and vent run
fixes.description: Wash the lint screen, then disconnect and brush out the full exhaust duct to the exterior hood.

fixes.title: Test thermostats and thermal cutoff
