fixes.description: Until fixed, move meat/fish out of the drawer to the main compartment shelves where temperature control still works.

fixes.title: Replace the drawer sensor
fixes.description: Behind the drawer's rear trim panel; clip and connector replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing trim panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power. The code clears once the control reads a plausible drawer temperature.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Because main cooling is unaffected, this repair isn't urgent — bundle it with any other service visit if you're not DIYing it.
error_codes.models_affected: Bosch refrigerators equipped with a VitaFresh or chiller temperature-controlled drawer, typically mid-range and upper-tier French-door and bottom-freezer models
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report E03 appearing after the drawer struggles to hold temperature, sometimes noticing produce or meat spoiling or freezing sooner than expected. A quick power-reset occasionally clears it if the reading was a transient glitch, but a persistent code usually points to the sensor or its connector.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator compartment sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The fridge-compartment temperature sensor is returning an implausible signal, so the control can't regulate the compartment accurately. Miele units typically keep cooling on a fallback program, but temperature drifts. Miele documents this as a fault requiring sensor access behind interior panels.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch the unit off, wait 60 seconds, and switch it back on — transient sensor glitches clear with a restart.

fixes.title: Monitor with a thermometer
fixes.description: Confirm whether the compartment actually holds 37–40°F. Drift alongside F1 confirms a real sensor fault.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: Miele's compartment sensors sit behind interior panels that aren't designed for customer removal.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. If F1 returns, note the actual compartment temperature for the technician.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele recommends authorized service for sensor faults — interior panel access without the right procedure risks damaging the liner, and authorized parts keep the (long) Miele warranty intact.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The sensor on the evaporator coil is reading open, shorted, or out of range. The control uses this sensor to time defrost cycles, so a failure leads to frost buildup on the evaporator and gradually weakening cooling. A unit that cools poorly with F2 showing has likely already iced up.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on.

fixes.title: Check for icing symptoms
fixes.description: Weak cooling, a rear interior wall of frost, or a fan working loudly all suggest the defrost timing has already been affected.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Evaporator sensor access requires opening the cooling-compartment panels — an authorized-service job on Miele units.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. If cooling is already weak, a full manual defrost (unit off, doors open, 24h) restores temporary performance until service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Evaporator-side work on Miele refrigeration is authorized-service territory. If cooling has degraded, don't delay — running iced-up stresses the compressor.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment air sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The air-temperature sensor in the freezer compartment is returning an implausible value. The control can't verify the freezer is holding temperature, so it runs a fallback program. Food safety is the practical risk — the display can no longer be trusted to reflect real freezer temperature.

causes.description: Connector/harness fault on the cold side

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on.

fixes.title: Verify with a thermometer
fixes.description: Place a thermometer in the freezer — it should hold 0°F (-18°C). Use this, not the display, while the fault is active.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Sensor replacement requires freezer panel access per Miele's service procedure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. Keep a thermometer in the freezer until the sensor is replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the freezer can't verifiably hold 0°F, prioritize the service visit and avoid loading new unfrozen food in the meantime.

error_codes.meaning: Fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: An interior circulation fan isn't running as commanded. Cold air stops moving evenly, so some areas run cold while others warm up — the classic symptom is temperature stratification between shelves. Ice jamming the fan or a failed fan motor are the documented causes.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on; listen for the fan resuming.

fixes.title: Manual defrost if iced
fixes.description: If you hear the fan straining or chirping, a full defrost (off, doors open, 24h) frees an ice-jammed blade.

fixes.title: Book fan replacement
fixes.description: If the fan stays silent after a thaw and reset, the motor has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. A 24-hour manual defrost is the one effective self-help step before service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Repeated fan icing means the defrost system needs diagnosis too — mention the history when booking Miele service so both get fixed in one visit.

error_codes.meaning: Open thermal sensor — cavity temperature not detected
error_codes.detail: GE documents F1 as an open thermal sensor: during convection, combination, or roast cooking the control expects the cavity to reach temperature within 15 minutes, and an open sensor circuit means it never sees the rise. Microwave-only cooking may still work; convection functions stop.

fixes.title: Clear and retry
fixes.description: Press Cancel/Off to clear the code, then retry the convection function once — a one-off glitch clears.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch the breaker (or unplug the unit) for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: The thermal sensor mounts on the cavity behind the outer case — microwave case removal carries shock risk even unplugged.
fixes.warning: Microwave capacitors store a lethal charge after unplugging. Do not open the case.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear the display, or power-cycle at the breaker for 60 seconds. F1 returning during convection cooking confirms the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: All internal microwave repairs are professional territory — the high-voltage capacitor can hold a lethal charge long after unplugging. The fix itself (a sensor swap) is quick for a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted thermal sensor
error_codes.detail: The opposite of F1: the cavity thermal sensor circuit reads shorted, so the control sees a false high-temperature signal. The microwave may refuse convection functions or abort cooking. Sensor or harness replacement is the standard fix per GE's fault table.

fixes.title: Clear and retry
fixes.description: Press Cancel/Off, then retry. If F2 returns immediately, the short is hard.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off (or unplug) for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Sensor and harness access requires opening the case — a technician job on microwaves.
fixes.warning: Do not open a microwave case — the HV capacitor stores a lethal charge.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear, or power-cycle at the breaker for 60 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Same safety rule as every internal microwave fault: case-off work is for technicians. If the unit is over ~8 years old, replacement often beats the service call on countertop models — OTR models are usually worth fixing.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted touchpad — keypad fault
error_codes.detail: The control detects one or more keypad lines stuck closed, as if a button were held down permanently. Phantom button presses, an unresponsive pad, or continuous beeping accompany it. Moisture or grease wicking into the membrane keypad — common above a range — is the usual cause.

causes.description: Moisture or grease in the membrane keypad

fixes.title: Dry it out
fixes.description: If the code appeared after heavy stovetop steaming or panel cleaning, power off at the breaker for a few hours and let the panel dry.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off for 60 seconds, then retest each button row.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/control panel
fixes.description: If specific buttons stay dead or phantom presses continue, the membrane assembly needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Panel replacement requires case access — leave it to a technician.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds (longer if moisture is suspected — give it a few hours to dry), then restore power and test the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: On over-the-range models the touchpad assembly is usually worth replacing professionally; on budget countertop units the part plus labor often exceeds a new microwave.

error_codes.meaning: Open humidity sensor — sensor cooking disabled
error_codes.detail: GE's sensor-cook features (Popcorn, Reheat, Sensor Cook) read steam through a humidity sensor. F4 means that sensor circuit reads open, so sensor-cook modes fail or abort — manual time cooking keeps working normally. The sensor or its connector has failed.

fixes.title: Use manual modes meanwhile
fixes.description: Time-based cooking is unaffected — the fault only blocks sensor-driven modes.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off for 60 seconds and retry a sensor-cook mode.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: The humidity sensor mounts in the vent path inside the case.
fixes.warning: Internal access is technician-only — HV capacitor hazard.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear the code. The microwave remains fully usable in manual modes while you decide on the repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Because manual cooking still works, this is the rare microwave fault you can reasonably live with — repair it opportunistically or when convenience matters.

error_codes.meaning: Inverter / magnetron power fault
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool inverter-type microwaves, F7 means the control detected a problem delivering power to the magnetron — the inverter board reported a fault or the expected power draw never materialized. The unit may run but not heat, or abort with the code. A failed inverter board is the documented usual cause, sometimes with a visible burn spot on the board.

causes.description: Harness fault between control and inverter

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug (or breaker off) for 60 seconds — a transient inverter fault clears with a reboot.

fixes.title: Test heating
fixes.description: Heat a cup of water for 60 seconds on high. Running-but-not-heating alongside F7 confirms the power path to the magnetron.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Inverter and magnetron replacement are internal, high-voltage repairs.
fixes.warning: Never open a microwave case — the capacitor holds a lethal charge after unplugging.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 60 seconds and retest with a cup of water. If F7 returns or heating is weak, stop using it and have the inverter diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Magnetron/inverter repairs run $150–$350 with labor — worth it on built-in and OTR models, rarely on countertop units. Whirlpool magnetrons carry longer warranty terms on some models; check yours before paying.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool inverter-equipped microwaves — typically over-the-range and built-in models that use an inverter board instead of a conventional transformer, roughly mid-2000s onward
error_codes.real_world_notes: Owners commonly report F7 appearing when the microwave runs normally but food comes out cold, or the cycle aborts partway through. A one-time code after a power flicker or brownout often clears with a 60-second unplug, so a reboot is worth trying first. Persistent F7 after a reset usually points to the inverter board rather than a fluke.
